Over the past decade, it has grown into a practice centred on upcycling, restoring old fabrics, bottles and cable wires .

title: The evolving China-Pakistan space cooperation
summary: China is in the process of undertaking a manned moon mission by 2030 . China has consistently promoted its space technologies and pushed for cooperation under Belt and Road Initiative (BRI) The ‘all-weather friendship’ between China and Pakistan has been replicated in their space cooperation as well .

title: Indonesia train crash toll rises to 7, rescuers work to remove trapped passengers
summary: The death toll from a train collision in Indonesia has risen to seven with another 81 injured . The accident happened late on Monday (April 27) in Bekasi, just outside ‌Jakarta . The head of Indonesia’s search and rescue agency said it ‌was a delicate process to get survivors out of the mangled carriages .

title: Ultratech Cement PAT up 19.4% in Q4FY26; announces special dividend ₹240 a share
summary: Ultratech Cement comprehensive income attributable to owners increased 19.4% . Revenue from operations increased 11.9% to ₹25,799.5 crore in the reporting quarter as against � 23,063.32 crore in QFY25 .

title: Enhanced CAR-T therapy clears solid tumours by finding ‘faint’ targets
summary: Chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy, an approach that modifies a patient’s own immune cells to hunt down cancer, has struggled when applied to solid tumours such as kidney or ovarian cancer . CAR-T cells only destroy ‘visible’ targets, so the invisible cells survive and allow the cancer to grow back .

title: MobiKwik Group receives NBFC license from RBI
summary: Reserve Bank of India has approved the Group’s application for a Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) license . This will enable the launch of a new lending division— MobiKwik Financial Services Private Ltd (MFSPL) This will help expand its regulated lending capabilities .

summary: A 33-year-old Indian-origin man was sentenced to 19 months’ jail for molesting his friend’s minor sister over a decade ago . The crime took place over the years when the victim was seven to nine years old, and Melvinder Singh Gurmit Singh was 19 to 21 years old .

title: Odisha tribal produces skeleton of his sister’s body as proof to claim the money in her account
summary: The incident occurred in the Malipasi area under the Patna block of Keonjhar . The man, identified as Jitu Munda, approached the Odisha Grameen Bank to withdraw the savings of his sister, Kalara Munda of Diananali village, who had died two months earlier . Bank authorities asked him to furnish the required documents before processing the claim .

title: Child rights activist flag violations in TVK chief Vijay’s political messaging
summary: Tamil Nadu Child Rights Watch (TNCRW) has registered a complaint with the Election Commission of India (ECI) regarding violations to the use of children for political purposes in the Tamil Nadu elections . They have also urged the State Commission for Protection of Child Rights (SCPCR) to take suo moto cognisance of such violations by political parties .

title: Islamic State militants kill at least 29 in attack on a village in northeastern Nigeria
summary: Militants with the Islamic State group attacked a village overnight in northeastern Nigeria . The attack took place late on Sunday (April 26) in Guyaku, a village in the Gombi local government area in the country’s Adamawa state .

title: Morning Digest: India discussing Chabahar issue with Iran, U.S.; WH dinner shooter charged with attempted assassination of Trump, and more
summary: The Chabahar port issue is being discussed with both Iran and the United States . The man who tried to storm the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner with guns and knives was on Monday (April 27, 2026) charged with the attempted assassination of President Donald Trump . Mr. Trump was rushed off the stage and guests ducking for cover underneath their tables .

title: U.S., Iran clash over Tehran's nuclear programme as review of atomic treaty begins at UN
summary: The United States and Iran clashed over Tehran's nuclear programme . Review of the treaty meant to prevent the spread of atomic weapons got underway on Monday (April 27, 2026) at the United Nations . At issue was the election of Iran as one of 34 Vice-Presidents of the conference .

title: Ennore police apprehend five, including two minors, for attempted two-wheeler theft
summary: Ennore police apprehended five persons, including two minors, for injuring a woman . Police sources said that unidentified persons damaged two-wheelers parked on Third Street . One of the vehicle owners, Deepa, woke up and confronted them for damaging the vehicles . The youths, who were allegedly in an intoxicated condition, attacked her by hurling stones before fleeing the spot .

title: Hot Westerly winds set to increase temperatures from May 1 in city
summary: Temperatures in the coastal stations of the State could see a slight increase from May 1 when the Westerly winds start blowing from the hot Rayalaseema region . Interior areas that are already witnessing above normal temperatures by 2-3 degrees could witness a further increase . Light to moderate rain have also been forecast till May 2 over isolated places across the State .

title: ‘Basava Sri’ award for H.D. Deve Gowda
summary: Former Prime Minister H.D. Deve Gowda said there has been a general decline in all aspects of society and pro people movements have reduced . “Those with knowledge have become cynical about the system. What we require now is the philosophy of Allama, action of Basavanna, and anger of Ambigara Chowdaiah,” he wrote in a thanking letter to the Basava Samiti .

title: CMRL’s Koyambedu-Chennai Trade Centre stretch delayed, likely to be launched in August
summary: Phase II stretch from Koyambedu to Chennai Trade Centre is likely to be launched only in August . The ₹63,246-crore project will touch various corners of the city with three corridors—Madhavaram to SIPCOT, Poonamallee to Light House and Sholinganallur .

title: Muthukumaraswami’s grace
summary: Lord Murugan in the Vaideeswaran temple in Tamil Nadu goes by the name of Muthukumaraswami . Devotees lovingly refer to Him as Muthiah, said T. Rajarathinam in a discourse .

title: Four of a family in Mumbai die after eating biryani and watermelon; investigation underway
summary: Four members of a family in the Pydhonie area of SouthMumbaidied on Sunday (April 26, 2026) after experiencing vomiting and diarrhea following a meal of biryani and watermelon . The police have registered an accidental death report and sent food samples for forensic testing .

title: Untreated sewage flows into Pallikaranai marshland
summary: Untreated sewage and domestic garbage continue to be discharged into the Pallikaranai marshland from adjacent residential neighbourhoods . Vidyasagar Jagadeesan, a Chennai-based activist, said he recently observed sewage inflows at two locations close to the marshland limits during a visit to the park close by .

summary: More than 600 Google employees demanded that the company reject a proposed Pentagon deal that would allow its artificial intelligence technology to be deployed in classified military operations . The tech giant is in active negotiations with the U.S. Department of Defence to deploy its flagship Gemini AI model in classified settings .

title: Afghan officials say Pakistani strikes killed 7, wounded 85 in first attacks since peace talks

Pakistan dismissed the accusation of targeting a university . The strikes were the first violent incident since Chinese-mediated peace talks between the two sides earlier this month .

summary: An integrated inter-disciplinary approach should be made to the development of the entire coastal belt between Tiruvanmiyur and Mahabalipuram . The approach to development should cover land utilisation, urbanisation, groundwater exploitation and the like .

summary: The State government signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with the Telangana State Legal Services Authority (TSLSA) to strengthen legal literacy and child protection mechanisms in schools across the State . The initiative aims to integrate legal awareness into the school education system .

title: ‘Harvest more water; banning paddy is not the solution to groundwater stress’
summary: “Banning a crop is not the solution to groundwater stress,” says E. Sridhar . The Ground Water Department and various departments and institutions that are part of the State-level committee on Groundwater Resource Assessment must study the various ways to enhance groundwater recharge .

title: Prison-run fuel station inaugurated at Khanapur, inspects women’s prison and opens new block in Warangal

title: Letters to The Editor — April 28, 2026

Reporting snake bites along with the species involved would be helpful . There are tribes traditionally involved in snake rescue in different States, and many times they are not available for the job .

title: ₹127.69 crore has been restored to victims of Rose Valley Group scam: ED
summary: The Rose Valley Group fraudulently mobilised about ₹17,520 crore, from investors across West Bengal, Assam, Odisha, and other States promising high returns . Of these funds, � 6,666 crore remains unpaid . The Enforcement Directorate has completed 16 phases of disbursements and released more than 1.73 lakhs .

title: J&K court orders release of AAP MLA after 8 months of jail, says ‘PSA appears an unjustified exercise’
summary: The Jammu and Kashmir High Court quashed the detention of Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) legislator Mehraj Din Malik after eight months of jail . The administration’s move to invoke Public Safety Act (PSA) was “a shortcut to bypass ordinary criminal law”

title: Madras High Court commutes death sentence of a man convicted of repeated sexual assault on minor daughter
summary: Madras High Court has commuted the death sentence awarded by a trial court in Tirunelveli district to a man convicted of sexually assaulting his minor daughter . The prosecution case was that the appellant exploited the girl over a period of time; as a result, she became pregnant and had to undergo abortion .

title: Telangana Deputy Chief Minister Bhatti Vikramarka’s CPRO Madhusudhan passes away
summary: Maraboyina Madhusudhan, (47), was in the office of the Deputy Chief Minister in Praja Bhavan, when he complained of chest pain . He was rushed to a nearby hospital and from there to Apollo Spectra Hospital in Begumpet . He suffered cardiac arrest and attempts to revive him proved futile .

title: IPL 2026: We felt Pooran was the best option against Narine in Super Over: Langer
summary: Lucknow Super Giants lost to Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026 match . LSG coach Justin Langer says Nicholas Pooran was his best option against Sunil Narine . Pooran has faced Narine more than anyone in world cricket, says Langer .

title: IPL 2026: Jacks all set to make his first appearance of the season for MI
summary: Will Jacks was expected to be a vital cog for Mumbai Indians in IPL 2026 . England all-rounder finally linked up with the squad ahead of last week’s humbling at home by Chennai Super Kings . Mumbai Indians’ campaign is already teetering with five defeats in seven games .

title: Bahrain strips dozens of citizenship over Iran support
summary: Bahrain's Interior Ministry said 69 individuals revoked the citizenship of 69 individuals for their support of Iran after weeks of attacks on Gulf states . The Ministry, in a statement, listed the dozens stripped of their citizenship, which it said included dependents, adding they "were found to have supported hostile Iranian acts, including colluding with foreign entities"

title: IPL 2026: Bhuvneshwar, Hazlewood blow away DC
summary: Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood were so irresistible with the new ball that the proceedings resembled a Test match unfolding on a green deck in Dunedin . The deadly combination broke the back of the host inside the PowerPlay, and made victory for Royal Challengers Bengaluru a matter of formality .

title: BJP set for huge victory in West Bengal elections: Nitin Nabin
summary: Bharatiya Janata Party national president Nitin Nabin stated on Monday (April 27, 2026) that the party was set to win theAssembly elections in West Bengalowing to the “hard work, struggle and sacrifices” of party workers . Mr. Nabin was on his first visit to Rajasthan after his appointment as the BJP chief .

title: Minister refutes allegations of delay in caste certificates to Talwar community
summary: RDPR Minister Priyank Kharge said multiple high-level meetings have already been held to ensure the timely issuance of certificates to eligible beneficiaries . The Minister said that a meeting chaired by Chief Minister Siddaramaiah was held during the Belagavi Legislature Session in the presence of senior officials .

title: Verify your or your parents/grandparents details, get mapped with SIR 2002: Telangana CEO
summary: Telangana Chief Electoral Officer C. Sudarshan Reddy has urged all voters to verify their or their parents/grandparents’ details and get linked or mapped with Special Intensive Revision (SIR) 2002 . The voters were requested to meet their polling stations’ BLOs or Electoral Registration Officer for further details .

title: Ties with India unaffected, says Nepal Envoy ahead of Foreign Secretary’s Kathmandu visit
summary: Nepal Envoy ahead of Foreign Secretary’s visit to Kathmandu . Nepal's relations with India 'unaffected' with India, says Nepal envoy . Foreign Secretary says Nepal's ties with India unaffected . Nepal Foreign Secretary's visit to Nepal seen as a 'positive plus' for India .

title: PM to flag off Banaras-Pune, Ayodhya-Mumbai express trains
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will flag off two new Amrit Bharat Express trains connecting Banaras-Pune, and Ayodhya-Mumbai, on April 28, 2026 . The introduction of the two new weekly trains will link Uttar Pradesh with Maharashtra via Madhya Pradesh .

title: HC asks govt. to come up with rehabilitation scheme for  transpersons at the taluk level
summary: Madurai Bench of the Madras High Court has directed the Tamil Nadu government to formulate a comprehensive rehabilitation scheme for transpersons at the taluk level . Justice K.K. Ramakrishnan said justice has not been done adequately .

title: Congress targets Centre for lack of consensus on joint statement at BRICS meeting
summary: The Congress described as ‘shameful’ the absence of a joint statement following last week’s BRICS+ meeting in the capital . The Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh claimed that India’�s position was unacceptable to several BRICS+. members .

title: Nida Khan has Malaysia links, needs custodial probe, Nashik police informs court
summary: Nida Khan is an absconding accused in the alleged TCS sexual exploitation and forced religious conversion case . The Nashik Police said she needs to be interrogated in custody . The court was hearing Ms. Khan’s anticipatory bail application .

title: Kodagu DC reviews progress of enumeration work in the district
summary: Deputy Commissioner of Kodagu S.J. Somashekar reviewed the progress of the door-to-door survey as part of the ongoing Census 2027 . He directed the officials to achieve substantial progress by the end of this month .

title: German Chancellor Merz says Iran is humiliating U.S. as talks stall
summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Iran’s leadership was humiliating the United States and getting U.S. officials to travel to Pakistan and then leave without results . Comments underlined deep divisions between Washington ‌and its European NATO allies, which had already been festering over ‌Ukraine and other issues .

title: Bengaluru to host pan-India IIT summit on May 16
summary: The Government of Karnataka will organise a Pan-IIT Bangalore Summit 2026 on May 16 . Themed around ‘Technology sovereignty’, the conference is expected to bring together 2,000 participants from across 23 IITs . The conclave will feature high-level plenaries, deep-dive panels, and independent tracks on AI, defence, semiconductors, energy, mobility and healthcare .

title: CUB Q4 net rises 25% to ₹360 cr., opens 1,000th branch at Kumbakonam, T.N.
summary: City Union Bank reported a 25% growth in net profit during Q4FY 2026 . The bank also opened 1,000th branch at Ammachathiram in Kumbakonam . Total deposits rose 23% to ₹78,308 crore, while total advances climbed to � 66,699 crore .

title: ONGC approves joint venture with MRPL, OPaL focussed on petrochemicals marketing
summary: State-owned explorer ONGC approved formation of a joint venture company with subsidiaries Mangalore Refinery Pvt Ltd and ONGC Petro additions Ltd . ONGC would hold 50% stake in the joint venture, with rest being equally divided among the two subsidiaries .

title: Kochi Corporation proposes facilitating nightlife along Marine Drive Walkway
summary: A detailed project report (DPR) on facilitating nightlife along the Marine Drive Walkway is likely to be prepared shortly . Discussions are under way with Kochi-based consultants to frame the DPR for the project for which the Corporation has allocated ₹50 lakh . Additional funding, including through corporate social responsibility contributions, will also be mobilised .

title: Five women thieves arrested, jewellery worth ₹6.8 lakh recovered
summary: The Government Railway Police (GRP) and the Railway Protection Force (RPF) arrested a five-member women’s gang on charges of alleged theft in a train . Following a complaint, the teams led by GRP Circle Inspector J.V. Ramana and RPF Inspector Fateh Ali Baig arrested the gang on Monday .

title: CAPFs go ‘all out’ to win ‘trust’, foster ‘peace’ ahead of last phase of Bengal polls
summary: More than 100 armoured and anti-riot vehicles have been deployed across sensitive areas to instil confidence among voters . The intensified security measures include round-the-clock area domination exercises, night patrols and high-visibility movement of forces . The state is heading into the final phase of the Assembly election in West Bengal .

title: Oil India reports hydrocarbon discovery in Libya
summary: State-owned Oil India announced an oil and gas recovery from its exploration block in Libya . The explorer holds a 25% participating interest in the consortium, which also includes IndianOil . The block holds eight exploratory wells with drilling of five wells completed .

title: NITI Aayog revamp shows tilt towards science and health
summary: The revamp of the NITI Aayog last week shows a tilt towards technology and health sectors . Usually, the choice for the policy think tank has leaned towards economists . Three of the five new members — Abhay Karandikar, M. Srinivas, and Gobardhan Das — have had long careers in health, biotechnology, and science .

title: Fertiliser security strong and stable: Union Government
summary: The Union Fertilisers Ministry said the country’s fertiliser security remained “strong, stable, and well-managed” Retail prices of Urea, Di Ammonium Phosphate (DAP) and Triple Superphosphate would remain unchanged at � 266.5, ₹1,350 and � 1,300 per bag .

title: Dinesh Trivedi appointed India’s next Envoy to Dhaka
summary: Dinesh Trivedi appointed India’s next Envoy to Dhaka . He will be the next Indian envoy to Bangladesh . He was appointed as the new ambassador to Bangladesh from Dhaka, Dhaka. He has been in charge of the Indian Embassy in Dhaka for six years .

title: Kashmir seminary-cum-school sealed under UAPA provisions
summary: Authorities sealed a seminary-cum-school with around 800 students in south Kashmir’s Shopian district . PDP president Mehbooba Mufti and Kashmir chief cleric expressed dismay over the move . The district Magistrate, Shopian, barred any entry into the notified place .

title: Talwar community stages protest against delay in STs certificates
summary: Members of the Talwar community staged a massive protest in the city on Monday saying there is delay in the issuance of Scheduled Tribes (STs) caste certificates . The agitators under the banner of the Karnataka Rajya Talwar Samaj took out a march from Jagat Circle to the Deputy Commissioner’s office .

title: Ladakh groups seek meeting with Shah and steps to build trust
summary: Leh Apex Body (LAB), an amalgam of socio-political-religious organisations, advocates for trust-building exercise ahead of Amit Shah’s visit to Ladakh . LAB sought a meeting with Mr. Shah during his two-day tour of the Union Territory (UT) from April 30 .

title: Sabari rail should have double track, says P.C. Thomas
summary: Senior Kerala Congress leader P.C. Thomas demands that the proposed Angamaly-Erumely Sabari rail project be built in double track . He said at least land should be acquired to meet the requirements for doubling in the future . Work on the rail line is expected to resume in due course .

title: Admission restrictions for mLAC degree courses due to non-renewal of land lease
summary: Bengaluru City University restricted Maharani Lakshmi Ammanni College for Women from admitting students for the academic year 2026-27 due to non-renewal of its land lease . The land lease agreement of mLAC expired in 2008 and had not been renewed for the past 23 years .

title: Mangaluru cooker blast case: NIA court sentences Mohammed Shariq to 10 years  rigorous imprisonment
summary: Mohammed Shariq sentenced to 10 years rigorous imprisonment in connection with the 2022 Mangaluru pressure cooker blast case . The court also imposed a fine of about ₹92,000 under the provisions of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA) and relevant sections of IPC .

title: CM Naidu announces ₹500 crore to make Andhra University a global brand
summary: The Centenary Celebrations of Andhra University (1926–2026), themed “Century of Excellence… Paving Path to the Future,” were held at the College of Engineering grounds on Monday . Vice-President C.P. Radhakrishnan, the chief guest, described the university as “a melting pot of ideas” that had nurtured generations who contributed to the nation .

title: RBI tightens bad loan rules to align with global norms
summary: The Reserve Bank of India has rejigged the rules governing classification of bad loans, definition, and recovery, to align with globally-accepted standards, effective April 1, 2027 . “These Directions are intended to further strengthen credit risk management practices, improve comparability across regulated entities, and align the regulatory framework more closely with internationally accepted financial reporting principles .

title: K.N. Nehru moves Madras High Court against direction to register FIR in cash for jobs allegation
summary: Minister K.N. Nehru too has filed a review application urging the Madras High Court to set aside its February 20, 2026 direction to the Directorate of Vigilance and Anti Corruption (DVAC) to register a First Information Report .

title: Rani Park walkers condemn arson, seek tighter security measures
summary: Several trees were allegedly set on fire in a public park in Bengaluru on Monday morning . Residents associated with the Rani Park Walkers Association strongly condemned the incident . The association expressed concern over what it described as a serious lapse in the safety of public spaces .

title: Surgeon from Bhadravati participates in London Marathon wearing tricolour and Kannada flag
summary: Dr. Ramachandra Madatti Gowda is a Non-Resident Indian doctor and Oral and Maxillofacial Reconstructive Surgeon . Dr. Gowda has completed his BDS from Bengaluru Dental College, MDS in KLE VK Institute of Dental Sciences .

title: CSMT Express to be short-terminated at Thane till May 19
summary: Mangaluru Junction-Mumbai Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT) Superfast Express (Train No. 12134) will continue to be short-terminated at Thane till May 19 . Short-termination is being enforced owing to the ongoing platform extension work at CSMT platforms 16 and 17 .

title: Over 1,700 riders booked by Kochi City police during special drive to promote helmet use
summary: Kochi City police booked over 1,700 two-wheeler riders for not wearing helmets . Police intensified enforcement as part of a State-wide special drive . A total of 1,716 violations were detected from different parts of the city police limits .

title: ASI assault case: Mangaluru court sentences accused to seven years rigorous imprisonment
summary: Mahammed Niyaz was found guilty of attempting to murder Assistant Sub-Inspector Aithappa . The Additional District and Sessions Judge, Fast Track Sessions Court-1, Mangaluru, on Monday sentenced him to seven years rigorous imprisonment and imposed a fine of ₹25,000 .

title: Virudhunagar district registers 84.85% of votes as per final figures
summary: Virudhunagar district has registered a total of 84.85% polling percentage in all the seven Assembly constituencies . Out of the 15,09,518 total voters in the district, 12,80,756 had polled their votes on April 23 .

title: Govt. says Chabahar issue being discussed with Iran, U.S.
summary: The Chabahar port issue is being discussed with both Iran and the United States . India faces a crucial test of its strategic autonomy with the U.S. sanctions waiver having expired on April 26 . New Delhi has two options now of either choosing to exit the 23-year-old project in the Iranian port city .

title: Watch: 7 AAP MPs officially merge with BJP: What next? | Above the Fold | 27.04.2026
summary: 7 AAP MPs officially merge with BJP: What next? | Above the Fold | 27.04.2026 . The AAP MPs are the first to officially merge into the BJP . They are the only members of the seven-strong-strong party in the state .

title: Actor Suriya inaugurates comprehensive cancer care centre at Madurai hospital
summary: Actor Suriya inaugurated a comprehensive cancer care centre and bone marrow transplant unit at Madurai Meenakshi Super Speciality Hospital . The hospital aims to provide end-to-end cancer management — from early diagnosis to advanced treatment and long-term recovery — under one roof .

title: SEBI urged to probe alleged ₹275 crore fund diversion in slum rehab project by listed firm
summary: A city-based complaint has urged the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) to investigate alleged fund diversion, investor cheating and money laundering involving more than ₹275 crore . The complaint filed by one Nikesh Raghani relates to a redevelopment project under the SRA scheme at Rani Sati Marg, spread over about 5,600 sq. metres .

title: Flower supply increases in Dindigul
summary: Supply of jasmine has increased in Dindigul district and is now selling at a price ranging from � 300 to � 500 per kg . Last month, it was selling as high as ₹3,000 .

title: 25 enthusiasts get training at theatre workshop
summary: Amateur theatre group Ranga Sampada organised a two-day theatre workshop in Belagavi . Award-winning theatre personalities Prakash Garud and Rajani Garud trained 25 enthusiasts at the Kannada Sahitya Bhavan auditorium .

title: Punjab journalists complain of censorship by AAP Government
summary: Independent journalists from Punjab alleged that the Aam Aadmi Party-led government was systematically targeting voices in the State . “This is very dangerous for journalists, for free speech and for independent press,” said Sahildeep Singh, who runs the Duniya Facebook page with more than 1.2 lakh followers .

title: Uttarakhand CM lauds direct selling rules, highlights role in self-reliance
summary: Uttarakhand Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami said Consumer Protection (Direct Selling) Rules, 2021, implemented by the Centre, had created a transparent and credible framework for the direct selling sector . He was speaking at an event titled ‘Atmanirbhar Bharat Ka Vijay Parv’, organised by a direct selling company .

title: West Bengal polls phase II: BJP veterans and CPI(M) youth brigade put up a challenge in Trinamool stronghold
summary: Fresh faces are taking on Trinamool Congress candidates in its bastion of Kolkata and adjoining districts . Senior BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari is fighting from the Bhabanipur seat opposite Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee .

title: 10-year jail for sexual assault of teen girl
summary: The Special Court for POCSO (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences) awarded 10 years imprisonment to a youth for sexually assaulting a teen girl . Ramesh, 30, of Valliyoor sexually assaulted a 13-year-old girl in 2019 .

title: Medical waste discarded, set on fire
summary: The Sivanthipatti police have registered a case against unidentified persons who discorded medical waste and set on fire . Police said unidentified persons brought medical waste in a mini lorry to the forest near MGR Nagar under police station limits on Sunday .

title: Administration chops trees in Deputy Commissioner’s office complex after employee’s electrocution
summary: Administration chops trees in Deputy Commissioner’s office complex after employee electrocution . The incident occurred after employee was electrocuted by a tree in the Deputy Commissioner's office complex .  ‘I’m not sure what happened,’ says Deputy Commissioner. ‘I don’t know what happened.’

title: BJD asks Odisha Speaker to disqualify MLAs who cross-voted in Rajya Sabha election
summary: Biju Janata Dal (BJD) demanded disqualification of eight MLAs who voted against the party’s decision in the Rajya Sabha election held on March 16 . The party submitted eight separate petitions to the Speaker to disqualify the MLAs .

title: Five workers killed, several injured after crane collapses at NCC central workshop in Shankarpally
summary: Heavy-duty crane collapsed at NCC central workshop in Mahalingapuram village of Shankarpally mandal on Monday afternoon . The deceased were identified as Ali Hussain, Nagendra Singh, Sajim, Mukesh Kumar and Sonu Rajibi, hailing from Bihar and Jharkhand .

title: MACT awards ₹9.01 crore as compensation in road accident case
summary: Shijin, a native of Thiruvangoor who worked in the UAE, died after being hit by a lorry . The Kozhikode Motor Accidents Claims Tribunal awarded ₹9.01 crore as compensation to the family of a person who died in a road accident .

title: DKMUL to set up mega dairy in Mangaluru
summary: The Dakshina Kannada District Cooperative Milk Producers’ Societies’ Union Ltd. (DKMUL) proposed to build a mega dairy in Mangaluru . The dairy at Kulashekara in the city is 30 years old .

title: Silk garments offered from Indrakeeladri to Annavaram Satyanarayana Swamy Temple
summary: Silk garments were ceremoniously offered from Indrakeeladri, the abode of the Kanaka Durga Temple, to the Annavaram Satyanarayana Swamy Temple . The delegation was led by temple trust board chairman Borra Radhakrishna and executive officer V.K. Seena Nayak .

title: Kozhikode Corporation to set up designated feeding spots for stray dogs
summary: Kozhikode Corporation has initiated steps to identify designated feeding spots for street dogs . The move follows a Supreme Court directive to create dedicated spots for feeding street dogs, instead of allowing feeding on streets and in public places . Mayor O. Sadasivan recently asked councillors to identify suitable locations in their respective wards .

title: Rajnath Singh arrives in Bishkek for SCO Defence Ministers’ Meeting
summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh is leading a high-level Indian delegation for the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers’ Meeting scheduled to be held on Tuesday (April 28, 2026) The meeting will bring together Defence Ministers of SCO member states to deliberate on key regional and global security challenges .

title: Police launch probe into ‘dumping’ of septic waste into well at RMP leader’s house in Kozhikode
summary: The alleged incident took place at the residence of T.P. Manojan at Thuneri in Nadapuram . It is reported to have occurred between Saturday evening (April 25) and Sunday morning (April 26) The well was used for drawing drinking water by the families of Mr.Manojan and his brothers .

title: New party formation not a matter of concern to BRS: KTR
summary: Working president of the Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) K.T. Rama Rao made the comments when his notice was drawn to the formation of Telangana Rashtra Sena (TRS) He stated that only very few regional parties have sustained in the Telugu States for more than 25 years .

title: 17 lakh people held in Bihar over last 10 years for violating liquor ban
summary: The manufacture, import, storage, sale and consumption of liquor is illegal under the law . Of the 17,18,058 accused held so far, 7,75,979 persons had been arrested by the Prohibition & Excise Department .

title: DME panel to probe mental harassment charges against faculty members
summary: The Directorate of Medical Education (DME) has set up an inquiry committee to look into allegations of mental harassment against two faculty members of the Department of Psychiatry, Government Medical College, Kozhikode . The panel has been asked to submit a report in a week .

title: Karnataka Konkani Sahitya Academy presents its annual awards to six personalities
summary: Karnataka Konkani Sahitya Academy presents its annual awards to six personalities .     Karnataka Konksani Sahitam Academy presents an annual awards for six personalities.  - -  ‘-’ - ‘- ’ – “-” – - "-“ ” ‚- - “- " -‘– "- –’s  – ’-  ”: "–“– ‪’–”’—  – “ "” - “Karn

title: Govt. begins clearing ₹1,000 crore dues to hospitals under Dr. NTR Vaidya Seva
summary: The State government has initiated the process of clearing dues amounting to ₹1,000 crore to private hospitals . So far, around � 62 crore has been released to about 25 hospitals . Overall, dues totalling � 2,044 crore are pending to be cleared .

title: Kerala’s out-of-pocket expense in hospitals still among the highest in country
summary: Kerala has the highest morbidity, defined as Proportion of People Responded as Ailing (PPRA), during the last 15-day period of the survey, at 39.7%. This is three times the national average of 13.1% . The PPRA for Kerala has risen sharply since the 75th round — from approximately 24.5% in 2017-18 to the current 39 .7% .

title: Amaravati capital city works halted as diesel shortage disrupts construction activity
summary: Infrastructure works in the capital city of Amaravati have taken a serious hit due to an acute shortage of diesel . Nearly 60% of machinery remained non-operational since Saturday (April 25, 2026) A senior Andhra Pradesh Capital Region Development Authority official said that nearly three lakh kilolitres of diesel was required per day to operate large machines deployed for capital city works .

title: High Court imposes costs on CDSCO officials
summary: The Telangana High Court imposed  ₹50,000 costs on the officials of Central Drug Standards Control Organisation-Hyderabad zonal office . The judge also quashed the case, registered against the retired person A.K. Gupta, pending on the file of a court at Nampally .

title: Sachin Tendulkar inspires students at Andhra University centenary celebrations
summary: Sachin Tendulkar addressed students, faculty, alumni, and distinguished guests at the AU College of Engineering Grounds in Visakhapatnam on Monday . As a special invitee, he drew sustained applause from students who held posters hailing him as one of the greatest cricketers of all time .

title: Another hitch in the BJP-JD(S) alliance in Karnataka
summary: The Janata Dal (Secular) is learnt to be upset with its alliance partner the Bharatiya Janata Party . The latest hitch in the alliance has come amid confusion over the BJP and the JD(S) fighting the still-to-be-announced local body polls together .

title: Do not foresee any requirement to import petrol or diesel: Petroleum Ministry
summary: The government does not foresee any situation where the country may be required to import petrol and diesel . Sujata Sharma, Joint Secretary at the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG), told reporters at the inter-Ministerial briefing on West Asia on Monday (April 27, 2026)

title: Residents urge BMRCL to include ‘Bilekahalli’ in IIMB metro station name on Bengaluru’s Pink Line metro
summary: Residents in south Bengaluru have written to the Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited (BMRCL) seeking a change in the proposed name of the upcoming ‘IIMB” metro station on the Pink Line . They are urging authorities to recognise the locality’s identity by renaming it ‘Bilekahalli’

title: Ukraine, West Asia conflicts put attack drones at the centre of modern warfare
summary: The footage has become grimly familiar: shaky camera feed from afar, showing a drone hovering above a target before the sudden moment of impact, all filmed and circulated within minutes . What once required a fighter jet, a cruise missile, or a team of special forces operatives is now being done for a fraction of the price from the back of a pickup truck .

title: Good prices help Kerala’s Muthalamada mango farmers offset crop losses
summary: Farmers said they were worried at the start of the season when irregular rain threatened the flowers . But the rain did not destroy their hopes, though some pests and thrips caused damage . Most of the 500-plus farmers in Muthalamada made some profit because there was early demand for their mangoes in north Indian markets .

title: Problems in land transactions continue to persist a year after passage of Bhu Bharati Act
summary: Farmers continue to face problems related to their land transactions a year after the enactment of Telangana Bhu Bharati (Record of Rights) Act, 2025 . The recent suicide by farmer Madhav Rao is indicative of the difficulties faced by farmers in securing rights over their lands .

title: Telangana launches ‘Spandana’, an all-women response system to enhance safety for women and children
summary: Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy inaugurated the initiative at the Integrated Command Control Centre premises in Banjara Hills . Spandana is an all-women first responder system aimed at strengthening safety measures for women and children in the city .

title: Man convicted in two POCSO cases in Salem
summary: R. Mini, alias Manikandan (30), was convicted in two Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act cases . He was arrested under the POCSO Act for sexually assaulting an eight-year-old boy in 2022 . Later, he was arrested again under the Act for assaulting a girl, 11, in 2023 . The cases' trials were held in the Special Court for the Exclusive Trial of Cases under the act .

title: BJP Mahila Morcha accuses Congress of stalling Women’s Bill, stages protest in Vijayawada
summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party Mahila Morcha staged a protest rally condemning the alleged role of the Indian National Congress in stalling the Women’s Reservation Bill . The protest was organised as part of a nationwide call by the BJP against the ‘anti-women’ actions of the Congress and its allies .

title: Janivara row: Act by college staff was intentional, says DC
summary: Bengaluru Urban District Administration has decided to submit a report to the government regarding the recent incident of three students being stripped of their janivara during the CET-2026 . Krupanidhi Pre-University College no longer be considered as an examination centre for any exams .

title: KCR faults comments linking State formation to birth of Pakistan
summary: Former Chief Minister K. Chandrasekhar Rao says recent incident highlighted need for BRS presence in Telangana . He says had there been even one MP of BRS in Lok Sabha, he/she would have condemned such an unwanted and cynical remark tooth and nail .

title: CWC binds over entire family for child marriages of two sisters
summary: The Krishna District Child Welfare Committee bound over an entire family for conducting child marriages of two minor girls . The committee directed that the girls be produced before the committee every month . “We rescued a minor girl aged about ten years,” the DCPU staff said .

title: Vizianagaram Collector urged to lift prohibitory status on retired Army officials’ land
summary: Retired Army officials urge district collector to remove their 15 acres of land from prohibitory list . The land would allow them to build houses in government allocated land . Vizianagaram District Army Officers Welfare Association president Devara Eswara Rao and secretary Barukula Venkata Rao submitted a detailed memorandum .

title: Eswari, the prime accused in the Virudhunagar fireworks unit blast, arrested
summary: Eswari, the licensee of Vanaja Fireworks at Kattanarapatti near Virudhunagar, was arrested on Sunday . 25 workers were killed in a fire accident on April 19 . The police said a special team arrested the woman and produced her .

title: Authorities in Khammam assure no fuel shortage despite temporary outlet closures
summary: Reports of some fuel outlets running out of diesel and petrol in various parts of Khammam district in the last 24 hours . District authorities maintain that there is no disruption in fuel supply to the district . Official sources attributed the reported temporary shutdown to ‘panic buying’ triggered by rumours of fuel shortage .

title: 29-year-old man found murdered near Kalligudi
summary: A 29-year-old man, R. Srinivasan, was found hacked to death at Sholampatti under Kalligudi police station limits on Monday morning . The man had quarrelled with two persons who were consuming liquor during a temple festival a few weeks back .

title: KSEB pinning hopes on summer rainfall as electricity consumption remains high
summary: Kerala State Electricity Board (KSEB) pinning its hopes on summer rainfall gaining strength this week to bring cooler weather and lower electricity consumption . IMD has issued yellow alert for isolated heavy rainfall in a number of districts in the state .

title: Career Guidance and Counselling Centre at Tirunelveli Collectorate to help Plus Two students
summary: District Collector R. Sukumar said the Plus Two Public Examination results would be released on May 8 . The Career Guidance and Counselling Centre would channelise Plus Two students in choosing the right course of their choice in arts, science, engineering, medicine, law, agriculture, architecture etc.

title: There’s adequate stock of essential medicine in govt. hospitals, says Minister
summary: Health and Family Welfare Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao said that there is adequate stock of essential medicine in all government hospitals and PHCs . Supplies are being dispatched immediately based on demand, he said . Inaugurating the new vaccination centre located within the District Health . and the District Integrated Public Health Laboratory at the District Hospital .

title: CPM stages protest in Vijayawada over fuel shortage, blames government inaction
summary: Leaders and activists of the Communist Party of India (Marxist) (CPIM) staged a protest at the NTR district Collectorate in Vijayawada on Monday (April 27, 2026), demanding immediate steps to resolve the ongoing petrol and diesel shortage in Andhra Pradesh .

title: 15,264 postal ballots cast by election duty officials in Thoothukudi
summary: A total of 15,264 election personnel have cast their votes through postal ballots . 5,646 officials voted in person at the polling station after obtaining an election duty certificate . The vote counting is scheduled to take place on May 4 (Monday)

title: Director Krishnadas Murali on the success of ‘Bharatanatyam 2: Mohiniyattam’
summary: Krishnadas Murali's sophomore directorial venture, Mohiniyattam, is dark humour at its best . The sequel of Bharatanatyam(2024) is a murder which leads to comedic chaos .

title: Perarivalan, Rajiv Gandhi assassination case convict let out on remission, dons black robes
summary: A.G Perarivalan was one of the life convicts in the former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi assassination case . He was enrolled to the Bar Association of Tamil Nadu and Puducherry and will practice as an advocate in the Madras High Court .

title: YSRCP slams Andhra Pradesh government over fuel crisis, seeks Centre’s intervention
summary: Opposition YSR Congress Party demands immediate restoration of fuel supplies . Former Ministers Kurasala Kannabababu and Velampalli Srinivas say agricultural operations have been badly hit due to diesel shortages . They alleged that Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu has failed to ensure adequate supply despite being part of the NDA .

title: Art expo showcasing Lakshadweep’s underwater beauty begins in Kozhikode
summary: An exhibition of paintings by a Lakshadweep-based couple, showcasing the breathtaking beauty of the deep sea, began in Kozhikode . The exhibition features vivid depictions of the colourful underwater world of the Arabian Sea as experienced by the islanders themselves .

title: Telangana Chief Minister bets on healthcare boom, positions Hyderabad as global medical tourism hub
summary: Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy positioned Telangana as an emerging hub for healthcare and medical tourism . Hyderabad accounts for nearly 40% of India’s bulk drug exports . Reddy said he wants the State to contribute 50% of the country's healthcare infrastructure and services in future .

title: Cyberabad Municipal Corporation launches ‘One Ward Every Day’ initiative
summary: Cyberabad Municipal Corporation (CMC) Commissioner G. Srijana launched the ‘One Ward Every Day’ initiative . The initiative will cover all three zones and 76 wards . Key focus areas include street lighting, drainage systems, sanitation hotspots, and overall service delivery .

title: Demand for jackfruit soars in Madurai markets as festival fervour grips city
summary: The much-awaited Chithirai festival has commenced in Madurai, along with it has come the demand for jackfruits . Markets and wholesale shops in areas like Yannaikal have been witnessing crowds throughout the day .

title: Deputy CM Bhatti Vikramarka completes self-enumeration online for Phase-I of Census-2027
summary: Deputy Chief Minister Mallu Bhatti Vikramarka registered his household data online as part of the ongoing Self-Enumeration (SE) for Phase-I of Census 2027 in Khammam . He completed his self-enumeration process through the official online platform .

title: Joshi sees need to equip hospitals with enough doctors, staff and medicine
summary: Pralhad Joshi said that development is not just about constructing hospitals but equipping them with adequate doctors, staff and medicine . He said that the country is emerging as a reliable nation globally in both the service and manufacturing sectors . Joshi inaugurated the new building of the Amminbhavi Community Health Centre in Dharwad taluk on Monday .

title: CAFIT Premier League Beach Cricket Tournament concludes
summary: The CAFIT Premier League (CPL) Beach Cricket Tournament was held at Town Beach, Kozhikode . IOCOD Infotech Pvt. Ltd and eSynergy Technologies Pv. Ltd were crowned champions in the men's and women’s categories . The three-day event saw participation from 35 IT companies .

title: Bank pensioners’ meet in Kochi from April 28
summary: The fifth triennial national conference of the All India Bank Pensioners and Retirees Confederation will be held on April 28 and 29 at the Ernakulam Town Hall . The conference and general body meeting will deliberate issues of bank retirees .

title: ‘Innovation in higher education is required urgently’
summary: The need for innovation in higher education is not a long-range luxury; it is a near-term urgency with long-term consequences . Reform on this scale cannot be left to inspirational speeches, isolated model institutions, or sporadic pilot projects, Mungekar said .

title: Babu hints at political backstabbing behind 2016 loss
summary: Senior Congress leader and Thripunithura MLA K. Babu hints at political backstabbing behind his defeat in the 2016 Assembly elections . ‘I lost the election as those around me ‘helped’ me a lot. However, I don't want to go into the details since the matter is no longer relevant.

title: K. Muthukrishnan passes away
summary: K. Muthukrishnan Nair, a founding member of the Kerala Secretariat Association, died aged 91 . Nair retired as joint secretary to the government in 1990 and was private secretary to E. Chandrasekharan, when he was the Minister of Education and Law .

title: Garbage hotspots on rise amid new solid waste management rules
summary: A heap of garbage has been causing misery to hospital visitors and daily commuters . Despite regular cleaning, the garbage keeps reappearing . Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Limited (BSWML) is attempting to enforce the new solid waste management rules . The city is witnessing an uptick in garbage black spots .

title: Where is question of offence when there is consensual relationship? Supreme Court asks
summary: A woman challenged an order of the Madhya Pradesh High Court that had quashed an FIR against her former live-in partner in a case of alleged sexual assault on a false promise of marriage . A bench of Justices B.V. Nagarathna and Ujjal Bhuyan noted that the woman lived together with the man and had a child from him .

title: SP urges Mahila police to be compassionate towards women, children
summary: Superintendent of Police Tushar Dudi instructed women police personnel to discharge duties with compassion for women and children . He said crimes against women could be dealt through community engagement and timely police intervention . Mr. Dudi informed the personnel to handle victims sensibly and ensure prompt action .

title: Sexual assault plaint: rights panel seeks report from NIT-C Director
summary: Kerala State Human Rights Commission has asked the Director, National Institute of Technology-Calicut, to submit a comprehensive report within two weeks . The case will be heard at a sitting scheduled in Kozhikode on May 29 . The order was issued by the Commission’s judicial member K. Baijunath .

title: Coast Guard gets new administrative complex in Puducherry
summary: The Coast Guard District Headquarters, Puducherry, has got a new multi-storey office complex near the helipet at Lawspet . Director General of Indian Coast Guard Paramesh Sivamani recently inaugurated the new building .

title: Mid-Day meal workers protest Andhra Pradesh government’s ‘Smart Kitchen’ proposal
summary: Andhra Pradesh Mid-Day Meal Workers Union staged a protest demanding that the government withdraw its proposal to privatise the mid-day meal scheme . Union leaders alleged that centralised cooking and supply of food to schools in boxes would result in children receiving cold and low-quality meals .

title: Union Minister flags off Narsapuram-Charlapalli weekly train
summary: The superfast express train is scheduled to depart at 8 p.m. at Narsapuram and arrive at Charlapalli 7.30 a.m the next day via Palakollu and Bhimavaram stations in the West Godavari district .

title: Cardamom price in Kerala hits a seven-year high of over ₹6,000 per kg
summary: The price of green cardamom hit a seven-year high of over ₹6,000 per kg on Monday . Spices Board sources noted that the all-time record was set in August 2019 . The average price, however, dipped to � 2,545 in the second auction .

title: APGEA welcomes High Court probe into Commercial Taxes Department
summary: The Andhra Pradesh Government Employees Association welcomed the directions issued by the High Court on a petition alleging irregularities in the Commercial Taxes Department . The APGEA president G. Suryanaraya said the court's order for a detailed inquiry into allegations against Chief Commissioner A. Ahmad Babu was a positive step towards ensuring accountability .

title: Women candidates participate in Agniveer recruitment rally in Mysuru
summary: A large number of women candidates participated in the Agniveer Recruitment rally . The nine-day recruitment rally for candidates from Karnataka and Kerala began in Mysuru on April 19 . More than 190 women candidates underwent various physical tests as part of the rally .

title: Watch: Iran needs ‘fundamental change’ to get sanctions dropped: EU
summary: Iran needs ‘fundamental change’ to get sanctions dropped, EU says . EU says Iran needs 'fundamental' change to get EU sanctions dropped . Iran must change to win EU support for sanctions to be lifted . Iran has been hit with a series of sanctions against the country since 2009 .

title: Sigma Advanced bags £300 million aerospace systems supply contract from Rolls Royce
summary: Sigma Advanced Systems has signed a seven-year agreement valued nearly £300 million . The contract provides a long-term revenue stream and stronger visibility over future growth . It is a programme-level partnership delivered through its manufacturing network across India and the UK .

title: Organs of brain dead accident victim donated at government hospital in Puducherry
summary: A 46-year-old man’s organs were donated after he was declared brain dead by doctors at Indira Gandhi Government General Hospital and Postgraduate Institute in Puducherry . According to official sources, this is the second instance in the history of the Government Hospital where organs have been retrieved from a brain-dead donor .

title: Tambaram City Police Commissioner inspects security arrangements in counting centre
summary: Sanjay Kumar inspected strong rooms and counting centre at MIT, Chromepet . Reviewed deployment of police personnel across all security layers . Directed officers to maintain strict vigilance and adhere to Election Commission protocols . All electronic voting machines (EVMs) transported under armed escort to designated counting centres .

title: Snakebite care available at key govt. hospitals in Wayanad: DMO
summary: Wayanad District Medical Officer (DMO) Rekha K.T. has urged the public to remain vigilant and seek immediate medical care in the event of a bite . People are advised to call the 108 ambulance service without delay during emergencies .

title: Death of student: hartal called on Wednesday unlikely to disrupt normal life
summary: Shops will remain open and private buses will operate as usual during a Statewide hartal called on April 28 . Nithin Raj Action Council is backed by Dalit organisations and groups, including the Socialist Unity Centre of India and Welfare Party of India .

title: Adani Airports and MakeMyTrip partner to offer pre-booking of duty-free products
summary: Travellers can browse through over 10 categories, more than 100 brands, and over 14,000 SKUs . Travellers will have access to online-exclusive offers across categories . The service is available for both departing and arriving international passengers, enabling advance planning of purchases .

title: 16 snakebite cases, 6 suspected cases reported in Kerala
summary: 16 snakebite cases and six suspected cases were reported until 6 p.m. on Monday (April 27, 2026) Several cases of suspected snakebites were reported from various places in Kerala . The cases are reported from Kozhikode, Alappuzha, Kannur, Malappuram, and Thiruvananthapuram .

title: Digital destination board of TNSTC bus altered to ‘TVK’; inquiry on
summary: Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation officials in Tirunelveli have ordered an inquiry . The digital destination board of a bus was unauthorisedly altered to ‘Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam” (TVK)

title: Fire at leased warehouse triggers negligence case
summary: The Madanayakanahalli police have registered a case under Section 287 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) against Reliance Project and Property Management Service Pvt. Ltd . The complainant, H.S. Padmaraju, stated that he owns a warehouse measuring 60,000 sq. ft. situated at Adakamaranahalli village in Dasanapura Hobli .

title: Urdu Wikisource, open source global digital library for Urdu literature, launched
summary: Urdu Wikisource is a free, open source, global digital library for Urdu literature . The Punjabi Wikimedians User Group has also urged the Punjab government to ease copyright restrictions, which continue to hamper regional languages in the digital domain .

title: Puducherry Congress, DMK, AIADMK oppose CBSE’s circular on making third language mandatory in affiliated schools
summary: The Congress, the DMK and the AIADMK oppose the move by the CBSE . The political parties were based on a CBSE circular issued by Professor and Director (Academics) CBSE, Praggya M. Singh on April 9 .

title: Mettupalayam Station set to link Rameswaram, Mangalore, Madurai and Kannur from August, Vande Bharat train extension on the anvil
summary: Southern Railway has proposed to the Railway Board operating new train services from Mettupalayam to Southern districts in Tamil Nadu and distant destinations in Kerala and Karnataka . New connectivity to Rameswaram, Mangalore, Madurai, and Kannur with stoppage at Podanur Station .

title: Woman, minor daughter found dead in farm pond in Anekal
summary: The deceased have been identified as Lavanya and her daughter Lakshita, 8, residents of Lakshmisagara village in Anekal . Lavanya had married a farmer from the same village in 2017 . The deceased were found in a farm pond on Friday (April 24)

title: Two arrested for illegally possessing pain relief tablets in Ramapuram
summary: Based on a tip-off, a police team kept watch near the Thirumalai Nagar Park in Ramapuram on Sunday . They arrested two persons — Suresh, 23, and Sriram, 19, for possessing pain relief tablets illegally .

title: IPL 2026: Selection blunders and a lack of clarity hurting CSK
summary: IPL 2026: Selection blunders and a lack of clarity hurting CSK . CSK: "Selective blunders are hurting the future of CSK" CSK has been criticized for its lack of transparency in its selection process . It will be the first time CSK will be able to make a decision on the future direction of the project .

title: Death toll in Mundathikode accident rises to 17
summary: The death toll in the Mundathikode fireworks accident has risen to 17 with the demise of Ragesh, 29, of Kundannur . Unnikrishnan, 56, from Edappal, succumbed to his injuries on the night of April 26 . Authorities have decided to hand over the remains of four victims to their relatives as DNA examination results have confirmed their identity .

title: Nandyal MP extends timely assistance for treatment of cancer patient
summary: Nandyal MP Byreddy Shabari provided timely assistance to a cancer patient during a recent public grievance meeting at her office . Family members of Tamballi Veerakumar sought help for specialised treatment . The MP coordinated with officials and facilitated a Central Government Health Scheme approval worth ₹2.4 lakh for DOTATATE therapy at KIMS Hospital, Hyderabad .

title: Delhi HC protects actor Allu Arjun’s personality rights
summary: Delhi High Court has protected the personality rights of Telugu actor Allu Arjun . Mr. Arjun has been able to establish “a strong case in his favour” Justice Tushar Rao Gedela, in an interim order passed on April 17, said .

title: Banana field gene bank inaugurated
summary: A field gene bank of banana, established at the Indian Council of Agricultural Research’s National Research Centre for Banana (NRCB) in Tiruchi, was inaugurated on Monday . The gene bank was established under a new project for maintenance of crop varieties registered with the Authority .

title: Fire safety drill at Telangana Secretariat
summary: A full-fledged fire safety mock exercise was conducted at the Dr BR Ambedkar Telangana Secretariat . Fire tenders, a firefighting robot and other equipment were deployed as part of the drill . The exercise also included a fire safety awareness programme for Secretariat staff .

title: Watch: Big trade boost: India, New Zealand ink historic deal
summary: India, New Zealand ink historic deal to boost trade between the two countries . New Zealand and India are the two biggest economies in the world . India and New Zealand are the world's most populous countries . The two countries signed the deal in a joint effort to boost their trade ties .

title: Lokesh gets warm welcome in Vizag as TDP working president
summary: TDP working president and IT, Electronics, and Education minister Nara Lokesh visited Visakhapatnam . Party cadres accorded him a grand welcome from the airport to the party office . It was his first appearance in the city after taking over as the Telugu Desam Party (TDP) working president .

title: Collector reviews arrangements for NEET in Madurai
summary: Madurai District Collector K.J. Praveen Kumar chaired a consultative meeting on preliminary arrangements and security measures for the National Eligibility- cum- Entrance Test (NEET-UG) The exam is scheduled to be conducted by the National Testing Agency on May 3 across 14 centres in Madurai district .

title: MBBS student alleges ragging, administrative inaction at Erode govt. college
summary: A third-year MBBS student at the Government Erode Medical College and Hospital in Perundurai has alleged ragging, bullying and social isolation by seniors . The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has directed the National Medical Commission and State Medical Education Department to inquire into the complaint .

title: Forest Department compiling details of open wells nearby reserve forests in Manapparai and Thuvarankurichi
summary: The Forest Department has embarked on the task of compiling details of the number of open wells in Manapparai and Thuvarankurichi forest ranges in Tiruchi district . The exercise is being carried out in the wake of incidents of wild animals having fallen into the open wells on private lands in both ranges .

title: Kurnool Collector highlights nutrition’s role in TB elimination efforts
summary: District Collector A. Siri called on the Medical and Health Department officials to intensify efforts to eliminate tuberculosis (TB) He said nutrition and the right medicines would play a key role in transforming the lives of TB patients . The Collector said that nutrition and right medicines .

title: 641 colleges join Kerala’s unified internship portal for FYUGP students
summary: Kerala State Higher Education Council (KSHEC) has successfully operationalised a comprehensive internship framework for the inaugural batch of the four-year undergraduate programme (FYUGP) The initiative marks a significant milestone in the State’s mission to bridge the gap between academia and industry .

title: PM degree case: Delhi High Court grants DU more time to file objections
summary: Delhi High Court granted Delhi University two more weeks to submit objections to the delay in filing appeals against an order refusing to disclose details of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's bachelor’s degree . The court, on February 10, granted the DU three weeks to file its objections .

title: Mosquito menace in Tamil Nadu: Madras High Court orders State-wide survey to identify hotspots
summary: Madras High Court has directed the Tamil Nadu government to conduct a comprehensive survey throughout the State within four weeks . The survey will identify hotspots of water stagnation and mosquito breeding . There were complaints of alarming increase in mosquito density in various places .

title: Campus connect
summary: Sethu Institute of Technology organised a literary event, titled ‘False Praise, Fatal Fall’ (Tragedy of King Lear – Act I) The Department of Science and Humanities, SethuInstitute of Technology, under the aegis of SAHAS, organised the literary event .

title: Thrissur Pooram concludes with ‘Upacharam Cholli Piriyal’ ritual
summary: The 2026 edition of Thrissur Pooram concluded with the ‘Upacharam Cholli Piriyal’ ritual . The idols of the Thiruvambady and Paramekkavu temples were ceremoniously taken back to their respective temples .

title: Orthodox parishes drop fireworks from fetes this year, set aside funds for charity
summary: Several parishes under the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church in Central Travancore have decided to forgo fireworks displays this year . Decision comes in response to a call by Baselios Marthoma Mathews III Catholicos in the wake of the Mundathikode fireworks accident .

title: Notice issued to Coimbatore Collector over delay in removing caste-based locality name
summary: The Tamil Nadu Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare State Commission has sought an explanation from the District Election Officer and Collector of Coimbatore over the continued use of a caste-based name in a polling station description in the electoral roll . In a communication dated April 27, 2026, Commission Chairman Imayam alias V. Annamalai, wrote .

title: Drop in KIA’s international passenger numbers in March due to West Asia crisis
summary: The ongoing crisis in West Asia has led to a reduction in international flights and passengers at the two international airports in the State during March . Bengaluru’s Kempegowda International Airport (KIA) and Mangaluru International Airport reported cancellations of flights .

title: Parking woes linger near Kamaraj market despite multi-level facility
summary: The multi-level parking facility was constructed under the Smart Cities Mission, at an outlay of around ₹10 crores and was inaugurated in May 2023 . This strategic development near the market was expected to reduce the traffic snarls in the area .

title: Brick placement for traffic islands on trial basis raises safety concern in Coimbatore
summary: Bricks placed as a temporary arrangement for traffic islands at a few locations in Coimbatore city have raised safety concerns among motorists . Road users said the arrangement lacks visibility, especially during night hours and in low-light conditions, increasing the chances of two-wheeler riders losing balance or vehicles hitting the obstruction .

title: Horticulture crop on 724 acres hit
summary: Horticulture crops grown in over 724 acres and six guntas affected by rains in Shivamogga taluk . In Holehonnur hobli, 685 acres and 13 gunstas; in Anaveri hobli about 30 acres; and in Kallihal hobli around 8 acres and 15 gun tas of horticulture crops badly hit .

title: BJP stages protest against Kharge’s remarks on Modi
summary: BJP Yuva Morcha district unit staged a protest in Kalaburagi . Mallikarjun Kharge referred to Prime Minister Narendra Modi as a ‘terrorist’ during an event in Chennai . Protesters demanded that Mr. Kharge apologise to the people of the country for his remarks .

title: Over ₹30 lakh fine slapped on motorists for traffic violations in Puducherry
summary: Puducherry police have imposed a fine of over ₹30 lakh on motorists for various traffic offences . During the drive, challans were issued to 1,495 persons for not wearing helmet, 1,095 for using mobile phone while driving, 50 for rash and negligent driving and 47 for drunken driving .

title: Detention centre for foreign nationals shifted to Jogipet in Sangareddy district
summary: Hyderabad City Police relocate and operationalised a detention centre for foreign nationals at Jogipet in Sangareddy district . The facility is meant for accommodation and processing of foreign detainees involved in violations such as illegal migration, overstaying, visa violations, human trafficking and drug peddling .

title: 13 Indian fishermen from Nagapattinam attacked, robbed of their catch mid-sea; one suspected Sri Lankan fisherman nabbed
summary: In separate incidents, 13 fishermen from Nagapattinam district were assaulted mid-sea, allegedly by Sri Lankan Tamil fishermen, and robbed of their catch and their belongings . One of the suspected Tamils — identified as Ayanthan, 27, of Kankesanthurai — was nabbed by some fishermen and brought ashore .

title: Collector calls for transparency in resurvey operations in Chittoor
summary: District Collector Sumit Kumar stressed the need for error-free and transparent resurvey operations, while directing officials to dispose of public grievances without bias . The Collector received 97 petitions, the majority of which concerned revenue issues . The majority of the petitions were related to revenue issues, the Collector said .

title: U.S. Pacific Air Forces chief’s visit to India cements defence ties
summary: The Commander of the U.S. Pacific Air Forces, General Kevin Schneider, visited India from April 19 to 25 . During the visit, Gen. Schneider held discussions with senior Indian defence leadership, including officials from the Indian Air Force, aimed at reinforcing long-standing military ties .

title: 59 offenders booked by SHE Teams in Malkajgiri; police urge women to report harassment
summary: As many as 59 persons were apprehended by SHE Teams for harassing women and girls in Malkajgiri Commissionerate limits . 49 of those apprehended were adults and 10 were minors . Counselling sessions were held for the offenders at the CP Camp Office in LB Nagar .

title: Karnataka HC refuses to quash case against man for filming voyeuristic videos of women on Bengaluru Metro and posting on social media
summary: The High Court of Karnataka refused to quash a criminal case against a 28-year-old man . The man was filming voyeuristic videos of women inside Bengaluru metro and posting them on an anonymous Instagram page called metro_chicks .

title: DGP inaugurates temporary office of Future City Police Commissionerate
summary: Telangana Director General of Police B. Shivadhar Reddy inaugurated the temporary office space for the Future City Police Commissionerate at Fab City in Thukkuguda . Special focus had been given to developing infrastructure for the new commissionerate .

title: Bodies of two recovered from Idukki in Kerala, police suspect them to be of woman, son
summary: Marykkutty and her son, Reji, had been missing since April 9, 2026 . The 70-year-old woman lived with Reji and her other son, Saji . Police recovered their decomposed remains from the premises of their house on Monday .

title: Meet in Mysuru presses for social and economic security of domestic workers
summary: A meeting of domestic workers held in Mysuru highlighted their problems and urged the authorities to ensure social and economic security for them . The Karnataka State United Domestic Workers’ Union submitted a petition to the Labour Inspector, Mysore, seeking fulfillment of their demands .

title: MITE inks pact with Wipro for opening AI Centre of Excellence
summary: Mangalore Institute of Technology and Engineering (MITE) signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Wipro Limited . The MoU aims to create a robust platform for advanced learning, skill development, and industry integration, said a release .

title: GESCOM wants swift replacement of faulty transformers
summary: Gulbarga Electricity Supply Company (GESCOM) chairman Praveen Patil Harwal has directed officials concerned to expedite restoration work across affected regions . Concern was raised over prolonged disruptions in areas like Kalaburagi Rural, Aland, Afzalpur, Kalgi, Farhatabad and Herur .

title: Bharathiar University reconstitutes 16 Syndicate Sub-Committees with nine available members
summary: Bharathiar University has reconstituted 16 sub-committees with the available strength of nine members . Three members from among academic experts nominated by the Chancellor, one university assistant professor nominated by rotation according to seniority . Decision-making in the Syndicate sittings will be easier when the vital administrative tasks are carried out .

title: Engineering student critical after self-immolation on Osmania University campus
summary: Vinesh, a first-year mining engineering student, reportedly poured petrol on himself and set himself ablaze near the Renuka Yellamma temple on the campus at around 9.30 a.m. The 19-year-old is undergoing treatment .

title: YSRCP falsely claiming credit for Google Data Centre in Vizag: TDP MLC
summary: MLC Panchumarthi Anuradha said that while the NDA government was working to transform Visakhapatnam into a global technology hub, the YSR Congress Party continued to spread negativity . She objected to YSRCP leaders taking credit for the AI-powered Google Data Centre coming up in the port city .

title: Telangana CM demands a Commission or Cabinet sub-committee for national consensus on delimitation and simultaneous polls
summary: Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy urged the Centre to constitute either a Commission or a Cabinet Sub-Committee . He said the Union Government should consult political parties, intellectuals, and social scientists before making decisions on critical issues .

title: Campaign bells fall silent for second phase of WB polls, polling for 142 seats on April 29
summary: Campaigning for the second and final phase of the 2026 West Bengal Assembly election concluded on Monday . About 3.21 crore voters across 142 constituencies and seven districts will determine the fate of 1,448 candidates when voting is held on April 29 . The votes for the Assembly election will be counted on May 4 .

title: Dindigul campus connect
summary: Kamaraj College of Engineering and Technology celebrated Placement Day 2026 . The college of engineering and technology celebrated the day 2026 at the University of Hyderabad, Hyderabad . The school celebrated Placements Day 2025 . The day is April 27, 2026, when the college celebrates Placement day .

title: Tirunelveli campus connect
summary: 196 women achievers were presented with awards . Rojavanam International School, Pudhugramam, hosted ‘Golden Star Award 2026’ 196 women were honoured with awards at the school . Women achievers honoured .

title: A.P. CM to lay stone for Google’s $15 Billion Data Centre in Visakhapatnam on April 28
summary: Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu will lay the foundation stone for Google’s massive $15 billion (₹1.35 lakh crore) AI data centre hub at Tarluvada, near here, on April 28 . The event will be attended by Google's senior executives, including Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian .

title: 962 motorists booked for drunk driving across tri-commissionerate limits
summary: A total of 962 motorists were booked for driving under the influence of alcohol . Traffic police booked 449 offenders during a two-day special drive on April 24 and 25 . Two-wheeler riders accounted for the majority at 371 cases, followed by 48 four-wheelers .

title: Court stays proceedings in Maha Kumbh Mela ‘viral’ girl’s age dispute case
summary: A POCSO Court in Thiruvananthapuram stayed further proceedings on a private complaint involving the girl who went 'viral' during the Maha Kumbh Mela . The complaint alleged that her marriage was conducted while she was a minor, and sought to arraign the political leaders who attended her wedding .

title: Mangaluru: Bharat Gas holds camp to promote mini-LPG cylinder
summary: 5 kg FTL (Free Trade LPG) is a 5 kg portable cylinder designed for easy, on-the-go cooking . The camp was organised through Bharat Gas distributor Kamadhenu Gas Agencies, Mangaluru . FTL is available at retail outlets, other shops, or LPG distributors .

title: Commuters hit by severe congestion at Thuraiyur Bus Stand
summary: Passengers report long delays as buses struggle to enter and exit the facility . Narrow approach roads, coupled with unregulated parking of two-wheelers and autorickshaws, have significantly reduced available road space . Road users have expressed frustration over the lack of effective traffic management .

title: IPS officer gets PhD from Kuvempu University for his thesis on wildlife
summary: Inspector-General Harsha P.S. has obtained the Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) from Kuvempu University . He conducted the research under the guidance of Vijay Kumar of the Department of Wildlife and Management . He submitted the thesis titled “A Study on Stability of Tiger Population in Nagarahole, Bandipura and BRT Tiger Reserves with Special Reference to Human-Tiger Conflict”

title: Tiruchi Today
summary: College of Engineering and Technology: Value added course on Design and modelling of semi conductor devices, 10 a.m. M.A. college of engineering and Technology . M.C.College of Engineering & Technology:Value added course is designed and modelled semi conductor device .

title: Consumer commission orders insurer to honour accident claim, pay compensation
summary: The Kottayam District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission has directed The New India Assurance Company to pay insurance benefits and compensation . The ruling came on a complaint filed by V.N. Shijimol, wife of the late Shibu, along with their children Shivananda and Gaurinanda .

title: Butterfly Park to stay open throughout April, May
summary: The Butterfly Park, functioning under the control of the Tamil Nadu Forest Department, will be open for visitors on all days during the months of April and May, according to an official release . In view of the summer holidays and considering the request of the general public, the Butterfly Park will be kept open .

title: Maintenance works on mechanised boats hauled ashore in Rameswaram and Pamban areas
summary: Every year, from April 15 to June 14, a government ban is imposed on fishermen from going out to sea . This is the fish breeding season across all coastal areas of Tamil Nadu . Maintenance work has begun by hauling mechanised boats onto shore in Rameswaram and Pamban .

title: Safeguard utilities before commencing roadworks, warns DC; officials to be held accountable if roads dug up after completing work
summary: Deputy Commissioner of Mysuru Lakshmikant Reddy chaired a meeting of officials from MCC and other departments on Monday in the light of the approval granted for white-topping works on 12 roads in the city with the first phase beginning on Manandavadi Road, Bogadi Road and Vishwamanava Double Road .

title: Saliyamangalam ‘Bhagavathamela’ begins on April 28

The spiritual dance dramas – ‘prahalada charithiram’ and ‘rukmini kalyanam – will be performed on April 30 and May 1 . title: How an expecting mother’s booster shots can protect her newborn from a host of diseases
summary: Babies have natural protection at birth, but their immunity is weak and not fully developed . The main way protection is achieved from mother to baby, is via placental transmission of antibodies in the third trimester . When the mother is given an antigen booster, the body is stimulated to release large amounts of the necessary proteins into her body .

title: Supreme Court dismisses a plea by former Kerala Minister to suspend his conviction in evidence tampering case

summary: Madras High Court dismisses writ petition seeking inquiry into assets declared by Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) president C. Joseph Vijay . Vijay had declared assets declared while filing his nomination papers from thePerambur Assembly constituency for the elections held this year .

summary: Four organisations working with survivors of the 1984 Bhopal gas tragedy have expressed grief over the death of Raghu Rai . They say Rai's images ensured the truth of the disaster could not be buried . title: ‘Not all seven MPs can be wrong’: Raghav Chadha on exit from AAP
summary: Raghav Chadha said the work environment in Arvind Kejriwal’s party had turned ‘toxic’ with leaders stopped from working . Chadha — along with 6 other MPs — merged with the BJP . He said he had been receiving several messages over the last three days, with many congratulating him .

title: Kerala reels under intense heat; Palakkad crosses 40°C
summary: Large parts of Kerala continue to experience hot and humid weather . Several districts recording high temperatures and no significant rainfall . Northern Palakkad was the hottest in the State, with the maximum temperature touching just over 40 degrees Celsius . The India Meteorological Department (IMD) released 24-hour data .
