Elevating the council from a curriculum advisory authority to a degree-granting institution could strengthen curriculum quality through deeper research .

The tool can find one’s name (or any other text) among the vast geological structures of Earth . title: ‘Real Kerala story’: With puja and festivities, Indian Union Muslim League hands over new houses to 51 Wayanad families

title: ‘He didn’t want to take up census duty’: Death of 2 teachers puts spotlight on process amid heatwave in Odisha

title: Tahawwur Rana, 26/11 conspirator, makes 5 visits to AIIMS, due for medical procedure next week

title: Centre picks former Union minister Dinesh Trivedi as Indian envoy to Dhaka

title: J-K High Court quashes AAP MLA Mehraj Malik’s detention under Public Safety Act

summary: A two-and-a-half-year-old girl was found wandering alone along the edge of the National Highway in Madhya Pradesh's Sheopur district . Police say she was brought home by a Bhopal-based couple when she was three months old . The couple allegedly admitted that the child had not been adopted through any legal process but was purchased by an illegal network operating out of Indore .

title: Banaras-Pune (Hadapsar) Amrit Bharat train to commence inaugural run on April 28: check stoppages, timings
summary: New train service will run between Banaras and Pune (Hadapsar) It will be flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Tuesday, April 28 . The train will cover a distance of around 1530 km in approximately 31 hours . During its journey, the train will stop at 18 stations en route .

title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project: How NHSRCL is constructing the Sabarmati River bridge
summary: The 480-metre-long Sabarmati River bridge is being built alongside the Western Railway’s Ahmedabad-Delhi main line . The 508-km-long project is passing through Gujarat, Maharashtra and Union Territory of Dadra & Nagar Haveli . NHSRCL: Bridge construction work has achieved a remarkable progress with all foundation and substructure works now complete .

title: Renaming Lansdowne will hit tourism, BJP MLA warns Centre
summary: Centre's directive to rename Lansdowne Cantonment has kicked up a row . BJP MLA Dileep Rawat has written to Defence Minister Rajnath Singh seeking reconsideration of the move . The proposal is to rename the hill station as Jaswantgarh after Rifleman Jaswant Singh Rawat, Maha Vir Chakra .

title: Railways permanently upgrades India’s 42nd Vande Bharat train to 20 coaches – check route, stoppages
summary: India’s 42nd Vande Bharat train runs betweenAhmedabadand Mumbai Central . The semi-high-speed train was flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modion March 12, 2024 . The train was initially launched with 16 coaches . In January 2026, four additional coaches were introduced for the first time .

title: Siraj-ul-Aloom, one of Kashmir’s largest seminaries, declared ‘unlawful entity’
summary: Jammu and Kashmir administration declares Siraj-ul-Uloom an unlawful entity under the Unlawful Activities Prevention Act (UAPA) The seminary is accused of having “covert linkages” with banned outfit, Jamaat-e-Islami (JeI) and of fostering “an environment conducive to radicalisation” The order said the seminary was given an opportunity to explain its position, but the objections submitted by the institute were “misconceived, factually untenable, and devoid of legal merit”

title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: Yogi Adityanath says inside Bengal’s heart is ‘Kali’, not ‘Kaba’
summary: Yogi Adityanath says inside Bengal’s heart is ‘Kali’, not 'Kaba’ He added that the state of Bengal's heart is 'Kali' and 'Bali' The state of Uttar Pradesh will go to the polls for the first time in 20 years .

title: Heatwave tightens grip over Delhi, Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan
summary: India Meteorological Department (IMD) warns of host spell persisting in isolated pockets across Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Bihar and Gujarat . Highest maximum temperature recorded Saturday was a scorching 47.4°C in Uttar Pradesh’s Banda .

title: Rajya Sabha tally: BJP’s numbers rise to 113 as 7 AAP members merge
summary: The latest Rajya Sabha party position shows a sharp shift in numbers, with the Aam Aadmi Party’s strength reduced to three MPs and the Bharatiya Janata Party's tally rising to 113 . Earlier, the AAP had 10 members in the Upper House, while the BJP had 106 . Five nominated members who joined the BJP have already been counted in the saffron party's tally .

title: Aizawl Municipal Corporation election results 2026: Counting of vites underway, ZPM leads in 5 wards
summary: Zoram People’s Movement (ZPM) leads in five wards . A voter turnout of 62.77 per cent was recorded with 2.39 lakh voters exercising their franchise . 68 candidates, including 28 women, fought the election .

title: In new video, Raghav Chadha addresses backlash on joining BJP, cites ‘toxic work environment’ in AAP
summary: Raghav Chadha Monday shared a video on Instagram addressing the criticism he is facing for switching over from Aam Aadmi Party to Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) In the video, he spoke about being flooded with messages following his announcement of joining BJP and explained his reasons behind quitting AAP . He described himself as the right person in the wrong party .

title: Indian researchers decode why standard drugs for breast cancer treatment don’t work for some patients
summary: Loss or damage to a gene called CDKN1B could be one of the reasons why standard drugs used to treat what is known as hormone receptor–positive (HR+) breast cancer) do not work effectively in some patients . HR+ type breast cancer accounts for nearly 70 per cent of all cases in India .

title: ‘Once-in-a-generation agreement’: New Zealand PM Luxon ahead of signing FTA with India
summary: New Zealand PM Christopher Luxon calls it a ‘once-in-a-generation agreement’ The free trade agreement is aimed at doubling bilateral trade to $5 billion in five years . The pact will give Indian companies duty-free access to the island nation’s markets . New Delhi will also get quota-based tariff cuts for kiwifruit and apple exports .

title: Pani puri food poisoning suspected as a child dies, 60 others fall ill in Jharkhand
summary: At least one child has died, and more than 60 people have fallen ill in two panchayats of Jharkhand’s Giridih district . Villagers alleged that all those affected had consumed pani puri sold by the same vendor . The six-year-old child who died had been brought to hospital after his condition worsened .

title: Ajit Doval meets UAE President, gives New Delhi’s Gulf outreach a push
summary: Ajit Doval met UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in Abu Dhabi on Saturday . Doval’s visit is an important outreach given that he has been coordinating with the Emirati establishment on security and strategic issues, especially related to counter-terrorism cooperation . Both sides discussed ways to strengthen the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership between the two countries .

title: Why churches in Kerala are abandoning fireworks this year
summary: Several prominent churches in Kerala have abandoned plans for fireworks displays . The St Joseph’s Church at Pavaratty in Thrissur dropped its three-day fireworks display as a mark of respect . The decision was taken in response to the appeal made by the church head .

title: Centre’s online content blocking orders double to 24,000 in a year, over half on X
summary: 60% of orders for blocking URLs were for content on X (formerly Twitter); 25% for Facebook and Instagram; and 5% for YouTube . The most recent “high-profile” complaint to the Home Ministry and MeitY came earlier this month from Congress MP Shashi Tharoor .

title: Relief from heatwave: Western disturbance likely to bring rain from April 28, IMD says
summary: A western disturbance is set to bring thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds across parts of India from April 28 . The system will trigger rainfall at isolated places, with wind speeds of 30–40 kmph . Thunderstorm activity will likely continue till at least May 2, the India Meteorological Department said . Heatwave conditions will continue over isolated pockets over the next two days .

title: EC orders crackdown in Bengal after 79 bombs recovered ahead of Phase 2 voting
summary: Election Commission has instructed West Bengal Police to carry out a special drive to arrest bomb makers . The directive comes three days before polling in the second and final phase of the West Bengal Assembly elections on April 29 . Earlier in the day, police recovered bombs and explosive material from Majherhat, in Uttar Kashipur police station area .

title: Stalled talks between Ladakh groups, Centre to resume next month
summary: Talks between the Centre and Ladakhare set to resume next month . Ladakh Lieutenant Governor V K Saxena announced Sunday that the Union Ministry of Home Affairs has taken a decision to convene a meeting of the sub-committee for political dialogue on May 22 . He also said Union Home MinisterAmit Shahwould visit Leh on April 30 for a two-day trip .

title: In Mann ki Baat, Modi hails India’s civil nuclear programme; urges citizens to wholeheartedly participate in Census
summary: PM says India's civil nuclear programme has greatly helped everyone, from agriculture to modern innovators . India's Fast Breeder Reactor in Kalpakkam,Tamil Nadu, has achieved Criticality . PM also spoke on the wind energy sector, biodiversity, Gautam Buddha’s teaching, and ongoing Census .

title: ‘Panic buying’ forces hundreds of petrol pumps to shut in Andhra, CM Naidu orders action
summary: Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu directed district authorities to take action to prevent panic buying of fuel in several cities and towns . Thousands of people lined up outside fuel stations at several locations, purchasing more fuel than required, officials said . Most of the fuel outlets that closed by Sunday afternoon did so because of panic buying rather than empty stocks .

title: Kerala’s plan to fight the heat: Water kiosks at every junction, reduced waiting at red lights, expanded monitoring system
summary: The IMD issued a yellow alert for high temperatures in several districts of Kerala for two days, warning of hot, humid weather across much of the state . Over the last week, Kerala has seen at least one suspected heatstroke death and several snakebite deaths, with experts warning that the heat could prompt snakes to venture closer to human habitation .

title: Ganga Expressway opening: PM Modi to inaugurate UP’s longest expressway on April 29
summary: The Ganga Expressway will be the longest expressway in Uttar Pradesh . Spanning 594 km, the six-lane, access-controlled high-speed corridor stretches from Meerut to Prayagraj . Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to inaugurate the expressway on April 29 . It will cut travel time from nearly 12 hours to 6-8 hours .

title: Railways sanctions Rs 300 crore to upgrade Odisha’s historic Brahmapur station
summary: Odisha’s Brahmapur railway station is one of the oldest in the country and a major rail hub in southern Odisha . It handles over 162 trains and around 25,000 passengers every day . The Ministry of Railways has decided to modernise the station under the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme (ABSS)

title: ‘Beaten, warned against leaving’: 100 tribal workers from Jharkhand flee Tamil Nadu textile factory
summary: A group of around 100 Adivasi workers from Jharkhand's West Singhbhum district have returned home over the past two days . Workers allege physical assault and non-payment of wages at a textile factory in Tamil Nadu’s Namakkal . Workers also claimed they had to arrange travel expenses themselves .

title: Eastern Railway plans platform upgrade at Howrah station for 22-24 coach trains
summary: Eastern Railway(ER) has planned to upgrade platforms at Howrah station to accommodate 22-24 coach Mail and Express trains . The zonal railway has already completed upgrading work at platforms 14 and 15 . Platform number 14 has been extended to 581 metres, enabling it to handle full-length 22 to 24 coach trains . Meanwhile, platform number 16 has been renovated under a Rs 15 crore project .

title: ‘Sheeshmahal 2’ pics sourced from Pinterest? AAP factchecks Kejriwal’s residence pictures shared by BJP
summary: Delhi minister Parvesh Verma shared images of Kejriwal’s 95, Lodhi Estate residence at a press conference . Former Delhi Chief Minister Atishi rejected the accusations, calling out the saffron party for presenting the house's “fake” pictures, taken off Pinterest, a digital 'pinboard' on which social media users post images .

title: The man who saved ‘much of the greenery we have left in Goa’
summary: Edgar Ribeiro passed away at his ancestral house in Socorro in Goa Friday night . He was a former chief planner with the Government of India . He helped move urban planning beyond rigid land-use maps to an approach integrating transport, services and land use .

title: Legend behind the lens & ‘explorer of life’, Raghu Rai dies at 83
summary: Raghu Rai passed away in Delhi on Sunday at the age of 83 . He was one of India’s foremost photojournalists, the 1972 Padma Shri awardee also recorded a spectrum of the country’�s history . His archive spans from photojournalism to documentation and portraits of some of the most recognised figures .

title: Back-to-back cub deaths in Kanha put the spotlight on tiger monitoring systems
summary: Two tiger cubs from the same litter have died within days inside Kanha Tiger Reserve . The latest cub, estimated to be between one and one-and-a-half years old, was found dead in the Sargi area of the reserve’s core zone . Forest officials said a post-mortem has been conducted and the report is awaited . The tigress had given birth to four cubs; two now remain .

title: Headmaster dies by suicide over ‘pressure to release funds for poorly made Chhattisgarh school’, contractor arrested
summary: Raju Pujari, 50, was found hanging in a forest near his home in Chhattisgarh's Bijapur district . His suicide note allegedly names three men, including contractor Devashish Mandal, who was arrested Saturday . The incident comes a year after journalist Mukesh Chandrakar was murdered allegedly by a road contractor over a report exposing substandard road work .

title: Clashes across Manipur’s valley as protests against killing of 2 Meitei children spiral
summary: Protesters clashed with security forces in Manipur’s Imphal on Saturday during a mass rally organised by a valley-based civil society organisation . The rally was aimed at ‘seeking answers’ from the state government on key issues, including the recent killings of two Meitei children in Bishnupur .

title: Skyroot flags-off Vikram-1, India’s first private orbital launch soon
summary: Skyroot Aerospace is likely to become the first private company to launch an orbital space rocket . Vikram-1 is a multi-stage launch vehicle with solid and liquid fuel-based engines designed for launching satellites weighing up to 350kg to low earth orbit . The launch of India’s first privately manufactured PSLV (polar satellite launch vehicle) is also likely to take place this year .

title: Bihar schoolteacher murdered and dumped on highway, husband who ‘remarried days later’ arrested
summary: Schoolteacher Aradhya, 23, was riding pillion on her husband's motorcycle when she disappeared on April 12 . Her husband, Deepak Kumar Sah, is accused of strangling her and dumping her body in a sack . Police recovered her remains on Friday evening and are conducting raids to arrest others .

title: ‘I will break your teeth and bury you alive’: Madhya Pradesh minister’s brother booked after threatening woman Janpad CEO
summary: Madhya Pradesh minister's brother has been booked by the police after he allegedly confronted a Janpad Chief Executive Officer in Alirajpur . Inder Singh Chouhan allegedly threatened to break her teeth and bury her alive . CCTV footage purportedly shows him charging towards the woman while being held back by two men . Police have confirmed that an FIR has been registered in the case .

title: Vande Bharat Sleeper among 2 new trains planned for Mumbai-Bengaluru route: Railway Minister
summary: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw approved a new Vande Bharat Sleeper train service between Bengaluru and Mumbai earlier this month . The new orange and grey coloured train will run from KSR Bengaluru to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT) This will be the second such train in the country .

title: School ‘harassment’ row: 6 booked under PSA for ‘vandalism’ during ­Sopore protest
summary: At least six persons have been booked under the stringent Public Safety Act (PSA) in Sopore on charges of alleged vandalism and disturbing law and order during a protest in theJammuand Kashmir city against alleged harassment of a student in a government school . The Jammu and Kashmir Police said Friday said that “in a firm action against elements involved in disturbing public order, the Sopore Police booked six miscreants under the PSA”

title: Bengaluru’s BEML Aditya plant to manufacture India’s first bullet train ‘B28’
summary: Ministry of Railways has set a target to manufacture the country’s first bullet train, ‘B28’, by March 2027 . The facility was inaugurated byRailway Minister Ashwini Vaishnawon Saturday . B28 (Bharat made bullet) trainset will undergo extensive testing and trials thereafter .

title: Mangaluru-Bengaluru Vande Bharat train to launch soon: Ashwini Vaishnaw
summary: A new semi-high-speed train will further improve rail connectivity in Karnataka . This new train is expected to be operated by South Western Railway (SWR) zone . At present, a total of 162 Vande Bharat Express trains are running across various routes in India .

title: AIIMS Delhi director, DST Secretary appointed members of NITI Aayog
summary: Ashok Lahiri has been appointed as the new vice-chairperson of the NITI Aayog . He will replace Suman Berry, who has been serving in the post since April 2022 . Abhay Karandikar, Secretary in Department of Science and Technology and AIIMSDelhidirector Dr M Srinivas are among the four new full-time Members .

title: ‘She came the night before too’: Cop’s partner kills his wife and son, daughter critical
summary: The incident occurred at an STF colony residence of Special Task Force constable Lalitesh Yadav . Sarojni Bharadwaj, 27, allegedly entered the house and got into a heated argument with his wife Reena, 32 . Reena and Aditya, 32, died in hospital, while Reena's 12-year-old daughter Naina was seriously injured . Police have arrested the accused .

title: Kerala’s first Vande Bharat train completes 3 years of service: Check route, stoppage, fare
summary: Kerala’s first Vande Bharat Express operates between Thiruvananthapuram and Kasaragod, running via Shoranur Junction . The train was launched on April 25, 2023, by Prime Minister Narendra Modi . It covers a distance of 588 km in about 8 hrs and 5 minutes from both ends .

title: Ram Madhav apologises for his remarks on India-US ties; Rahul hits out at RSS
summary: RSS leader Ram Madhav issued an apology for his “factually incorrect” statement on India-US relations . Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi said that the comments “’revealed the Sangh’s true nature”

title: Railways to launch 2 Amrit Bharat trains from Uttar Pradesh on April 28 – check routes, stoppages
summary: Indian Railways set to launch two new Amrit Bharat Express trains from Uttar Pradesh . The two new trains will operate between Banaras and Hadapsar, and Ayodhya Cantt and Lokmanya Tilak Terminus (LTT) These trains offer a travel experience comparable to theVande Bharat .

title: Centre allows American India Foundation to receive foreign funding
summary: The American India Foundation (AIF) Trust has been granted registration under the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA) The move will enable it to receive foreign funding for its initiatives . Earlier this month, MHA also granted FCRA registration for Babhya Pradesh godman Dhirendra Krishna Shastri's Baba Bageshwar Dham .

title: Drug crackdown in Kashmir: 20 hotspots raided in Anantnag, properties of ‘kingpins’ under scanner
summary: Police conducted raids targeting drug distribution networks across south Kashmir’s Anantnag district . Move comes amid aJammu& Kashmir-wide crackdown on drug networks led by the administration . Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha launched the ‘Drug-Free J&K’ campaign in Jammu on April 11 .

title: Exclusive: Decomposed samples, broken custody chain, and evidence that wasn’t – Bhopal ‘cow slaughter’ case unravels
summary: Bhopal Municipal Corporation got embroiled in a major controversy over allegations that a slaughterhouse that operated under a public-private partnership had slaughtered cows . Police seized meat from a refrigerated truck in December last year based on allegations raised by a group called Ma Bhawani Sangathan that the slaughterhouse at Jinsi was slaughtering cows and passing it off as buffalo meat .

title: ‘Remember the name: Telangana Rashtra Sena’ — With new party, Kavitha revives TRS name, takes on KCR and rivals
summary: Kalvakuntla Kavitha launched her party Telangana Rashtra Sena (TRS) Saturday morning . She is the daughter of former Chief Minister and Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) supremo K Chandrashekar Rao . She quit the BRS party seven months ago, over differences with her brother and BRS working president K Taraka Rama Rao .

title: ‘Significant morale booster for BJP in Punjab’
summary: BJP welcomed seven AAP Rajya Sabha MPs to its fold on Friday . Political observers say it will strengthen the party when it comes to public perception and enhance its negotiating leverage for a possible pre-poll alliance with the Akalis for the 2027 Punjab elections . BJP national general secretary Tarun Chugh, a crucial link to the party’s organisation in Punjab, termed it “a historic day for Punjab”

title: Instagram user posted video alleging VIP bias in Kedarnath devotee’s death. He now faces FIR
summary: The Uttarakhand Police registered a case against an Instagram content creator for allegedly “spreading false information” in connection with the death of a devotee at Kedarnath on Wednesday . The content creator, Nitin Soni, runs a page called “thecurlypoet” with 8 lakh followers . He has accused the government of taking down his original post .

title: Saudi job to Op Sarp Vinash: J&K man who had key role in 2003 strike on terror stronghold dies at 63
summary: Fazal Tahir, a Muslim Gujjar, died of a heart attack in Uttarakhand and was laid to rest in his native Murrah village . He was instrumental in the Indian Army's Operation Sarp Vinash, an exercise to purge Hilkaka of terrorists who had established a parallel state there . Tahir was a Special Police Officer in Jammu& Kashmir Police .

title: ‘We agreed to stop buying oil from Iran, Russia… tariffs’: RSS’s Madhav expresses surprise at strain in India-US ties
summary: Ram Madhav spoke at a panel discussion at the Hudson Institute in Washington on Thursday . He said the three pillars underpinning the India-US relationship were under stress . He flagged anxiety within the Indian diaspora over remarks such as ‘hell hole’ and ‘laptop-wielding charlatans’

title: Rahul Gandhi opposed Raj Kumar Goel’s name, gave three alternatives for CIC post: DoPT documents
summary: Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi dissented over the selection of Raj Kumar Goel as Chief Information Commissioner (CIC) However, the selection committee headed by Prime Minister Narendra Modi proceeded with Goel’s recommendation during its meeting on December 10 last year . All shortlisted candidates were serving or retired IAS officers .

title: Former CEA Ashok Lahiri to be Niti Aayog vice-chairman
summary: Renowned economist Ashok Lahiri and scientist Gobardhan Das named as Niti Aayog vice-chairman and member . Lahiri is a BJP MLA in West Bengal and is a former chief economic advisor to the government of India . Das is the director of the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER),Bhopal .

title: Rahul Gandhi to begin 3-day visit to Andaman and Nicobar Islands tomorrow
summary: Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi will embark on a three-day visit to Andaman and Nicobar Islands starting Sunday . The Congress leader’s visit comes in the backdrop of his meeting with members of the Great Nicobar tribal council last month . The tribal council had invited him to the island during this meeting .

title: Jal Shakti Ministry PSUs refer corruption cases against their officials to CBI
summary: Water and Power Consultancy Services (WAPCOS) and the National Projects Construction Corporation Limited (NPCC) wrote on April 24 to the CBI to investigate and file FIRs . The NPCC has referred to CBI for investigation into alleged “corrupt practice and demand of money against works” involving one of its Executive Director Dharmendra Mudgal .

title: West Asia conflict: No BRICS consensus on war, India’s Chair summary notes ‘deep concern’
summary: India has not been able to forge a consensus among the BRICS grouping on the US-Israel war on Iran . Delhi issued a Chair’s summary on Friday and said that “members expressed deep concern on the recent conflict in the Middle East [West Asia] and offered views and assessments on the matter” The BRICS Foreign ministers’ meeting on May 14-15 is likely to be held in India .

title: AAP moves to disqualify Raghav Chadha, rebel MPs after BJP switch
summary: AAP leader Sanjay Singh says he will write to the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha seeking their disqualification . The development follows a major political setback for AAP, with seven of itsRajya Sabha MPs resigning from the party . More than two-thirds of the party’s Upper House MPs have quit and will join the BJP as a faction .

title: Extreme events could impact 36% of land animal habitats by 2085, says new study
summary: By 2050, 74% of animal habitats on land will be exposed to heatwaves, 16% to wildfire, 8% to droughts, and 3% to floods . Extreme climate events can have devastating impacts on terrestrial biodiversity, study says . Biodiversity hotspots such as the Amazon, tropical Africa and Southeast Asia will see a sharp increase in frequency of these extreme events .

title: Goa row: YouTuber detained in Himachal over remarks on St Francis Xavier; crime branch to bring him back
summary: Gautam Khattar has been detained by Himachal Pradesh Police from Kullu . He was booked for allegedly hurting religious sentiments for making derogatory remarks against St Francis Xavier . Police had lodged a case against him under sections 299 (deliberate and malicious acts, intended to outrage religious feelings of any class by insulting its religion or religious beliefs)

title: In major setback to AAP, seven of its 10 Rajya Sabha MPs cross over to BJP
summary: Seven of AAP's 10 Rajya Sabha members cross over to the BJP, creating ripples in political circles . The AAP reacted angrily, calling it a “betrayal” and another “Operation Lotus” of the BJP . With the “merger’s’ of the AAP faction, the BJP strength in the 245-member Sabha is set to rise to 113 .

title: Heatwave alert: IMD warns of 45 degrees in north, central India over next 3 days
summary: An intense heatwave is likely to grip the plains of northwest and central India over the next 3–4 days . Maximum temperatures across northwest, central, and parts of peninsular India have remained significantly high . Highest temperature recorded was 44.5°C in Sri Ganganagar (Rajasthan), the IMD said .

title: 70-year-old in Madhya Pradesh abducted, beaten, forced to drink urine after son ‘elopes’ with minor
summary: A 70-year-old man was abducted, beaten and forced to drink urine by a group of 10–12 men . The incident took place on March 7, but came to light after a video of the assault was circulated on social media . Police say the complaint was connected to an earlier case where his son had taken away a minor girl .

title: 3 killed in 2 gunfights as fresh tensions erupt in Manipur’s Ukhrul
summary: A Tangkhul man, identified as Horshokmi Jamang (29), a resident of Chatric Khullen village in Kamjong district, was killed in the gunfight . Police said that another gunfight broke out close by, in Mullam village in Ukhrul district, in a Kuki-inhabited village .

title: From Manipur and Mizoram to Tel Aviv, 240 from ‘lost tribe’ make their way to Israel
summary: The B’nei Menashe are a Jewish community among the Mizo and Kuki tribal communities of Manipur and Mizoram . They believe they are descendants of one of the ‘ten lost tribes of Israel’ They are not considered Jewish under Israeli law, and therefore their relocation to Israel required special authorisation . The new immigrants will be settled in various parts of northern Israel .

title: 73 Opposition MPs sign fresh notice to remove Gyanesh Kumar as CEC
summary: The Opposition submitted a fresh notice signed by 73 MPs in Rajya Sabhaseeking a motion to remove CEC Kumar . The notice was submitted to the Rajya Sabha Secretary General by Congress MP Jairam Ramesh and TMC MP Sagarika Ghose . The first charge against Kumar is regarding the “partisan asymmetry” in the enforcement of the Model Code of Conduct .

title: RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank licence
summary: The Reserve Bank of India has cancelled the banking licence issued to Paytm Payments Bank Limited . The bank is severely limited to processing withdrawals of existing deposits and facilitating loan referrals . Paytm had obtained a limited banking license in ‌August 2015 (backed by One 97 Communications)

title: ‘BJP has jolted Punjabis’: Arvind Kejriwal’s first reaction after Raghav Chadha exit
summary: AAP MP Raghav Chadha announced that he, along with other party MPs, would merge with the Bharatiya Janata Party . Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann said that the BJP is a party of “useless leaders” Mann said the people who are leaving AAP are “gaddaars” (traitors) Mann said, “Punjabis are with AAP.”

title: Raghav Chadha leads big mutiny. What this means for AAP
summary: Raghav Chadha announced on Friday that he and six of the AAP MPs in the Upper House had “merged the party with the BJP” The AAP has 10 Members in the Rajya Sabha – seven from Punjab and three from Delhi . Seven AAP MPs met BJP chief Nitin Nabin at the party headquarters .

title: ED raids expose foreign debit card network linked to unregistered group
summary: The Enforcement Directorate has uncovered a suspected cross-border financial network involving the use of foreign bank debit cards to withdraw large sums of cash across India . The discovery was made following raids at six locations across multiple states on April 18 and 19 . The Timothy Initiative (TTI) in India is not registered under the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA)

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary wins trust vote, Tejashwi says BJP ‘finished Nitish ji’
summary: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary wins confidence vote of confidence in Bihar Assembly . Opposition leader Tejashwi Yadav questions the need for a special session . Yadav warns the Chief Minister to guard his position carefully, suggesting there were others in the BJP eyeing it . Chaudhary says the mandate ultimately came from the people .

title: ‘Punjab will not forget these 7 traitors’: AAP after Raghav Chadha, 6 others quit party
summary: Raghav Chadha, Ashok Mittal, Sandeep Pathak, and 4 other AAP MPs quit the party . Party leader Sanjay Singh said Punjab will “never forget these traitors who stabbed the people in the back” Singh further attacked the BJP, saying the party was conducting “Operation Lotus’s” ahead of the Punjab elections to break the AAP . Singh further said: “The people of Punjab should remember these 7 names. The people will never forget them”

title: Split-AAP: Raghav Chadha leads revolt, joins BJP with 6 MPs
summary: AAP Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha has joined the BJP along with Ashok Mittal and Sandeep Pathak . Chadha said, “We have decided that we, the two-third members belonging to the AAP, merge ourselves with the BJP’s.”

title: 9 charges of ‘proven misbehaviour’: Opposition files fresh notice to remove CEC Gyanesh Kumar
summary: The Opposition submitted a fresh notice in Rajya Sabha seeking the removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar, listing nine charges of “proven misbehaviour” and backed by 73 MPs from multiple parties . The first charge against Kumar is “continued partisan asymmetry in the enforcement of the model code of conduct” The second charge is ‘partisan and public denunciation of a recognised political party on the official social media handle of the Election Commission’s “straight-talk” post .

title: Top News Headlines of April 24, 2026 | Trump dispatches Witkoff, Kushner to Pakistan for new talks with Iran’s foreign minister, JD Vance won’t attend: White House
summary: Trump dispatches Witkoff, Kushner to Pakistan for new talks with Iran’s foreign minister . JD Vance won’t attend: White House won't attend; JD Vance will not attend; White House says he won't be in Pakistan .

title: Congress leader’s 19-year-old son shot dead in midnight attack on their Chhattisgarh home
summary: The 19-year-old son of Congress leader Sammelal Kashyap was shot dead in Chhattisgarh's Janjgir-Champa district . His younger son was injured in the attack by three gunmen who barged into their home . The incident occurred around 12.30 am in Karhi village at the residence of Congress block-level leader . The family runs a business supplying cement and sand for construction work .

title: ‘Bengal first, Delhi next’: Mamata’s warning to BJP ahead of phase 2 polls
summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee hinted that after the elections are over she will be targeting Delhi . She said: “Not only will there be destruction in Bengal, but the BJP will also have to be removed from Delhi…” She alleged that Modi’s stop to eatjhal muriin Jhargram was carefully orchestrated .

title: ‘What is PM scared of?’ Opposition slams Modi’s silence over Trump’s ‘hellhole’ remark support
summary: Congress national president and Leader of Opposition in Rajya SabhaMallikarjun Kharge criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi for his silence over the matter . Kharge also wondered what PM Modi is scared of. “Indians have played a vital role in America’s success. Modi ji remains absolutely mum on these ridiculous utterances.” Trinamool Congress MPMahua Moitra asked if he would register a protest against Trump for calling India a “hell hole” and all Indians “gangsters with laptops”

title: ‘Custodial interrogation needed to track associates’: Why Gauhati HC rejected Pawan Khera’s anticipatory bail plea
summary: Gauhati High Court rejects Pawan Khera’s anticipatory bail plea in connection with an FIR registered against him based on a complaint by Assam Chief MinisterHimanta Biswa Sarma's wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sarma . Assam Advocate General Devajit Saikia argued that the police have already detected that the documents he based his claims on are false . The court noted that the Advocate General “seems to have force in it” with the petitioner not making any claims that police have fabricated claims .

title: Nitish’s heir apparent puts himself ‘under probation’: Why Nishant is in no rush for a title
summary: Nishant Kumar has been seen as the long-term leader of the JD(U) However, he has not yet taken up any key post in the party . Nishant is set to embark on a yatra across Bihar from West Champaran next month .

title: Manipur again on the boil around them, bodies of two children wait for ‘justice’
summary: Five-year-old Tomthin and five-month-old Yaisana died together, instantly, in their sleep in Bishnupur . Their mother, Binita, 37, suffered splinter injuries and has moved to her parents’ home in Imphal East to recuperate, away from the media glare . Five suspected cadres of the United Kuki National Army have been arrested and their role is being investigated .

title: China in mind, India and Japan move to deepen defence ties
summary: Japan relaxed restrictions on its arms exports, and this is seen as a major departure from the pacifism that has characterised its post-World War II defence policy . Restrictions that limit arms exports to just five categories – rescue, transport, warning, surveillance and minesweeping – will be lifted . This means Japan can now sell lethal weapons to the 17 countries with which it has defence agreements .

title: India, UK vow cooperation amid ‘turbulent geopolitical environment’
summary: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval held extensive talks with his British counterpart Jonathan Powell . The two sides agreed to further strengthen the India-UK Comprehensive Strategic Partnership against the backdrop of a “turbulent geopolitical environment” Doval highlighted that dialogue and diplomacy was the only way forward to resolve the conflict .

title: Kharge’s remarks on Modi: Congress alleges EC issuing ‘casual’ notices, seeks time to reply
summary: Election Commission issued a show-cause notice to Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge over his alleged remarks on Prime Minister Modi . The Congress party sought a hearing and more time to file a detailed reply to the poll panel . The party also alleged that issuing the notice showed “non-application of mind’ by the EC’s poll panel, despite “knowing the hectic election schedule”

title: Indians no longer need transit visas for layover flights via France
summary: The exemption applies to Indian nationals transiting through France to board a connecting flight, without leaving the international zone of the airport . The move follows an announcement by French President Emmanuel Macron during his visit to India in February, that measures would be taken to ease Indian nationals’ travel via France .

title: ‘Party gave me lesson’: BJP MLA apologises for remarks against cop after son ‘rammed Thar into 5 people’
summary: Mahdya Pradesh BJP MLA Pritam Singh Lodhi has issued a public apology and said he would abide by the party’s decision in the matter . The apology comes after days of escalating statements by the MLA, including a threat to mobilise 10,000 people and fill the residence of Karera SDOP Ayush Jakhad with cow dung, and earlier remarks challenging the officer's authority .

title: Tragedy after funeral in Uttarakhand: Vehicle falls 300 metres as driver loses control at curve, 8 killed
summary: Eight people were killed, and two were injured after a vehicle fell into a gorge in Uttarakhand’s Tehri Garhwal on Thursday . The vehicle fell about 300 metres down into the gorge at around 3 pm . The group was returning to Ghansali after attending a funeral in Rishikesh .

title: ‘It will be a message of stability in a turbulent world’: New Delhi to host fourth India-Africa Forum Summit next month
summary: New Delhi will host the fourth India-Africa Forum Summit next month . The event will bring together leaders from across the African continent and charting a roadmap for further cooperation between the two sides across various sectors . External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar said the two regions are not merely partners in development, but also “in shaping a better world”

title: 2 rapes and a murder in span of hours, Alwar village left with more questions than answers
summary: Rahul Meena, 23, is accused of raping two women and murdering one of them in Delhi . Meena was arrested for killing an IRS officer’s daughter at their family home in Delhi on April 22 . He used to work at their residence before he was let go earlier this year . Police say he used a screwdriver to open the safe before stealing in cash .

title: Over 96% polling in Bengal seat with highest SIR deletions in Phase 1
summary: In West Bengal, where 152 of the total 294 Assembly seats went to polls in the first phase, the turnout was 92.88%, according to the ECINET app data around midnight . In Tamil Nadu, according to ECINNET app data, the turnout was 85.15% . While women voters recorded 85.76% polling on Thursday, male voters recorded 83.57% .

title: Ahead of Bihar floor test, NDA asserts ‘brute majority’ under Samrat Choudhary
summary: Deputy Chief Minister Vijay Kumar Choudhary said the “Nitish Kumar-blessed government” has a brute majority in the House . The National Democratic Alliance won 202 of 243 seats in the 2025 Assembly polls . He said the new government will stick to policies followed by the Nitish Kumar government .

title: ‘In poor taste’: MEA hits back at MAGA influencer’s comment reposted by Trump
summary: US President Donald Trump reposted a comment by a prominent MAGA influencer and supporter calling India and China “hellhole on the planet” India on Thursday said this was “uninformed”, “inappropriate” and ‘in poor taste” The US Embassy sought to control the damage by putting out a message that Trump believes India is a “great” country led by a ‘good friend of mine”

title: Sibling rivalry, mystery death, black magic: Behind an Odisha businessman’s murder
summary: Businessman Sudhir Kumar Patra, 47, was abducted in Odisha's Gopalpur on April 15 . Police recovered his decomposed body a week later on the side of a road leading to a jungle . Investigators say they cracked the case by connecting the dots between an earlier untimely death in the family, the suspicion of black magic, business rivalry between two brothers, and a revenge plot .

title: Summer vacation 2026: Railways adds 132 coaches to 104 trains to ease rush – Full list
summary: The North Western Railway has added 132 extra coaches to 104 trains to ease passenger rush . This augmentation will generate additional berths, further improving the chances of ticket confirmation . A total of 908 summer special trains have been approved for operation between April 15 and July 15, 2026 .

title: In today’s interconnected world, partnerships are not optional but essential: Rajnath in Germany
summary: Indian companies keen to engage with German firms for co-development and co-production in areas such as advanced radar technology, multi-sensors, AI-enabled Unmanned Aerial Systems, Sonobuoys and High Power Low Frequency Underwater Transmitters . Singh is currently on athree-day official visit to Germany .

title: Mitigate arsenic & fluoride contamination in groundwater, NGT tells states
summary: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has instructed the state governments to mitigate the arsenic and fluoride contamination of groundwater . The court has also directed the Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) to regularly monitor the mitigation measures adopted across states . Consumption of the arsenic through drinking water is a major health concern, causing arsenicosis .

title: Banaras-Hadapsar Amrit Bharat Express: Train number, route, stoppages, frequency, time table
summary: The new Amrit Bharat Express will run between Banaras and Hadapsar (Pune) The new train is expected to offer affordable service for passengers from lower and lower-middle income category . The train will have 22 coaches comprising Sleeper, General Second Class and others .

title: Delhi sizzles under heatwave grip, relief unlikely this week as IMD maintains 44°C warning
summary: Delhi-NCR is heading into an intense spell of heat, with temperatures already soaring past seasonal norms . Maximum temperatures are expected to climb by 1-2°C over the next two days . The mercury has already been hovering between 40C and 42C, with Safdarjung recording 40.7°C, about 3C above normal . The heatwave is likely to persist over the weekend and some relief may be possible next week .

title: ‘Life term or death’: NCB warns Indians travelling to Maldives after it toughens drug laws
summary: Narcotics Control Bureau issues strong advisory to Indian citizens travelling to or visiting Maldives . Maldives recently strengthened anti-narcotics laws impose life imprisonment and death penalty for drug-related offences . Recent arrests of foreign nationals, including Indian citizens, in the Maldives for drug offences prompted the alert .

title: UP’s Shekha Jheel joins Ramsar List, India nears milestone of 100 sites
summary: Uttar Pradesh has also emerged as the leading state in India for Ramsar-designated wetlands, now boasting 12 such sites . The total number of wetlands recognized internationally now stands at 99 . Yadav hailed Prime Minister Narendra Modi for leading the country’s ecosystem restoration mission .

title: Mahila thana to Rs 36,000 women’s allowance: PM Modi announces ’10 guarantees’ ahead of Bengal phase 2 polls
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed a poll rally in West Bengal’s Krishnanagar in Nadia district ahead of the second phase of polling . He pledged a set of ten guarantees, which aims to empower the women and children, if it comes to power . These guarantees include direct cash transfers of Rs 36,000 to women's bank accounts in a single year, financial assistance of Rs 50,000 for young women for their graduation .

title: NEET UG 2026: NTA flags West Asia conflict, poll clash as key challenges
summary: National Testing Agency Director General Abhishek Singh said the challenge for the Agency this year will be to conduct the NEET-UG exam in West Asia and in poll-bound states, with the counting of votes scheduled for the day after the exam . Singh, a 1995-batch IAS officer who took charge as NTA Director General earlier this month, said the major challenges this year are the war in the Middle East and elections in the states .

title: ‘I have moved on (from autobiography row)… no harm if manuscripts are vetted before publication’: General Naravane to Express
summary: Former Army Chief General Manoj Mukund Naravane says he has put the controversy over his unpublished autobiography behind him . His target is now to write one book a year, and the books will be in various genres – both fiction and non-fiction . He did not get into specifics about whether copies of his unpublished title were actually printed or not .

title: Dalit groom dragged off horse, attacked mid-procession in Madhya Pradesh: ‘They said it’s not for us’
summary: Golu Ahirwar was taking part in his Rachwai, a customary ritual, when his procession was stopped in a narrow lane . According to his family, a vehicle was deliberately parked to block the route . When relatives asked for passage, a group of local men confronted them and escalated the situation into violence . Video footage from the scene shows the procession breaking into chaos as the attackers target the groom and those accompanying him .

title: ASI begins drilling into Konark Sun Temple to remove 100-year-old British-era sand
summary: Archaeological Survey of India has begun drilling a passage into the Jagamohan (assembly hall) of the 13th-century Konark Sun Temple to remove sand packed inside by the British over a century ago . Officials said successful drilling is crucial for sand removal . Once the passage is drilled, sand removal will begin manually .

title: Temple, township, skills: What Bihar’s first cabinet meeting under Samrat Choudhary proritised
summary: Cabinet meeting was first in a decade without Nitish Kumar at the helm . Meeting focused on reforms in registration, tourism, skill training, urban planning, policing, disaster response and land allocation . Cabinet approved procurement of over 4,000 two-wheelers for police personnel, including 1,500 scooters for women personnel or “police didi”

title: West Bengal reports over 91% voting, 84% in Tamil Nadu
summary: West Bengal saw an overall voter turnout of 91.35 percent in Phase 1 of the polls . Tamil Nadu went to polls on April 23 for 234 election constituencies, with the key constituencies being Kolathur, Edappadi, Tiruchirappalli East, Coimbatore South, and Dr Radhakrishnan Nagar . The counting of votes for both the states – as well as Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry – will take place on May 4 .

title: Along a shiny new expressway, a sole house and its lone caretaker are the face of an old fight
summary: Jaipal Singh has been guarding a two-storey house spread across almost 1,600 sq m in Mandola near Loni in Ghaziabad . The house falls in the middle of a service road for the Delhi-Dehradun Expressway, inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on April 14 . Singh, 46, has been working at the house since 2022, after its owner died .

title: Tamil Nadu Election 2026 Live Updates: State records 84.29% voter turnout as polling concludes
summary: Tamil Nadu Election 2026 Live Updates: State records 84.29% voter turnout as polling concludes . Tamil Nadu election 2026 live updates from polling stations across the state . State recorded 84.30% voter turnouts as polling ends in Tamil Nadu .

title: West Bengal Election 2026 Live Updates: Voting ends across 152 seats in West Bengal Phase 1 with 92.25% turnout
summary: West Bengal Election 2026 Live Updates: Voting ends across 152 seats in West Bengal Phase 1 with 92.25% turnout . West Bengal election 2026 live updates from West Bengal elections 2026 . Voting in Phase 1 of West Bengal phase 1 was held in the state capital, Kolkolkata .

title: MP Cabinet clears 4x land compensation, eyes faster project rollout and fewer protests
summary: Madhya Pradesh Cabinet clears a sweeping set of decisions that attempt to fuse welfare politics with infrastructure-led growth . The move is a politically sensitive intervention in a state where agrarian distress and land conflicts have long shaped electoral outcomes . The Cabinet simultaneously cleared Rs 33,985 crore for infrastructure spanning irrigation, roads, health and education .

title: India International Water Week in Sept
summary: The 9th edition of India International Water Week (IIWW-2026) will be held on the theme ‘Climate Resilient Water Management’ from September 22-26 this year . The event, organised by the Union Ministry of Jal Shakti, will include 49 sessions across various themes in the water sector .

title: Modi may travel to Europe in May for India-Nordic Summit
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will travel to Europe for the India-Nordic Summit in Oslo, Norway, next month . The Indian government and Nordic countries — which includes Norway, Sweden, Finland, Denmark and Iceland — have been discussing scheduling Modi’s visit since early this year . The major topic of discussion is expected to be the impact of the war in West Asia .

title: Rail corridor for Odisha bauxite mines days after tribal-police clash
summary: Centre has notified a dedicated rail corridor for the Sijimali and Kutrumali mines . The objective of the special railway project is to strengthen mineral evacuation and improve logistics connectivity to bauxite reserves . The notification came days after a violent clash broke out between tribals and police over the construction of a 3.5-km approach road .

title: ‘Won’t bow to terror’: President, PM pay homage to Pahalgam attack victims
summary: President Droupadi Murmu said the country remains resolute in its determination to defeat terrorism everywhere in all its forms . Prime Minister Narendra Modi, union ministers and leaders of different political parties paid homage to the victims . Defence Minister Rajnath Singhalso took to social media, saying, “We will never forget those wounds inflicted on our nation”

title: Rajnath in Germany: Defence cooperation pacts inked, talks held
summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh inked and exchanged the Defence Industrial Cooperation Roadmap and Implementing Arrangement for Cooperation in UN Peacekeeping Training . Singh is on an official three-day visit to Berlin, where he interacted with the Indian community and addressed German parliamentarians . Singh also visited the TKMS submarine building facility in Kiel, accompanied by Boris Pistorius .

title: Air raid blackout exercises announced in parts of Kashmir Valley
summary: The mock drills will be conducted by J-K’s Civil Defence for “emergency preparedness and safety training” The Deputy Commissioner (DCs) of Kupwara, Anantnag and Ganderbal have issued public notices asking people to switch off lights in the evening on drill days .

title: Sluggish wheat procurement in Madhya Pradesh drags down India’s Central pool sourcing by 16%
summary: Overall wheat procurement in the country has been significantly lower during the ongoing rabi marketing season 2026-27 as compared to last year . MP is the second biggest contributor to the Central pool maintained by the government to distribute food grain under the National Food Security Act, 2013 .

title: Goa row: YouTuber’s brother held over remarks on St Francis Xavier; LOC out for Gautam Khattar
summary: Gautam Khattar was booked for allegedly hurting religious sentiments with derogatory remarks against St Francis Xavier . Goa Police arrested his brother, Madhav, from Uttarakhand, on Wednesday . Police said he had accompanied his brother to the event and recorded and broadcast the speech .

title: She had declined his proposals. Days later, her 11-year-old son was found dead
summary: Shivraj Rajak was found with his throat slit inside his home after police broke open the door following a missing child report . Mathura Rajak, 45, who ran a nearby ironing shop, was arrested the next evening after locking his home and shop and switching off his phone . The family said they had faced persistent pressure, threats and a confrontation a day before the murder .

title: Tamil Nadu Election 2026: Check date and time for voting, poll results, and other details
summary: On April 23rd, Tamil Nadu will conduct its Assembly elections in a single phase . Voters from 234 constituencies will elect a representative for the 17th Legislative Assembly . The counting is scheduled to take place on May 4th, 2026 . Adults above the age of eighteen, male, female, and third gender are eligible to vote .

title: Pahalgam terror attack: US, EU express solidarity with India in fight against terror
summary: US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor paid tribute to the victims of the Pahalgam terror attack . The European Union and its 27 member states reaffirmed their unwavering support for India . The EU delegation also emphasised its solidarity with the grieving families and the Indian government . Argentina’s Ambassador Mariano Caucino also posted on X .

title: How to check your name on voter list ahead of Tamil Nadu Elections 2026, West Bengal Phase 1 polls
summary: Election Commission of India has enabled online tools to help voters verify registration . Voters can access the electoral roll through the official portal of the Election Commission (https://voters.eci.gov.in/) Tamil Nadu will vote in a single phase on April 23across all 234 constituencies . West Bengal will also enter Phase 1 of its Assembly elections, covering 152 constituencies, with the second phase scheduled for April 29 .

title: Govt rejects claims Indian oil tanker fired at by Iran had paid to cross Hormuz
summary: The government has categorically dismissed speculation that the captain of Sanmar Herald tried to secure safe passage for the vessel by paying individuals who claimed to represent the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) navy . The Indian government has stated on multiple occasions that none of the Indian vessels have paid any toll to cross the Strait of Hormuz . Iran had announced that the Strait would be completely open for commercial vessels for the remainder of the US-Iran ceasefire .

title: India may play role in bringing peace… can’t rule it out, says Rajnath
summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh made the comments during an interaction with the Indian community on Tuesday in Berlin . Singh said India was the least affected country in the West Asia crisis . He said neither the US nor Iran considers India an enemy because of India’s balanced approach towards the crisis .

title: Temple row erupts in Kerala after bhajan group sings Christian hymn, sparks debate on faith and harmony
summary: Nandagovindam Bhajans sang a Christian hymn at Sri Mahavishnu temple in Kottayam, Kerala . Right-wing groups slammed the performance of the hymn in a political row . The temple backed the group, saying Hindus and Christians live in harmony in the village .

title: Freebies worth over Rs 1,000 crore seized in Bengal, Tamil Nadu: EC
summary: Authorities seize cash, liquor, drugs, precious metals and other freebies in connection with West Bengal and Tamil Nadu Assembly elections . Freebies worth a total of Rs 1,072.13 crore had been seized until Wednesday, starting from February 26 . The EC had announced elections in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, Kerala and Assam on March 15 .

title: No marriage during course? Backlash forces Bihar nursing school to scrap notice, probe on
summary: General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM) training school in Bihar’s Hathua advised students not to marry during the academic session . The notice was treated as an official directive, triggering confusion and pushback from students and families, with several objecting to its wording . Principal Mansi Singh said the notice had been misinterpreted and stemmed from persistent attendance issues .

title: BJD protests voter roll ‘deletions’; flags risk to 9.8 lakh names, says migrants most vulnerable
summary: Around 9.8 lakh names have been identified for potential deletion during the elector mapping exercise in Odisha ahead of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) exercise . The Biju Janata Dal (BJD) has raised serious concerns over ‘potential large-scale disenfranchisement’ of eligible voters . Naveen Patnaik-led party staged a protest outside the office of the Chief Electoral Officer, Odisha .

title: Manipur moves toward resettlement of displaced persons: Panel assesses community assets’ plan
summary: Manipur has seen around 58,821 people displaced amid clashes between Meitei and Kuki-Zo communities since May 3, 2023 till March 30 this year . The latest proposal aims to foster self-sustaining habitats, addressing long-term needs in relief camps now transitioning to permanent settlements .

title: 5 MP cops suspended after businessman alleges they stole gold worth 36 lakh
summary: Businessman Gaurav Jain alleges police broke into his home in connection with a cheque-bounce case against him . Businessman says police broke down door, misbehaved with his family members and left with gold jewellery and silverware . The incident took place on the night of April 1 and police have suspended the officers .

title: Thrissur fire tragedy: Kerala orders judicial probe; Pooram fireworks likely scrapped
summary: Retired High Court judge C N Ramachandran Nair will probe the incident, which the state cabinet has declared a state-specific disaster . The tragedy struck a fireworks manufacturing unit assigned by Thiruvambadi Sri Krishna Temple . The unit was in Mundathikode village, 18 km from Thrissur .

title: ‘Learn to say Jai Shri Ram’: Himanta’s dig at Mamata on eve of Bengal polls
summary: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma accused Mamata Banerjee of sidelining Hindus . Assam chief accused her of raising ‘Inshallah’ slogans while ignoring “Jai Shri Ram” A total of 152 constituencies in 16 districts of Bengal will vote on Thursday in the first phase .

title: Pahalgam tragedy, one year on: Tears, tributes — and a vow against terror
summary: Twenty-five tourists and a local pony operator were killed in the attack at Baisaran meadow in Pahalgam last year . Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha said the attackers cannot be forgotten or forgiven . Tributes have been made across Kashmir on thefirst anniversary of the killing of 25 touristsand a local in a terror attack .

title: ‘Explain stance within 24 hours or face appropriate action’: EC issues notice to Kharge over ‘terrorist’ remark on PM
summary: Election Commission issued a show-cause notice to Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge for calling Prime Minister Narendra Modi a “terrorist” The Commission said the remarks amounted to prima facie violation of the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) in place for the ongoing Assembly elections . The EC asked him to explain his stance within 24 hours, failing which it would take “appropriate action” on its own .

title: Sluggish wheat procurement in Madhya Pradesh drags down nation’s overall sourcing quantity by 16%
summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has flagged the challenges being faced by farmers during procurement in his state of Madhya Pradesh . The procurement of 114.29 lakh metric tonnes of wheat has been reported across 10 major states as of April 20 . The pace of procurement in Uttar Pradesh, which is the biggest producer of wheat in the country, is reported to be lower in the state .

title: Yusuf Pathan’s relatives arrested for assault, family cries conspiracy ahead of elections
summary: Three relatives of former India cricketer Yusuf Pathan were arrested for allegedly assaulting a 31-year-old man . Pathan’s mother-in-law, Naseebjaan Mohd Khaliq, called it to be an attempt to “malign his reputation” ahead of the Assembly elections in Bengal . The fight broke out when a local, Yusuf Khan, got into an argument with them .

title: 19-year-old left home with 3 friends 2 years ago. Now, police find his remains from a drain — and unravel a murder plot
summary: Chandan Kumar, 19, was last seen with three youths on February 28, 2024 . Chandan was allegedly strangled to death and dumped in a drain in Bihar's Gopalganj district . The case was re-opened after the victim's family raised concerns over the lack of progress .

title: Days after content creator’s almond taunt video goes viral, Chhattisgarh official attached to head office
summary: Toran Sahu's application for a property document had been pending for a year . He was seen throwing almonds on the table of assistant estate manager Poonam Banjare . Sahu said he had been frustrated by officials’ alleged inability to find his file each time he visited their office .

title: ‘India won’t bow to any form of terror’: PM Modi on Pahalgam attack anniversary
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on the anniversary of the Pahalgam terror attack, in which 26 people were gunned down . “As a nation, we stand united in grief and resolve. The heinous designs of terrorists will never succeed,” he said . Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said India will respond with “strength and clarity” if any attempt is made to harm its people .

title: Controversy over opening rooms storing polling material in Kerala, CEO steps in
summary: On Monday, one of the rooms at a building in Kozhikode, where materials used for the Assembly elections were kept under guard, was opened . A similar move in Palakkad district also turned controversial, prompting the Chief Electoral Officer to issue directions to all the District Election Officers and Returning Officers .

title: Big dip in visitors, 30% occupancy in hotels: Kashmir tourism ‘limping back’ but long way still to go, say stakeholders
summary: A year on from the terror attack that killed 25 tourists and a local man, it continues to cast a shadow on Kashmir's tourism industry . Tourism, a critical driver of Kashmir’s economy, had come to a near standstill in the aftermath of the April 22, 2025, attack . Tourism industry is slowly getting back on its feet, but tourist numbers are still far from the boom witnessed in Kashmir between 2022 and 2025 .

title: ‘HM (Shah) said open tourist spots with maximum footfall, not ones easiest to open’: Omar Abdullah on first anniversary of Pahalgam terror attack
summary: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah on eve of first anniversary of terror attack in Pahalgam . He says he worked with the Centre to ensure security, and also simultaneously open all tourist destinations . Abdullah: "So long as places are closed, you’ll never be able to totally do away with the perception that J&K is an unsafe place"

title: Year after attack that changed their town, Pahalgam residents struggle with its emotional and economic toll
summary: Pahalgam used to be a popular tourist destination in south Kashmir . The meadow where 25 tourists and a local man were gunned down by Pakistan-based terrorists last year . The entire tourism-allied industry in the region has suffered since then . Of the 2,000 hotel rooms in and around the town, only about 10% are occupied .

title: Kharge calls PM terrorist, clarifies; BJP slams remark, to meet EC today
summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge called Prime Minister Narendra Modi a ‘terrorist’ at a press conference in Chennai . The remark drew condemnation and sharp reactions from the BJP and senior ministers in the Union government . A BJP delegation will be meeting the Election Commission Wednesday morning .

title: West Bengal polls: Campaign for first phase ends with taunts and name-calling
summary: Mamata Banerjee accuses TMC supremo of shielding ‘infiltrators’ in Chandipur . TMC general secretary Abhishek accuses the Union Home Minister of “reducing a respected leader to a taunt”

title: Rs 22.9 cr lost in digital arrest, five banks made to pay Rs 1.3 cr to victim
summary: Naresh Malhotra lost Rs 22.92 crore to digital arrest frauds between August and September 2025 . The Reserve Bank of India has asked five “beneficiary” banks to pay the victim between 5% and 7.5% of the deposited sums . The banks are Axis Bank, City Union Bank, ICICI bank,IndusIndBank and Yes Bank .

title: V-P Radhakrishnan: Women on the rise in civil services, hope Parliament follows suit
summary: Vice-President C P Radhakrishnan hailed the increased representation of women in the civil services . He was speaking at the 18th National Civil Services Day celebrations in Delhi . The share of women has risen from around 21% in 2016 to nearly 31% in the 2025 exam .

title: EC to ‘examine’ Opposition’s poll code violation complaint against PM
summary: Election Commission of India will “examine’s complaint against Prime Minister Narendra Modi . Opposition parties, including leaders of the Left, wrote to Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar . The poll code came into force on March 15 and will remain in place till May 4, the day votes will be counted .

title: Massive blasts, then a gruesome search: Days ahead of Thrissur Pooram, fireworks tragedy kills 14 in Kerala
summary: Two massive explosions tore through a set of temporary sheds at the edge of a large field in central Thrissur, Kerala . Workers inside the sheds had been assembling sample fireworks for the annual festival . By nightfall on Tuesday, officials said 14 people were confirmed dead and several others were injured .

title: Protesters try to storm event attended by CM in Manipur, man injured in police action
summary: Protesters attempted to storm a government event attended by Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh . One person was injured when security forces applied force to disperse them . These protests are playing out across Manipur’s central valley after two Meitei children were killed in a projectile attack on their home on April 7 .

title: In new video, MP BJP MLA threatens to fill police officer’s bungalow ‘with cow dung’
summary: Pritam Singh Lodhi was heard in a video confronting a senior MP Police officer . The confrontation began after the officer took action against the legislator’s son Dinesh in a road accident case in Shivpuri . In the latest video, Lodhi can be heard threatening to mobilise thousands of people and target the officer’�s residence if his demands were not met .

title: Pappu Yadav’s remarks on women in politics ‘damaging to their dignity’: Bihar commission demands explanation
summary: Bihar State Women’s Commission issues a notice to Purnea MP Rajesh Ranjan, popularly known as Pappu Yadav . Yadav allegedly remarked that women in politics “come only after sharing beds with some leaders” Commission asks why such a statement should not be recommended against him .

title: 9 killed in blast at unit making fireworks for Thrissur Pooram in Kerala
summary: The blast occurred at the unit, which was making fireworks for the Thrissur Pooram festival slated for April 24 . The fire force has deployed drones to contain the fire as rescue workers could not get close to the spot due to thick smoke and recurring blasts .

title: Did a ‘missing’ 18-yr-old lay undiscovered for 10 months,10 km from home? Jharkhand family wants answers
summary: The 18-year-old went to a local college for admission to a B.A. course, taking her friend's bicycle . She was last seen on July 21, 2025, days after she had cleared Class 12 . The family approached the local police station, which allegedly refused to lodge a missing person’s report . The girl's family moved the Jharkhand High Court with a habeas corpus petition . Following a rap by the court, skeletal remains suspected to be of the teenager were found, just 10 km from her home .

title: Wildlife board panel orders study on pastoralists’ dependence on forests inside national parks and sanctuaries
summary: India has an estimated 13 million pastoralists across 46 groups, as per research and advocacy groups . Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Rajasthan, Maharashtra, Uttarakhand, and Uttar Pradesh will be the focus of the study . Pastoralists rear livestock and depend on grasslands, scrublands, shrubs, and plateaus for their livelihoods .

title: ‘Abuse of power’: Congress moves privilege notice against PM over April 18 address
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modifor allegedly “cast aspersions’on the ‘independence and integrity” of elected MPs in his address to the nation on April 18 . Congress MP K C Venugopal said the PM used the word ‘bhroon hatya’ (foeticide) to attack the opposition for blocking the passage of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill-2026 .

title: On last day of Tamil Nadu polls campaign, Kejriwal slams BJP, says state rejects divisive politics
summary: Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) National Convenor Arvind Kejriwal launched a scathing attack on the BJP on the last day of campaigning for the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections . He said the people of Tamil Nadu have consistently rejected the BJP’s politics, attributing it to what he described as its “divisive and vicious” approach .

title: Behind month of labour shortage in Kerala: Elections in Bengal and Assam, SIR ‘fears’
summary: Kerala has seen a large number of migrant workers leaving to cast their votes . This has had a major effect on several industries, including construction, agriculture and plywood, which heavily rely on these workers . West Bengal accounts for the highest percentage of these labourers, with 35.4% estimated to be from the state .

title: Year after Pahalgam, a daughter remembers her ‘braveheart’ father, his lesson on staying calm
summary: N Ramachandran, 65, was killed in the Pahalgam terror attack on April 22 last year . He was shot dead in the presence of his daughter and grandsons in Kashmir . Daughter Arathy says her father was a model for resilience in life and a 'brave martyr'

title: We are not Vishwa Guru today: Murli Manohar Joshi
summary: Murli Manohar Joshi was speaking at the inauguration of the Central Office of Samskrita Bharati, an RSS-linked organisation that promotes Sanskrit . Terming it the “pran (lifeblood) of India”, Joshi, one of the most prominent BJP stalwarts, also threw his weight behind the promotion of Sanskrit .

title: Assembly Election 2026 Live Updates: ECI releases Bengal phase 1 voter list, clears who all can vote tomorrow
summary: ECI releases Bengal phase 1 voter list, clears who all can vote tomorrow . Bengal Assembly Election 2026 Live Updates: ECI declares Bengal phase-1 voter list . Bengal election 2026 will be held in Bengal's capital, capital and capital capital, New Delhi .

title: Heatwave in Delhi: Hydrate, cover up if stepping out in the day; IMD warns of mercury hitting 42°C today
summary: On Tuesday, the IMD warned, the temperature could go up to 42°C . The weather department has issued its first heatwave alert of the year . In India, heatwaves alerts occur mainly from March to June and in some rare cases even in July .

title: Manipur Earthquake: Two earthquakes detected near India-Myanmar border
summary: Two earthquakes struck the Northeast state and Sagaing (Myanmar), near the India-Myanmar border . A 4.6-magnitude hit was detected in Thickegyin (in the Sagaing region of Myanmar) at 05:38 am . Another one with its epicentre in Kamjong, Manipur, was recorded at 5:59 am. The latter measured 5.2 . A magnitude 5.4 earthquake was also felt in Colombia and Ecuador at 7:52 am .

title: A deadly doorstep delivery, a rare poisoning, and a bid to eliminate a family: How a UK-based man plotted his ‘revenge’ in Hyderabad
summary: Bala Sashirekha, in her early 30s, has been unwell for a while now . She has severe diarrhoea and vomiting, and oedema has left her limbs swollen and dysfunctional . Tests reveal she has arsenic in her body, about 10 times the normal limit . Her 62-year-old mother-in-law, M Uma Maheshwari, died on July 5, 2023 – a little over a month after her son’s marriage was fixed .

title: ‘Hard reset’: A year since Pahalgam, J&K’s security landscape looks vastly different
summary: The security apparatus in Kashmir and Jammu has witnessed what many call a “hard reset” The strategy, which has changed from defence to preemption and offence, has meant that while many gunfights continue to take place in the dense forests, Pahalgam was the last big militant attack in Jammuand Kashmir . J&K Police established two new elite units within its Special Operations Group to strengthen operations in challenging terrain .

title: Chhattisgarh saw 600% jump in cannabis seizures this year. Crackdown on key transit hub could be why
summary: Around 5,630 kg of cannabis, collectively worth Rs 28.09 crore, seized in Chhattisgarh's Mahasamund district in less than four months . The drugs would have impacted the lives of six lakh people, a police official said . A major crackdown was undertaken this year with the aim of stopping the smuggling of drugs through this district, police said .

title: Former diplomat and author Phunchok Stobdan dies at 67
summary: Phunchok Stobdan, former Ambassador of India to Kyrgyzstan, passed away on Sunday . The 67-year-old was a senior fellow for Eurasian Security at Delhi Policy Group . He served as a Joint Director in the National Security Council Secretariat and was Senior Fellow at the Manohar Parrikar Institute for Defence Studies and Analyses .

title: Omar questions Centre’s ‘right time’ for J&K statehood, flags delay after 3-step promise
summary: Chief Minister Omar Abdullah hits out at BJP-led Centre over unfulfilled promise of statehood to Jammu and Kashmir . He asked if its promises were “so light that you repeat them everywhere – in the Supreme Court, in Parliament, in election rallies – and then step back” “There is still no clear answer,” he said .

title: CBI arrests two RCOM senior executives in bank fraud case
summary: CBI has arrested two senior executives of Reliance Communications (RCOM) in connection with an alleged bank fraud case . The arrested have been identified as Joint President D Vishwanath and Vice President Anil Kalya . The agency had registered a case following a complaint by SBI, accusing RCOM, its promoter Anil Ambani, and others of fraudulent diversion of loan funds .

title: Nitish Kumar signals continued control as JD(U) recalibrates after power shift to BJP
summary: This was the first meeting of JD(U) legislators since Nitish Kumardemitted office as chief minister on April 14 . Nitish Kumar’s son, Nishant Kumar, who is not a legislator, did not attend the meeting . He may seek nomination to the Bihar Legislative Council against upcoming one-third vacancies .

title: 2020 Delhi riots: SC dismisses Umar Khalid’s plea seeking review of order denying him bail
summary: Delhi riots case accused Umar Khalid’s plea seeking a review of its order that denied him bail was dismissed . “We do not find any good ground and reason to review the judgment dated January 5, 2026. Accordingly, the review petition is dismissed,” a bench of Justice Aravind Kumar and Justice N V Anjaria said in its April 16 order . The order had cited “hierarchy of participation” to deny bail to Khalid and Imam .

title: Railways revises timings of Mumbai-Hazur Sahib Nanded Vande Bharat train: Check new schedule
summary: Train number 20706/20705 CSMT-Hazur Sahib Nanded-CSMT Vande Bharat Express train covers a distance of 610 km in 09:40 hrs . The semi-high-speed train was introduced on December 30, 2023 . The revised timings will come into effect from May 3, 2026 .

title: Son ‘rammed Thar into 5 people’, MLA father now warns police: ‘He’ll contest polls, try and stop him’
summary: BJP MLA Pritam Singh Lodhi is courting controversy after a video surfaced purportedly showing him threatening a police officer . His son, Dinesh Lodhi, is accused of injuring five people while driving a Thar last week . The incident occurred in Shivpuri’s Karera area, where he allegedly drove a Mahindra Thar into five people .

title: Over 300 Indians evacuated from Iran via Azerbaijan
summary: The Indian embassy in Azerbaijan has helped in the safe evacuation of over 300 Indian nationals by road from Iran since March 6, 2026 till date . Indian ambassador to Azerbaijan, Abhay Kumar, met the last batch of around 15 Indian students evacuated from Iran via Azerbaijan over the weekend, before they left on Saturday . According to officials, Iranian airspace remains partially open for cargo and chartered flights .

title: Summer Special Trains 2026: Indian Railways to run over 18,200 trips – check zone-wise data
summary: A total of 908 summer special trains have been approved for operation between April 15 and July 15, 2026 . South Central Railway (SCR) will operate the highest number, with 124 trains accounting for 1,184 trips . The East Central Railway will operate 64 trains (2,711 trips), followed by North Central Railway with 54 trains .

title: Chips to ships: PM Modi and President Lee Unveil vision to double trade, launch ‘digital bridge’ between India and South Korea
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met the visiting President of South Korea, Lee Jae Myung . They also agreed on a joint strategic vision for the India-South Korea special strategic partnership . The two sides also signed agreements on digital cooperation in AI, small and medium enterprises, sports, and culture .

title: CBI arrests DGCA official and Reliance executive in bribery case
summary: CBI arrested Mudavath Devula, Deputy Director General at DGCA’s Airworthiness Directorate headquarters in Delhi, and Bharat Mathur, senior vice president of Reliance Industries Ltd . Probe has revealed a trail of graft dating back to March .

title: Plane crash that wasn’t: Why Chhattisgarh officials spent hours searching the hills
summary: The Chhattisgarh administration rushed a team to the hills of Narayanpur after reports that a plane had crashed there . The Jashpur police, forest officials and the district administration undertook a search operation for a couple of hours but did not find any evidence that could confirm the incident .

title: ‘Wrongful deletion’ of 9.8 lakh names during pre-SIR exercise in Odisha, CEO orders strict verification
summary: Chief electoral officer has ordered strict verification before preparing final roll . Around 9.8 lakh names were deleted during electoral mapping before the Odishaleg of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) The CEO office has received around two lakh Form-7 applications (objection form for proposed inclusion/deletion of name in existing roll) after April 2 .

title: Fire at Balotra’s Pachpadra refinery; inauguration by PM postponed
