summary: A fire broke out at the HPCL Pachpadra refinery in Balotra on Monday afternoon . A day before it was set to be inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi . Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas said Tuesday’s programme has now been postponed and a new date will be announced in due course .

title: Rajasthan’s Pachpadra Refinery inauguration postponed after fire. Why this Refinery-cum-Petrochemical complex is important
summary: The dedication of the Pachpadra Refinery in Rajasthan’s Balotra has been postponed following a fire incident . The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas announced that the dedication was postponed . The fire has been brought under control and there are no reports .

title: Manipur on edge once more, police suggest instigators playing a role
summary: Large-scale protests continue to take place every night across the Manipur valley areas . Two weeks after two Meitei children were killed in a projectile attack on their home in Bishnupur . Authorities in the state have begun taking action against “instigators” in the past few days .

title: Indian Railways allows UDID card holders to travel in unreserved PwD coaches
summary: The Ministry of Railways has allowed Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) holding a valid UDID (Unique Disability ID) card to travel in unreserved coaches designated for PwDs . The national transporter has also undertaken several initiatives to enhance the welfare of Pwds or Divyangjans .

title: Space traffic rises: Isro performed record 140 collision-avoidance manoeuvres in 2025
summary: Indian Space Research Organisation performed 140 collision-avoidance manoeuvres (CAMs) in 2025 to protect Indian space assets . One CAM was specifically conducted for the Isro-NASA joint mission, NISAR . Four CAMs performed for geosynchronous earth orbit (GEO) missions and 14 for low earth orbit missions .

title: 21 killed in Udhampur bus tragedy; probe ordered, relief efforts continue
summary: An ex gratia of Rs 2 lakh from PMNRF would be given to the next of kin of those killed and Rs 50,000 for the injured . Most victims were locals, as the accident occurred during morning hours when people were heading to offices or other work in the district headquarters in Udhampur town .

title: Ukraine, India finalising enhanced security cooperation: Zelenskyy
summary: Ukraine’s Secretary of the National Security and Defense Council Rustem Umerov visited India and met NSA Ajit Doval . This assumes significance at a time when India and Russia have robust security and defence cooperation . India has sought to play a diplomatic tightrope walk in past four years of the war between Russia and Ukraine .

title: YouTuber’s remarks on Goa’s patron saint: FIR filed, Sanatan Dharm Raksha Samiti apologises
summary: Goa Police booked YouTuber Gautam Khattar on Sunday night for allegedly making derogatory remarks against St Francis Xavier . The Sanatan Dharm Raksha Samiti Mormugao event was attended by state Transport Minister Mauvin Godinho and BJP MLAs Sankalp Amonkar and Krishna Salkar . Organiser of the event distanced itself from the accused’s remarks and issued an apology to the Christian community .

title: Top News Headlines of April 20, 2026 | Trump’s ultimatum: ‘Lots of bombs’ to fall on Iran if Wednesday deadline missed
summary: Trump’s ultimatum: ‘Lots of bombs’ to fall on Iran if Wednesday deadline missed . Trump: 'Lots of bomb bombs' to be used against Iran if Iran fails to meet the deadline . ‘Bombs’ will be dropped on Iran, the U.S. president says .

title: ‘People should know why he died’: Family of IAF man from Arunachal prepares memorial ahead of Pahalgam anniversary
summary: IAF Corporal Tage Hailyanghas was killed in the Pahalgam terror attack on April 22, 2025 . His family has been putting the finishing touches on a memorial to him that they plan to inaugurate on that date . The centrepiece of the memorial, which is located near their village of Tajang in Arunachal Pradesh’s Lower Subansiri district, is a bronze statue .

title: Thousands of RTI appeals stuck in Bihar, High Court seeks update
summary: Patna High Court has sought a status report from the state government on filling vacant positions in the commission . Under the RTI Act, 2005, first appeals should be disposed of within 45 days and second appeals within 90 days . A similar matter is also being heard by the Supreme Court .

title: Expired blood, fake records: Probe reveals how 5 children with thalassemia got HIV
summary: Madhya Pradesh health department has found several lapses as part of its investigation into how five children with thalassemia, who received blood transfusions at Satna’s Vallabhbhai Patel District Hospital, tested HIV-positive . Report states that “proper records of blood donors were not maintained, company details of testing kits were missing, and blood was not properly tested for HIV/other tests before transfusion”

title: ‘Hardest year of our lives’: Year after Pahalgam, Pune victim’s family navigates trauma, financial strain
summary: Santosh Jagdale, 50, was killed in Kashmir terror attack on April 22 last year . His wife and daughter, Asawari, are yet to recover from the traumatic incident . Santosh was among the26 people killed in Pahalgam, in Kashmir’s Anantnag district, when terrorists opened fire .

title: Draped in legacy, weaved in neighbourhood: A sold-out Bangladesh saree exhibition
summary: Bangladesh High Commission in New Delhi opened its first-ever exhibition of 300 handloom sarees . The four-day showcase opened on April 18 . Within 24 hours, the exposition had been closed . The art of Tangail saree weaving received international recognition as an Intangible Cultural Heritage, by UNESCO .

title: Armed Forces eye biogas, solar to cut fuel use amid West Asia crisis
summary: India’s Armed Forces are exploring various measures to conserve LPG and fuel amid the ongoing global energy crisis triggered by the Iran war . Officials indicated that approximately 156,000 kg of cooking gas is consumed daily in the Army, and about 20 per cent of that can be saved through biogas .

title: India extends OCI card eligibility in Sri Lanka to sixth generation
summary: Vice-President C P Radhakrishnan announced extension of the eligibility for Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) card for the Indian community in the island nation till the sixth generation . Until now, this was limited to the fourth generation of the diaspora .

title: South Korean President reaches Delhi, key meeting with Modi today
summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung will meet Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Monday and discuss shipbuilding, trade, investments, AI, semiconductors, critical and emerging technologies . Lee will also meet President Droupadi Murmu, who will host a banquet in his honour . Lee’s visit, from April 19 to 21, marks the first state visit to India by a South Korean president in eight years .

title: 2 US citizens carrying satellite communicator detained at Srinagar airport
summary: Two US citizens were detained in Srinagar after the airport security recovered a satellite communicator and GPS device from their luggage . The use of satellite phones, especially Thuraya and Iridium phones, is prohibited in India without permission from the government . Illegal possession of these devices can lead to a fine, arrest and seizure under the Indian Telegraph Act .

title: Former Union minister Dinesh Trivedi set to be India’s next High Commissioner to Bangladesh
summary: Dinesh Trivedi is likely to be appointed India’s next High Commissioner to Bangladesh . A political appointment to the key diplomatic post in Dhaka would mark a deviation from the recent past . This comes amid efforts to reset bilateral ties after the new Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) government was formed in February following elections .

title: Railways plans four-line corridor along coastal Odisha to decongest Howrah-Chennai route
summary: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw announced that all 30 districts of the state are being brought under rail connectivity through planned interventions . He said this will ensure inclusive development and better integration of remote and interior regions with the mainstream rail network . The Howrah-Chennai route is one of the most popular rail routes in the country .

title: Ahead of Bengal phase-1 polls, CAPF chiefs hold joint meet
summary: With the first phase of West Bengal elections on April 23, Directors General of CAPF gathered in Kolkata earlier this week for a high-level joint meeting to fine-tune security plans . The primary objective of all these forces is the conduct of a free, fair, and transparent election .

title: Census portal shows Arunachal town as Chinese territory, glitch resolved
summary: Local resident of Pasighat, Arunachal Pradesh, tried to register on the Census official website, se.census.gov.in . The site’s map interface erroneously labeled his town as ‘Medog’, a location across the border in China . The issue was raised with the map services provider, and it has been resolved, authorities said .

title: Ahead of Pahalgam anniversary, J-K border district puts plans for mock drills on hold
summary: Jammu and Kashmir administration put on hold plans for mock drills in border district of Poonch . Officials say the decision was taken to allay apprehensions among the general public . The mock drills would have coincided with the anniversary of the Pahalgam terror attack .

title: 23 killed, 8 injured in explosion at Tamil Nadu fireworks manufacturing unit
summary: The blast occurred in the Kattanarpatti area of Virudhunagar, a district that is home to a substantial firecracker manufacturing industry . The cause of the blast was not immediately clear; investigators were expected to examine whether safety lapses or the handling of combustible chemicals played a role .

title: Assembly polls: Election Commission takes action on 11,000 social media posts
summary: The Election Commission has taken action on over 11,000 social media posts, including ordering the removal of the content, issuing clarifications and filing of FIRs . The majority of these actions pertain to posts related to theTamil Naduand West Bengal elections . Among some of the posts taken down recently were a 2022 video that was circulated again, alleging that Trinamool Congress workers were threatening voters not to vote for the BJP .

title: Two months after labour protest at Barauni refinery, 11 men await clarity on overtime dues
summary: On February 2, workers pressed for overtime wages, an eight-hour workday, and basic facilities . Vimlesh Kumar, acontractual worker at an Indian Oil Corporation Limited refinery in Barauni in Bihar’s Begusarai, is now involved in a legal fight .

title: Blood bank ex-staffer arrested 6 months after children get HIV ‘following blood transfusion’
summary: Incident dates back to October 2025, when five children undergoing regular treatment for thalassemia at the Chaibasa Sadar Hospital were allegedly administered infected blood from the hospital’s blood bank . The alleged lapse came to light after a minor tested positive for HIV . The families of the affected children approached the Jharkhand High Court . The petitioners argued that due to alleged acts and omissions by those in charge of the blood bank, the lives of the minors were put at risk .

title: Rajasthan’s Bhagat Ki Kothi to get India’s first Vande Bharat Sleeper train maintenance depot
summary: Maintenance facilities for the Vande Bharat Sleeper train and other trains will be commissioned at the depot . The depot will include a 600-metre track to support maintenance of 24-coach trains . It is expected to be ready by June 2026 .

title: Army soldier found dead at family quarters near Jammu
summary: An Army soldier was found dead in his room inside the family quarters at an Army camp in Nagrota near Jammu . Preliminary investigations suggest suicide, the police said . The Army has initiated a departmental inquiry into the circumstances leading to the soldier’s death .

title: Air India unveils first retrofitted Boeing 787 aircraft: Check photos, key features
summary: The first upgrade of the twin-aisle Boeing 787-8 aircraft took nearly 12,825 man-hours over 45 days to complete . Air India will subsequently retrofit 13 of its legacy B777-300ER aircraft . The airline will retrofit the remaining 25 B787 aircraft over the next year .

title: Assembly Elections 2026: Rahul Gandhi calls Nitish ‘compromised’, says BJP wants similar game in Tamil Nadu through AIADMK
summary: Rahul Gandhi calls Nitish Nitish ‘compromised’, says BJP wants similar game in Tamil Nadu through AIADMK . Rahul Gandhi: BJP wants to replicate its success in the state of Tamil Nadu . He says it is not a repeat of what happened in the Tamil Nadu Assembly Election 2026 .

title: 3 days before election, Chandrababu Naidu set for whirlwind campaign across Tamil Nadu
summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu will undertake a two-day campaign tour across Tamil Nadu on April 20 and 21 . The visit is aimed at energising cadre, strengthening voter connect, and reinforcing the NDA’s development-driven narrative . The Assembly election inTamil Naduwill be held on April 23 .

title: ‘Modiji mentioned Congress 59 times’: Kharge calls PM ‘desperate’ after women quota Bill fails in Lok Sabha
summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge hits back at PM Narendra Modi, calling him 'desperate' and 'frustrated' Kharge accused PM Modi of misusing the Model Code of Conduct, and turning a national address into a “political speech’, filled with “mudslinging, and outright LIES” Modi accused Opposition parties of “committing a sin” by opposing the passage of the Women’s Reservation Bill in the Lower House .

title: ‘Remarkably courageous’: PM Modi speaks to Pawan Kalyan after Andhra deputy CM undergoes surgery
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with Andhra Pradesh Deputy CM Pawan Kalyan on Sunday . The actor-turned-politician was admitted to the hospital on Friday after falling unwell while holding a review meeting with officials . Doctors have advised him to rest for one week to 10 days .

title: 58 Jamaat-e-Islami-linked private schools taken over by J&K govt
summary: Jammu & Kashmir government takes control of 58 private schools affiliated with the Falah-e-Aam Trust . The move comes eight months after the decision to take over these schools pitted J&K's elected government against the Lieutenant Governor’s administration . The Trust ran around 350 middle and high schools across the erstwhile state .

title: 48 days after death, Indian sailor Dixit Solanki, killed in Oman missile strike, finally cremated in Mumbai
summary: Dixit Solanki, a resident of Kandivali, Mumbai, was killed in a missile strike off the coast of Oman . He died on March 1, 2025, on a merchant vessel in West Asia, making him the first Indian casualty of the ongoing conflict in the region . His remains reachedMumbaionly on April 5, more than a month after his death .

title: To ‘weed out non-believers’, Gangotri temple will make panchgavya consumption a must
summary: Char Dham Yatra begins on Sunday, with the portals of Gangotri and Yamunotri temples set to open . The portals of the temples are set to restrict entry to select devotees this time . The temple committee chairperson Dharmendra Semwal said the step was aimed at “weeding out non-believers” and ensuring only those with “unshaken spirituality” visit .

title: Budget Session: Lok Sabha clocks 93% productivity, Rajya Sabha 110%
summary: No business was taken up on the last day of the three-day special sitting to consider the women’s quota legislation, and both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha were adjourned sine die Saturday morning . The session saw the passage of theUnion Budgetfor 2026-27 and the demands for grants and supplementary grants for various ministries . The Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026, was not taken up by the government during the session .

title: Ex-journalist, BJP veteran Balbir Punj  dies at 76
summary: Balbir Punj, a former journalist with The Indian Express Group, passed away on Saturday at the age of 76 . Punj was also a social commentator and author of ‘Tryst with Ayodhya: Decolonisation of India’ and ‘Narrative ka Mayajaal’ Prime Minister Narendra Modi in a post on ‘X’ said, “Fondly remember our interactions from those times”

title: Strait of Hormuz closure economic terrorism… is impacting India too: UAE minister
summary: Saeed Bin Mubarak Al Hajeri, Minister of State in the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in the UAE, says a ceasefire between US and Iran is not enough . Any sustainable resolution to the war must address Iran’s full range of threats, he says . He says the UAE made sincere and sustained efforts to avoid escalation, and those efforts were met with premeditated aggression .

title: After defeat, PM Modi cites ‘foeticide’; Opposition says ‘pathetic, partisan’
summary: The Constitution (One Hundred and Thirty-First Amendment) Bill, 2026, was defeated in Lok Sabha on Friday . The Bill was aimed at raising the ceiling of Lok Sabha members to 850 and operationalising 33% reservation for women in state Assemblies . Prime Minister Narendra Modi said there will be more opportunities going ahead .

title: ‘You don’t move on, you carry it’: Year after Pahalgam, a father shares his grief
summary: Lt Vinay Narwal was killed in the Pahalgam terror attack on April 22, 2025 . His father, Rajesh Narwal, said: "Afather is not meant to carry his son's loss like this... but I carry it every day, like a weight that never lifts"

title: ‘Distress address… full of mudslinging, lies’: Opposition slams Modi, alleges violation of sanctity, poll code
summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge said the PM mentioned the Congress 59 times and women “barely a few times’. “That tells the country everything about his priorities. Women are not the BJP’s priority. Congress is, because Congress stands on the right side of history,” he said .

title: PM Modi’s pointed attack on Opposition after Women’s Bill defeat: 6 points
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi used the word ‘Bhroon Hatya’ to attack the opposition for blocking the passage of the Bill, and two other Bills related to it that lapsed with the core Bill being defeated in a division of votes in the House . This happened a day after the Lok Sabha rejected an amended constitutional amendment bill to advance women’s reservation to 2029 . While attacking the Congress as an anti-reform party, the PM promised that he would work to remove every hurdle in the path of women's reservation .

title: ‘Investigate first’: Naveen Jindal defends Anil Agarwal as Vedanta blast toll hits 23
summary: Industrialist and MP Naveen Jindal has backed Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal, who has been named in an FIR . Jindal said the tragedy was ‘deeply painful’ and called for compensation, livelihood support and a thorough probe . Two more workers have succumbed to injuries, three remain critical and nine are under observation .

title: Government nod for Rs 12,980-crore maritime insurance pool amid global shipping upheaval
summary: The Centre announced the creation of a domestic insurance pool, Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool, with a sovereign guarantee of Rs 12,980 crore . Vessels with the India flag will be covered under this . It will cover all maritime risks including hull, machinery, cargo, P&I and war .

title: Day after Manipur CM’s visit, ex-armyman among two killed in suspected militant attack in Ukhrul
summary: Suspected militants attacked civilian vehicles along the Imphal–Ukhrul road on Saturday, leading to at least two casualties . Incident happened a day after Manipur Chief Minister Y Khemchand Singh visited Ukhrul district . Relations between the Tangkhul Naga and Kuki-Zo communities have been strained since violence erupted in February .

title: PM Modi apologises for women’s quota bill defeat, accuses Opposition of ‘female foeticide’
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi said the Opposition carried out ‘bhroon hatya’ (female foeticide) by defeating the women’s reservation amendment Bill . Addressing the nation hours after the special Parliament session ended, the PM “sought forgiveness” from the women of the country after the Bill failed to clear the floor test in the Lok Sabha on Friday .

title: Vice-President to begin two-day Sri Lanka visit today
summary: During the two-day visit, Radhakrishnan will call on Sri Lanka’s President Anura Kumara Disanayaka, and also meet Prime Minister Harini Amarasuriya . He will also interact with leaders of the Indian-origin Tamil community and Tamil leaders from the Northern and Eastern regions of Sri Lanka . On Monday, the vice president will travel to Nuwara Eliya, visit the Indian Housing Projects and interact with the local Tamil community .

title: First woman advocate from Kashmir among 11 named for J&K HC elevation
summary: The Collegium of the Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh High Court has recommended 11 names to fill existing vacancies . Tabasum Zafar, an advocate from Kashmir, is among those recommended . The selection followed an extensive consultative exercise led by Chief Justice Arun Palli . The high court has a sanctioned strength of 25 judges .

title: ‘Modi controlled by Trump’: Rahul Gandhi says BJP wants to install puppet CM in Tamil Nadu
summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi accuses the Bharatiya Janata Party of trying to install a 'puppet' chief minister in Tamil Nadu . Accuses the party of using the AIADMK as a front to gain control in the state . Gandhi alleges that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is “compromised” and influenced by US President Donald Trump .

title: In Manipur, the killing of two siblings threatens to turn back the clock
summary: Two children were killed by a projectile that landed inside their home in Bishnupur . The killing of the siblings – a boy aged five years and a five-month-old girl – sparked outrage in the state’s Meitei-majority valley . The state Home Department on Saturday lifted the ban on mobile internet services, which had been in place since April 7 .

title: To decongest, Cabinet clears Rs 24K-cr multi-tracking projects in UP, Andhra
summary: Ghaziabad-Sitapur (near Lucknow) line in Uttar Pradesh and Rajahmundry-Visakhapatnam line in Andhra Pradesh . The two projects will increase the existing railway network by about 601 km . The high-density network accounts for 11,051 km or 15.97% of the total 69,181 km railway route network .

title: Goa hotels and shacks are gaming registration process to dodge fees, minister warns
summary: Tourism Minister Rohan Khauntesaid that several hotels and private beach shacks in the state are downrating themselves during the registration process with the tourism department, to avoid paying higher government fees and taxes . This comes in the aftermath of a fire that broke out on Thursday night at a beach resort near Ajoba temple in Ashvem .

title: ‘Lives paused again’: Kota residents await land titles as Supreme Court stays Chambal denotification
summary: The National Chambal Gharial Sanctuary, notified in 1983, spans Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh with a 1 km protected stretch on both banks of the river . The sanctuary was originally notified under the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972, earlier covered the sanctuary covered the area from Jawahar Damar Damar to Kota Barrage . The order, on April 2, stayed the Rajaasthan government’s December 2026 circular de-notifying 732 hectares of sanctuary land, citing potential threats to endangered species .

title: Odisha’s Planning Board hadn’t met since 2007. Its replacement is finally here
summary: The State Institute for Transformative Initiatives (SITI) will replace the now-dissolved State Planning Board . The Planning Board, considered the highest planning body in the state, was dissolved in July 2024 . SITI-Odisha will guide the state government on six topics: economy and finance; rural economy and livelihoods; employment and urban development; ideation and innovation .

title: RSS’ Hosabale at Stanford: Invaders destroyed India’s scientific past, Modi govt reviving it
summary: Dattatreya Hosabale argued that the Patanjali Yogasutra exemplified the “great scientific tradition” of ancient India . The RSS leader placed ancient Indian knowledge systems alongside modern scientific inquiry, arguing that traditions embedded in texts such as the Upanishads reflected a long-standing culture of “cosmic enquiry”

title: 11 MPs absent, 7 from TMC, 2 from Congress: What final tally on women’s Bill voting shows
summary: Two MPs from the Congress and seven from the Trinamool Congress were among the 11 MPs who were absent during voting on the Constitution (One Hundred and Thirty-First Amendment) Bill, 2026 . The amendment was aimed at increasing representation in the House by fast-tracking implementation of women's reservation . Three of 543 seats in the Lok Sabha are vacant .

title: PM Modi Speech Highlights: ‘Distress, not national address’, Congress reacts to PM’s speech
summary: PM Modi Speech Highlights: ‘Distress, not national address’, Congress reacts to PM’s speech . PM Modi: 'Distress and national address is a national issue, not a national address' Congress also reacts to the PM's speech .

title: Cabinet extends rural roads scheme, increases outlay by Rs 3,727 crore
summary: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana-III (PMGSY-III) extended beyond March 2025 till 2028-29 . Increase in the outlay by Rs 3,727 crore to Rs 83,977 crore . Decision to this effect was taken during the meeting of the Union Cabinet .

title: Khata transfer for cash?: Panchayat officer, YouTuber held in Nelamangala in Rs 1.2 lakh bribery case
summary: A panchayat development officer (PDO) and a YouTuber were arrested for allegedly demanding and accepting a bribe to process a property-related application . The accused have been identified as Geethamani, PDO of Kallalughatta Gram Panchayat, and Gangadhar, who runs a local YouTube channel .

title: What is Kashmir Care Foundation, US-based non-profit that 3 varsities cancelled MoUs with?
summary: U.S.-based non-profit KCF came into spotlight after three universities terminated MoUs with it . Founder Altaf Lal said he had been “as ignorant as anyone else” about the development . The organisation aims to “empower” youth of Kashmir through education and mentorship .

title: CBI arrests former bank manager, 2 others in Rs 1.6 crore digital arrest scam
summary: The CBI conducted searches at five locations across Telangana and Andhra Pradesh, where investigators seized incriminating documents and electronic devices linked to the accused . The victim, a senior citizen, was allegedly coerced into transferring the massive sum under threats of a fake digital arrest . The case underscores the menace of sophisticated cyber scams targeting vulnerable citizens .

title: Who’s counting? In Andhra, chorus grows for Telangana-style caste survey
summary: Backward Classes (BC) in Andhra Pradesh are demanding a similar exercise there . BC leaders recently met Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu and suggested conducting a survey on the same lines . BC activists say the TDP-led NDA government claims to have started the BC census in the state and is nearing completion .

title: PM Modi to address nation at 8.30pm
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to address the nation at 8.30pm on Saturday . This comes a day after a bill to implementwomen’s reservation in legislatures was defeated in the Lok Sabha . A two-thirds majority was required for the passage of the crucial bill but the NDA could not muster the numbers . Under the Bill, seats in Lok Sabha were to be increased from 543 to to 816 .

title: Speaker Om Birla adjourns Lok Sabha, says House recorded 93% productivity during Budget Session
summary: Speaker Om Birla Saturday concluded the Budget Session that began on January 28 . Lok Sabha recorded 93 per cent productivity during the Budget session, he said . Nine of the 12 Government Bills that were passed include the Industrial Relations Code (amendment) Bill, 2026, the Finance Bill and the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill .

title: ‘Congress’s black spot won’t wash off’: Rijiju says party will now face ‘wrath of women’
summary: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju said defeat of women's reservation Bill in Lok Sabha was “black spot” on Congress . He said Congress leaders had approached him and said that the government was well-intentioned in introducing the Bills . “We are hurt women’s rights was crushed by Congress; this is a huge loss for the women of our country,” he added .

title: Priyanka Gandhi slams BJP over women’s Bill defeat: ‘Conspiracy to retain power, used women as decoy’
summary: Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra called the defeat of the Constitution (One Hundred and Thirty-First Amendment) Bill, 2026, a win for democracy . The Bill sought to advance reservation of 33 per cent seats for women in an expanded Lok Sabha and state Assemblies . Vadra said that the Opposition was very clear that it would support the 2023 Women's Reservation Act .

title: ‘They deserved this shock’: Priyanka Gandhi’s ‘Black Day’ dig at BJP after delimitation vote loss
summary: Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra said the BJP government at the Centre had lost the trust of the public . She said the failure to pass the women’s reservation and delimitation Bills reflected the “loss of trust” Vadra also said there was no reason to celebrate the defeat of the government over the passage of the women's quota Bill .

title: ‘Betrayal of Nari Shakti’: BJP to hit streets today for massive nationwide protests as INDIA bloc ‘stalls’ Women’s Reservation Bill
summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has planned nationwide protests alongside an outreach over the ‘Nari Shanti Vandan Adhiniyam’ or Women’s Reservation Bill on Saturday . The initial phase of the campaign, which includes a march by the party's women members, was chalked out on Friday during a huddle almost immediately after the Bill was defeated in the Lok Sabha .

title: A mother in need of treatment, a loan app, and a Kerala student’s death: Behind 3 arrests from Noida
summary: Nithin Raj R L (22), who hailed from Uzhamalakkal village in Thiruvananthapuram,died after a fall from a building at the Kannur Dental College campus on April 10 . His death, allegedly after incidents of humiliation over caste, skin colour and his family’s financial background, had taken Kerala by a storm of grief and protest .

title: Parliament special session ends; PM Modi-led security panel meeting today amid West Asia war
summary: Proceedings of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha were adjourned indefinitely soon after the session began at 11 am on Saturday . The 131st Constitution Amendment Bill was defeated in the Lok Sabha, after out of 528 members who participated, 298 members voted in support of the Bill, and 230 MPs voted against it . Subsequently, the Union government did not move forward with two related bills on delimitation, saying that the three bills could be viewed in isolation .

title: Delhi weather: No heatwave yet, but temperature likely to touch 42°C, says IMD
summary: Temperatures in Delhi-NCR and wider North India region will have to bear the brunt of the rising heat over the coming week . On Friday, Safdarjung recorded a maximum of 41C — around 4 degrees above the seasonal average . No heatwave conditions have been forecast for Delhi over the next week .

title: From Srinagar’s Jama Masjid, some praise for Pope Leo
summary: Mirwaiz Umar Farooq commends Pope Leo for his advocacy for peace and dialogue . Urges religious leaders across all communities to condemn wars and aggression . Pope Leo had earlier stated that “God does not bless any conflict. Anyone who is a disciple of Christ, the Prince of Peace, is never on the side of those who once wielded the sword .

title: 60-day ban on ‘communal content, hate speech’ on social media in Jammu
summary: The order comes into force with immediate effect and extends across all major social media platforms, including WhatsApp, Facebook, X, Instagram, YouTube and Telegram . The Cyber Crime Unit will maintain round-the-clock monitoring of social media, while special monitoring cells will be set up at sub-divisional level under the supervision of respective SDMs .

title: Gone in 20 minutes: Five gunmen, hostages, and a bank heist in Madhya Pradesh
summary: Five armed men stormed a bank branch in Madhya Pradesh’s Singrauli on Friday afternoon . They held staff and customers hostage at gunpoint, and fled with gold, silver and Rs 35 lakh in cash . The robbery took place at a branch of Bank of Maharashtra, when two men allegedly entered the premises posing as customers .

title: IRS officers taken to police station from Odisha Raj Bhavan: Panel set up to probe what transpired
summary: The two IRS officers were allegedly turned away from the Raj Bhavan campus and taken to the Capital police station in a police vehicle on Thursday . The incident occurred when they were on official duty to coordinate the visit of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax (PCCIT), Odisha . The committee was formed after PCCIT D Sudhakara Rao, in a strongly worded letter to the secretary to the Governor, termed the incident “deeply distressing”

title: Rajnath leads BJP chorus: Opposition stands exposed, put own interest above that of nation
summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singhalleged that the development had exposed the “anti-women character” of the Congress-led INDIA bloc . “The entire country will always remember that the Opposition placed its political self-interest above the national interest and societal good,” Singh said .

title: Vedanta plant blast: Key ‘fan problem’, maintenance lapses under probe
summary: Early indications suggest that the blast on April 14 at Vedanta’sthermal power plant in Chhattisgarh’s Singhitarai was triggered by the failure of a key equipment called the Primary Air (PA) fan that may have led to a dangerous pressure build-up inside the boiler . This, in turn, caused a downstream pipe to rupture, triggering the blast . Vedanta Ltd acquired the plant in 2022 through insolvency proceedings .

title: Welcome ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon: India
summary: A 10-day ceasefire announced by US President Donald Trump and agreed on by Lebanon and Israel appeared to be holding in Lebanon on Friday . Talks are underway to extend a lasting truce between US and Iran — brokered by Pakistan . India is participating in a meeting called by the UK and France, on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz .

title: Doval reiterates India’s position on peace during meet with Ukraine’s NSA
summary: Umerov and Ajit Doval reviewed bilateral relations and discussed the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict . The Indian side reaffirmed its consistent position on the need for a peaceful settlement based on dialogue and diplomacy . While Delhi has walked the diplomatic tightrope between Russia and Ukraine, it has been perceived to be closer to Moscow because of its traditional ties .

title: Kin demand return of Navy veteran stuck in Qatar jail; he’s detained in separate case: Govt
summary: Commander Purnendu Tiwari was detained in Qatar in 2022, while seven returned in 2024 . Ministry of External Affairs maintains that the officer has been detained in a separate case . His family claims the cases are “stitched together” and the Indian authorities should have been able to secure his release by now .

title: Chhattisgarh boiler blast toll hits 21 as Opposition seeks culpable homicide charges against Vedanta officials
summary: The death toll in the explosion at a Vedanta Ltd.power plant in Chhattisgarh’s Sakti district rose to 21 . Five others remain critical and nine are under observation, doctors said . The incident occurred around 2.30 pm Tuesday when a massive explosion took place in Boiler-01 at the Vedanta power plant .

title: Wherever you go for votes, you will have to face the ire of women, Amit Shah warns Opposition
summary: The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill aimed to increase Lok Sabha seats for fast-tracking implementation of women's reservation . Shah accused the Congress of reversing its stand on the women’s reservation issue, and alleged that the party is now hesitating with “kintu-parantu” arguments, claiming that it fears losing political ground as women voters strongly support PM Narendra Modi .

title: Kerala teachers among 9 killed as van plunges into gorge at Valparai ghat in Tamil Nadu
summary: Police say the victims were teachers from a government lower primary school in Pang and their family members . The van was descending the Valparai ghat section towards Pollachi when the driver allegedly lost control while negotiating the thirteenth bend . The vehicle plunged down a steep slope and was reduced to a mangled wreck when it came to a halt far below at another bend .

title: ‘Hail the Constitution’: Opposition rejoices as Bill to tweak women’s quota law is defeated in LS
summary: Opposition hailed it as ‘historic’ and emphasised it was not against women’s reservation . Tamil Nadu chief Minister M K Stalin said the state had “defeatedDelhi” Samajwadi Party (SP) presidentAkhilesh Yadav said the BJP's “deceitful chariot’ has been stopped once again .

title: Tripura ADC elections: High hopes for tribal solidarity in Northeast as Tipra Motha sweeps 24 of 28 seats
summary: Tripura Tribal Areas Autonomous District Council’s (TTAADC) ruling Tipra Motha party swept the crucial local body elections held on Sunday . The tribal regional party made a spectacular comeback for a second term and reduced the BJP's previous tally by half . The BJP had campaigned aggressively, setting targets of winning all 28 seats, but its strategy of challenging its ally backfired .

title: Women’s reservation Bill defeated in Lok Sabha, fails to get 2/3rd majority
summary: The Constitutional (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, seeking to advance reservation of 33% seats for women in an expanded Lok Sabha and state Assemblies, was defeated in Lok Sabha Friday evening . Of the 528 members present in the House, 298 voted in favour of the Bill and 230 opposed it . The result well short of the two-thirds majority needed for a Constitutional amendment Bill to clear the House .

title: Vishu post with Krishna image, non-veg dish: 2 owners of Kerala restaurant booked
summary: Arshad Asharaf and Shamnas Kunhimuhammed run ‘Mehar Mandi and Grills’ in Cherthala, Alappuzha district . Restaurant owners allegedly posted greetings on April 15, when Kerala celebrated its harvest festival Vishu .

title: Mob snatches four rifles from forest officers during Assam clash
summary: Violence broke out in Assam’s Chirang district Friday, with a mob attacking a forest range office there in connection with the detention of some locals the previous day overalleged encroachment on reserved forest land . According to officials, the mob violence included pelting stones at the Runikhata range office and the personnel stationed there .

title: What was Rahul Gandhi’s Number 16 riddle in Parliament? Congress drops hint
summary: Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi ended his speech in the Lok Sabha with a riddle . Gandhi referred to the number '16' in a cryptic manner, without explaining its meaning . His remarks came during a heated debate over proposed amendments that aim to increase Lok Sabha seats, redraw constituencies and introduce a 33% women's quota .

title: My Biju Patnaik vs your Biju Patnaik: On his death anniversary, a competition to claim legacy
summary: Biju Patnaik’s younger son took a dig at the sidelined leaders for holding parallel shows . ‘Some people in Odisha politics are always happy with the betrayal role. They should remember that no mask can hide their sins for long. People know who is what,” he said .

title: ‘My sister managed to do what I couldn’t in 20 years — she made Amit Shah smile’, says Rahul Gandhi in Parliament
summary: Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi began his address to the House saying that his sister (Priyanka Gandhi Vadra) managed to achieve a feat he couldn’t in 20 years . At the beginning of his address, the Lok Sabha erupted over his quip on Prime Minister Narendra Modi and him not having “wife issues”

title: Pawan Khera denied anticipatory bail extension, Assam CM Himanta says ‘he should surrender’
summary: The Supreme Court has refused to extend the transit anticipatory bail given to Pawan Khera by the Telangana High Court . Kherra had sought relief to allow him time to approach a competent court in Assam in connection with a case accusing him of making false allegations against the wife of Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, Riniki Bhuyan Sarma .

title: ‘Not even a single rupee?’: Kejriwal questions PM Modi over ED raids on AAP leaders
summary: Aam Aadmi Party’s (AAP) national convener Arvind Kejriwal referred to theraids conducted by the Enforcement Directorate (ED) at properties linked to party leader and Punjab Cabinet Minister Sanjeev Arora early Friday . “The entire country is watching how petty politics you are indulging in just for the sake of power,” he said .

title: Opposition: Delimitation will turn out to be political demonetisation, linking it to women quota a trap
summary: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor led the Opposition charge in the Lok Sabha against the Centre on the three Bills linked to delimitation and women’s reservation . He said a march towards a new Indiaould not lead to a “disunited India” He said: “Delimitation will turn out to be political demonetisation. Don’t do it”

title: Tying women’s reservation to delimitation is a trap, says Kanimozhi as DMK members turn out in black
summary: Kanimozhi Karunanidhi said tying it to the women’s reservation bill was a “trap” by the BJP-led Centre . The DMK MPs were sporting black clothes on Thursday as well as on Thursday . Prime Minister Narendra Modi had taken a dig at DMK members for turning up in black attire .

title: Opposition fumes as Centre notifies 2023 Women’s Reservation Act mid-debate
summary: Opposition demands answers from the government about how amendments can be moved without the Act being notified . The Women’s Reservation Act, 2023, will ‘come into force” on April 16 . DMK MP Kanimozhi Karunanidhi, who was the first MP to speak in the discussion, also demanded answers . Speaker Om Birla told the House he will give a ruling on the issue .

title: IRS officers removed by police from Odisha Raj Bhavan say they ‘offered breath analyzer test’
summary: The two IRS officers were allegedly turned away from the Lok Bhavan campus and taken to the Capital police station in a police vehicle . The incident occurred when the two officers were on official duty to coordinate the visit of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax (PCCIT), Odisha, who paid a courtesy visit to the Governor . The police said the incident occurred due to “miscommunication”

title: Night blaze engulfs 6 cottages at Goa beach resort near Ashvem, tourists escape unharmed
summary: The fire broke out at a beach resort near the Ajoba temple near Ashvem beach . The blaze soon spread and engulfed the wooden cottages at the resort . No casualties were reported, police and fire department officials said . The incident comes days after six shacks at Arambol beach were gutted in a fire .

title: Verification drive ordered for madrasas in Uttarakhand after video of ‘children being brought from Bihar’
summary: A video purportedly shows a group of men confronting a man who is alleged to have brought children from Bihar to madrasas in Vikasnagar in the capital city . The purported video was posted by a Facebook page . The government said that a ‘detailed investigation’ will be carried out into the source of the children’s arrival, parental consent, and the individuals responsible for bringing them .

title: ‘A carefully crafted trap’: Why Stalin wants Delimitation Bill completely withdrawn
summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin sharply escalates his campaign against the Union government’s proposed delimitation legislation . He dismissed fresh assurances from Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah as inadequate and demanded that the bill be withdrawn in its entirety .

title: As Parliament discusses women’s quota, Opposition women MPs weigh in
summary: DMKChennaiSouth MP Dr T Sumathy questioned the ‘hurry’ behind the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam . Congress Karur MP Jothimani alleged that the BJP-led NDA government was using the Bill as “a shield to destroy South Indian states” Congress’sRajya SabhaMP Ranjeet Ranjan accused PM Modi of introducing the Bills in Parliament for “ulterior motives”

title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: PM Modi attacks DMK over women’s bill, debt burden in Tamil Nadu
summary: Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: PM Modi attacks DMK over women's bill, debt burden in Tamil Nadu . PM Modi: Women’s bill and debt burden are key issues in the state . Tamil Nadu will hold elections in 2026 Assembly Election 2026 .

title: Not just English, Khasi and Garo languages will be ‘official’ too in Meghalaya
summary: The Meghalaya cabinet announced the indigenous Khasi and Garo languages as the official languages of the state . All official business in the state is carried out in English . The move comes amid a long-standing demand for the inclusion of the languages in the Eight Schedule of the Constitution .

title: A legacy battle erupts: Rival events on Biju Patnaik’s death anniversary set up political face-off
summary: Biju Patnaik’s 29th death anniversary has turned into a political flashpoint in Odisha, with rival camps planning parallel events . The BJD, which has observed the day since its inception, will commemorate it this year as ‘Prabada Purusha Dibasa’ with seminars on the role in the freedom struggle and shaping modern Odisha . Triggering the political face-off is a parallel programme by a group of loyalists sidelined from the BJD .

title: 2 IRS officers stopped by Raj Bhavan security in Odisha, taken to police station
summary: The two officers were assigned duty at the Lok Bhavan to coordinate the visit of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax (PCCIT), Odisha, who paid a courtesy visit to Governor Hari Babu Kambhampati . According to sources, the two officers did not have entry passes and were stopped by the security personnel and aide-de-camp (ADC) The PCCIT said they were treated as “unauthorized entrants” and taken to the Capital Police Station .

title: US President Trump greets TS Sandhu on appointment as Delhi Lt Governor
summary: US President Donald Trump congratulated diplomat Taranjit Singh Sandhu on his appointment as the Lieutenant Governor of Delhi . Sandhu served as India’s Ambassador to the US from 2020-24 . After retiring from the Indian Foreign Service, Sandhu joined the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)

title: Time stands still next to a stream in spate, as an SPO waits for his son in Jammu
summary: Tanveer Ahmad, 18, was coming home from Jammu with two cows and two calves in a pickup vehicle . He was attacked by self-styled cow vigilantes in two vehicles on Sunday . He ran down the road and reportedly jumped into a nullah flowing along the highway to escape . Four men, known for “cow vigilantism’s” in the area, have been arrested .

title: Delimitation Bill fundamental attack on federal structure: Opposition
summary: Opposition in Lok Sabha tore into the government over the three Bills related to women’s quota . Terming the Delimitation Bill “a complete fundamental attack on the Indian federal structure” and “anti-Constitution” DMK MPs attended the special session dressed in black as a mark of protest .

title: If Women’s Quota Bill passed, democracy will be finished: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra
summary: Congress Wayanad MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra accused the government of “finishing off democracy” by putting pressure on institutions, such as the EC and the judiciary . Gandhi asked the government why can’t women get 33% quota from the existing strength of 543 seats in Lok Sabha .

title: ‘Why didn’t you move when I used siren?’: BJP MLA’s son booked after SUV hits 5 in Bhopal
summary: Madhya Pradesh Police have booked Dinesh Lodhi, son of BJP MLA Pritam Singh Lodhi . Accident occurred around 7:30 am near Karera police station area in Karera . The victims were three labourers riding a motorcycle and two women walking on the road . In a purported video, the MLA’s son was seen arguing with the injured .

title: Meghalaya approves Khasi, Garo as official state languages alongside English
summary: The Meghalaya cabinet announced the indigenous Khasi and Garo languages as the official languages of the state . All official business in the state is carried out in English . The move comes amid a long-standing demand for the inclusion of the languages in the Eight Schedule of the Constitution .

title: Mizoram household census kicks off; refugees to be surveyed next year
summary: The House Listing and Housing Census (HLO) kicked off in Mizoram on Thursday . The process is expected to continue till May 15, after which the second phase, the Population Enumeration (PE) will begin in February 2027 . Mizos have been sheltering thousands of Chin refugees from Myanmar and around 2,000 refugees from Bangladesh .

title: Red Fort blast: ‘Doctors’ module used explosives used in global terror incidents’
summary: Chargesheet filed against 10 accused persons in connection with an FIR registered at Nowgam police station in Srinagar . Investigation in the case began in October last year, when “provocative posters” in the name of the proscribed terrorist outfit Jaish-e-Mohammad were seen to have come up in the Nowgam area of Srinag . The accused, officials said, had formed a clandestine module, actively engaged in “radicalisation, recruitment and operational preparations for carrying out terrorist attacks across the country”

title: Vedanta chairman Anil Agarwal named in FIR after blast at power plant killed 20
summary: Police in Chhattisgarh’s Sakti district file an FIR, naming Vedanta Group chairman Anil Agarwal and others, in connection with the explosion at the Vedanta power plant on Tuesday . An excessive accumulation of fuel inside the boiler furnace caused extreme pressure, leading to the explosion, police said . Vedanta announced Rs 35 lakh compensation for the families of the deceased and Rs 17 lakh for those injured .

title: ‘Samrat Choudhary is like my brother’: Amid speculation of discontent, Nitish’s son Nishant congratulates new Bihar CM
summary: There had been speculation in days leading up to the swearing-in that Nishant Kumar would enter the NDA government as a Deputy CM . But according to JD(U) sources, he eventually backed out . Bijendra Prasad Yadav and Vijay Kumar Choudhary took oath as Deputy Chief Ministers on Wednesday .

title: Priyanka, Akhilesh lead Opposition onslaught: Govt using women quota to push delimitation, change electoral map
summary: Opposition leaders accuse the BJP of using the plank of reservation of seats for women to push for delimitation of constituencies to seek electoral advantage . The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill to amend the women’s quota law was introduced in Lok Sabha after a division of votes demanded by the Opposition . Congress MPs Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and Gaurav Gogoi led the Opposition onslaught .

title: RS Dy Chairman Harivansh to be reelected unopposed as Opp boycotts polls
summary: Rajya Sabha Deputy Chairman Harivansh is set to be elected unopposed for a third term on Friday . The Opposition has decided to boycott the elections, as a mark of protest . The Central government has so far received five notices for motions to elect the Deputy Chairman – all from the Central government .

title: South Korean President to visit India from April 19 to 21
summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung will be visiting India from April 19 to 21, accompanied by First Lady Kim Hea Kyung and a high-level delegation . This will be President Lee’s maiden visit to India after assuming office in June 2025 . Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Lee met last year during the G7 and G20 summits .

title: Modi calls for ‘lasting peace’ in West Asia
summary: Austrian Chancellor Christian Stocker is on his maiden visit to India as well as Asia . This is the second high-level political visit by a European leader since the war in Tehran began on February 28 . The two sides also signed various pacts, including on military, counter-terrorism and skills development .

title: 173 years of Indian Railways: All you need to know about India’s first train journey
summary: On April 16, 1853, the country’s first passenger train ran between Bombay (now Mumbai) and Thane in Maharashtra . The launch of train services in India marked a new era of mobility and connectivity . In the coming months, the national transporter is also set to introduce hydrogen-powered trains .

title: As Lok Sabha discusses women’s quota, two Rajya Sabha women MPs weigh in: ‘There is a hidden intent’, ‘No question of seats being reduced’
summary: Women MPs from both sides of the aisle in the Rajya Sabha weigh in on the issue . The Congress’sRajya SabhaMP Ranjeet Ranjan accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of introducing the Bills in Parliament because of “ulterior motives” The BJP's Rekha Sharma called it a “historic day”

title: BJP turned ‘nari’ into ‘nara’, wants to deny OBC women their rights: Akhilesh slams party over women’s quota Bill
summary: Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh demanded that OBC and Muslim women be given reservation under theprovisions of the women’s reservationlegislation . He said that if the Bills were as good as the BJP claimed, they should be made public to women workers protesting in Noida and other cities . The SP president said he agreed with Congress MP Gaurav Gogoi, who alleged that through .

title: From first to final reservation chart: How Indian Railways confirms train tickets
summary: The Ministry of Railways has revised the timing for the preparation of the first reservation chart . The move aims to reduce the anxiety of passengers travelling from remote locations . The second reservation chart is prepared around 30 minutes before the scheduled departure of the train . According to Indian Railways, any vacancy of Tatkal quota are allocated to waitlisted passengers .

title: ‘In favour of a rules-based world order’: Austrian Chancellor Christian Stocker backs PM Modi’s call for ‘lasting peace’
summary: Austrian Chancellor Christian Stocker is on his maiden visit to India as well as Asia . The Austrian Chancellor is visiting India from April 14 to 18, and met External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar . This is the second high-level political visit by a European leader since the war in Tehran began on February 28 .

title: Meghwal introduces 3 Bills to advance women’s quota to 2029, says states’ share in expanded Lok Sabha won’t change
summary: Union Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal Thursday introduced all three Bills aimed at advancing women’s reservation in Lok Sabha and state assemblies by 2029 . He asserted that the proportion of seats allocated to each state in the expanded Lok Sabha would remain unchanged .

title: Telangana caste survey findings: SCs, STs three times more backward than general categories
summary: Backward Classes Welfare Minister Ponnam Prabhakar said SCs and STs were found to be three times more backward than General Castes . The survey covered over 3.50 crore Telangana households — nearly 97 per cent — and examined 242 caste groups . The report highlighted that large sections still borrow from moneylenders at high interest rates .

title: ‘Bid to take away protections offered by Indira Gandhi, Vajpayee’: Congress on Delimitation Bill
summary: Congress general secretary K C Venugopal said the intention behind introducing the Delimitation Bill is to take away the protection that was offered by Indira Gandhi and Atal Bihari Vajpayee — a tacit reference to the 1976 and 2001 freeze on delimitation . He said that the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP-led Centre) introduced the Bill in 2023, and now it is saying it cannot happen as per it .

title: Delimitation Bill violates federalism, denies states with low population fair share: Owaisi
summary: Lok Sabha MP and Asaduddin Owaisi said the Bill violates federalism and denies states with lower populations their fair share . The All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen chief said that a minister has to give seven days’ notice to introduce a Bill, which has been violated .

title: Centre allows Shikhar Dhawan, Baba Bageshwar foundations to receive foreign funding
summary: The Shikhar Dhawan Foundation has been added to the list of entities cleared by MHA to accept overseas contributions . This development comes amid a money laundering investigation by the Enforcement Directorate (ED) The ED attached immovable property worth Rs 4.5 crore belonging to former Indian cricketer Dhawan, linked to banned betting platform 1xBet .

title: Parliament Special Session Live Updates: ‘Very unfortunate… you have lost an opportunity’, says Kiren Rijiju as women’s quota Bill fails
summary: Parliament Special Session Live Updates: ‘Very unfortunate… you have lost an opportunity’, says Kiren Rijiju . ‘You have lost a chance’ he says as women’s quota Bill fails . Parliament special session continues to take place in Parliament .

title: Is the skeleton really hers? Questions linger over case of missing Jharkhand teen
summary: Jharkhand High Court raises questions over police investigation in the case of an 18-year-old girl who was reported missing eight months ago . Last week, police found skeletal remains that they said were the girl’s, but the High Court has questioned the basis of their conclusion .

title: Will Chhattisgarh be next to adopt UCC? Panel ready to meet public and experts
summary: The move seeks to replace the current system of religion-based personal laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and family matters with a uniform and simplified legal structure . The proposed UCC is aimed at simplifying laws to promote religious and gender equality . Article 44 of the Constitution directs the state to work towards implementing a UCC for all citizens .

title: Vande Mataram row intensifies in Madhya Pradesh as police book 2 Congress councillors
summary: Madhya Pradesh Police registered an FIR against two Congress councillors who refused to singVande Mataramduring the Indore Municipal Corporation’s Budget session earlier this month . The incident took place on April 8, when the issue of singing the national song led to a confrontation inside the House . Both councillors were summoned over two days for questioning, during which their statements were recorded .

title: ‘He was innocent’: BSF constable’s family seeks answers after custodial death
summary: BSF constable Jaswinder Singh, 30, died while in custody of Narcotics Control Bureau . He was picked up on March 3 while returning home to Diwangarh village near border with Pakistan . His mother and wife allege he was falsely implicated in the case, dating back to 2024 . The NCB says he died of “cardiac events” while under arrest .

title: Government plan: 50% more Lok Sabha seats for each state, South share unchanged
summary: The government will most likely bring on Thursday a ‘schedule’ in Parliament to clearly specify the number of Lok Sabha seats for each state . This will show that the percentage share of each state in the Lok Sabha’s total strength is the same as it is now, top sources said . “There will be no confusion… everything will be legally done,” said a source .

title: Former President Pratibha Patil, Meira Kumar, Mayawati back Bill
summary: Pratibha Patil, Meira Kumar and UP ex-CM Mayawati support Centre's efforts to expedite 33% reservation for women in Lok Sabha and Assemblies . Congress has been critical of Centre's alleged hurry and opacity in pushing the three Bills aimed at advancing implementation of women’s quota to 2029 .

title: Eminent women artistes invited to attend House sitting on quota Bills
summary: Parliament is set to convene a three-day special sitting to discuss the Women’s Reservation Bill . The Bill would introduce a 33 per cent quota for women in the Lok Sabha and state assemblies from the 2029 general elections . The invitation has been extended “only to leading female artistes of the country, including recipients of the Central Sangeet Natak Akademi Award, Padma honours and other reputed artistes”

title: Know the Articles which three proposed Bills will amend
summary: The amendments are related to Articles 55, 81, 82, 170, 330, 332 and 334A . These broadly fall under sections pertaining to the Executive . The amendments relate to the election of the President and Vice President of India . Home Minister Amit Shahtabling The Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill 2026 .

title: First-ever bat conservation assessment flags threat to species, data dark spots
summary: The first-ever national assessment (State of India’s Bats, 2024-25) has reported . West Bengal leads with 68 bat species, followed by 66 in Meghalaya, 52 in Uttarakhand, 41 each in Kerala and Karnataka and 43 in Sikkim . The report found bats roosting across natural habitats and man-made structures .

title: Women’s quota pretext to push through delimitation that could disproportionately favour certain states: Rajya Sabha MP
summary: The commitment to reserve one-third of seats for women has already been finalised with the constitutional amendment . Jose K Mani, the Chairman of Kerala Congress (M) party, said that the real objective seems to “lie in restructuring parliamentary representation among states”

title: Focus on ‘development roadmap’ as Mizoram signs agreement with ‘only remaining underground group’
summary: Mizoram government has signed a 'Memorandum of Settlement' with Hmar People’s Convention-Democratic . The agreement was signed in Sakawrdai on Tuesday by Home Department Secretary David Lalthantluanga and HPC-D(LF) head Lalhmingthanga Sanate . An arms surrender ceremony for 43 cadres of the organisation will be held on April 30 .

title: Expelled Congress leader Mohammed Moquim launches new party in Odisha
summary: Expelled Congress leader Mohammed Moquim launches Odisha Janata Congress . His daughter and suspended Congress MLA Sofia Firdous among hundreds in attendance . The new outfit is eyeing the forthcoming panchayat and urban local body polls scheduled early next year .

title: Vedanta plant blast toll at 20. Among victims: Man saving to build home, widower earning for kids
summary: A total of 20 people died and 16 were injured after superheated steam generated from a boiler tube blast rained on them at Vedanta’s Singhitarai power plant . A magisterial inquiry has been ordered into the incident, said Sakti Collector Amrit Vikas Topno, adding that the best possible treatment will be provided to those injured . “He was saving money to build our family home. Now, who will do it?” asks distraught young man .

title: Odisha’s Naveen Patnaik opposes delimitation bill, says it ‘hits spirit of cooperative federalism’
summary: Former Odisha Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik said his party will support it only if Odisha’s political rights remain intact . Odisha currently has 21 MPs in the Lok Sabha, but its share would fall to 3.4% . According to reports, Odisha would be fourth biggest loser in the exercise .

title: India calls for safe, unimpeded maritime transport at Japan energy meet
summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar underlined India’s “strong commitment to safe and unimpeded transit passage of maritime shipping” He participated in the AZEC Plus — Asia Zero Emission Community — meeting convened by Japan to discuss supply chain disruptions in the energy markets . US President Donald Trump said he was “permanently opening the Strait of Hormuz”

title: Samrat sworn in as Bihar CM; Cabinet rejig after May 4 poll results
summary: Samrat Choudhary took over as the first BJP chief minister with two deputies from JD(U). A Cabinet expansion is likely to take place after results are announced for West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry Assembly elections on May 4 . As Deputy CM, Samrat was in charge of the state home ministry .

title: BRICS Foreign Ministers’ meet on May 14-15; Wang Yi, Lavrov may attend
summary: India is likely to host the BRICS Foreign Ministers’ meeting on May 14-15, sources said on Wednesday . The meeting is expected to be attended by Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov . Iran Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi might join the meeting in person or virtually .

title: Opposition chorus: We back women’s quota, but delimitation a devious agenda, tool of BJP
summary: Opposition parties say they will vote against the Constitution amendment Bill provisions on delimitation . Opposition MPs said they will support women’s reservation if it is provided in the current 543-member Lok Sabha . Some among them called the plan “a devious agenda” and ‘a tool to win a majority for the ruling party”

title: ED attaches Rs 159 cr assets in West Bengal illegal coal mining case; hawala network also unearthed
summary: The Enforcement Directorate has provisionally attached assets worth Rs 159.51 crore under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002, in a case involving large-scale illegal coal mining and pilferage from Eastern Coalfields Limited (ECL) leasehold areas in West Bengal . A probe exposed a syndicate headed by Anup Majee, alias Lala, which allegedly orchestrated the operations .

title: Davanagere bypoll fallout: Karnataka Congress suspends MLC Abdul Jabbar from party membership
summary: Abdul Jabbar was among the aspirants for the party ticket for the Davanagere South bypoll . He was overlooked in favour of Samarth Shamanur, grandson of former MLA Shamanur Shivashankarappa and son of Karnataka minister S S Mallikarjuna . Jabbar had resigned aschairman of KPCC’s minority wing .

title: India, US to hold trade talks in Washington from April 20
summary: India is set to send an official delegation to Washington next week to resume high-stakes trade negotiations with the United States . The visit comes at a critical juncture as both countries move closer to finalising an interim bilateral trade agreement framework . The development comes barely a day after Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US PresidentDonald Trumpspoke over the phone for around 40 minutes .

title: ‘Age tokens’ via DigiLocker to regulate kids’ social media use in Andhra Pradesh
summary: The Andhra Pradesh government is exploring issuing ‘age tokens’ integrated with DigiLocker to verify the age of social media users . This is part of its plan to regulate social media use among children . The government wants to address growing concerns over online safety, especially for children and women .

title: Delimitation row escalates: Stalin calls urgent meeting of DMK district heads, black flag protest planned across Tamil Nadu
summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin calls for statewide black flag protests on Thursday . DMK convenes an urgent consultation with his party’s district secretaries even as the DMK called for statewide protests . The dispute is shaping into one of the most significant federal flashpoints in recent years, with implications that could ripple well beyond state boundaries .

title: ‘A decisive stride’: Former President Pratibha Patil writes to PM Modi, extends support to women’s quota Bill
summary: Pratibha Devisingh Patil has written to Prime Minister Narendra Modi commending efforts to expedite 33 per cent women’s reservation in the Lok Sabha and state Assemblies . Patil was a Congress MLA in Maharashtra from 1962 to 1985, aRajya SabhaMP from 1985 to 1990, and a Lok Sabha MP from 1991 to 1996 . The letter comes close on the heels of three Bills being introduced in Parliament to implement women's reservation .

title: Bihar gets first BJP CM as Samrat Choudhary takes oath in Patna
summary: BJP leader Samrat Choudhary sworn in as Chief Minister of Bihar . It is the first time the party will lead a government in the state . Nitish Kumar relinquished the chief minister’s post on Tuesday, dissolving his Cabinet .

title: This isolated under-construction building in Jharkhand village hosted a ‘Rs 20-crore fraud’ operation
summary: Jharkhand Police raid a three-storey under-construction building that is purportedly being built to house a private nursing college . The building stands on a plot of land spread across nearly 40 bigha (25 acres) at a location where there is little habitation within a 3-km radius . The exam, however, took place as scheduled on Sunday, with police saying there was no paper leak and the questions and answers the gang gave the aspirants did not match the exam question paper .

title: ‘Will never allow this to happen in J-K’: Omar Abdullah says won’t tolerate jungle raj after man ‘assaulted by cow vigilantes
summary: Tanveer Ahmad Chopan, 30, went missing while allegedly being chased by cow vigilantes in Ramban . Chief Minister Omar Abdullah said that ‘jungle raj’ will not be tolerated in Jammu and Kashmir . He urged Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha to take strict action against those involved .

title: Kerala Dalit student got 96 calls from online loan app day before his death: Police
summary: Nithin Raj R L (22), a native of Uzhamalakkal village in Thiruvananthapuram, died after a fall from a building at the Kannur Dental College campus on Friday . Police have filed a case under charges of abetment of suicide and under SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act . Two faculty members, including head of a department at the college, have been booked in the case .

title: Giant LED lotus, firecrackers, balloons: With BJP set to get its 1st CM in Bihar, Patna party office wears festive look
summary: Deputy Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary is set to become CMNitish Kumar’s successor, after he was elected BJP Legislature Party leader on Tuesday . The party is celebrating the upcoming swearing-in of the first BJP Chief Minister of Bihar .

title: Justice Shekhar Yadav, who faced ‘hate speech’ impeachment motion, retires today
summary: Justice Shekhar Kumar Yadav of the Allahabad High Court is set to retire Wednesday . A stalled impeachment motion in Parliament over a contentious speech he delivered at an event organised by an RSS-affiliated organisation in December 2024 . Justice Yadav allegedly used a derogatory slur for Muslims, while criticising practices such as triple talaq .

title: Delimitation divide: Stalin, Revanth raise alarm, TDP backs Centre
summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin warns the Centre against bulldozing through a Constitutional amendment in the midst of Assembly elections . Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy sought national and political consensus on increasing seats in the Lok Sabha and an immediate implementation of women’s reservation without linking it to the increase in seats .

title: Women’s quota: Govt for House cap at 850, delimitation on 2011 Census
summary: The government circulated three Bills to pave the way for fresh delimitationbased on “such Census, as Parliament may by law determine, of which the relevant figures have been published”, likely the 2011 count . The move builds on the Constitution (One Hundred and Thirty-First Amendment) Act, 2023, which had introduced women’s reservation but linked its implementation to a future Census and delimitation exercise .

title: IMD forecasts heatwave in northwest, central and south India up to April 20
summary: South peninsula, central and northwest India regions are bracing for heatwave conditions expected to last for the next one week . Heatwave spell across Telangana, Rayalaseema, north interior Karnataka, Madhya Maharashtra, Vidarbha, Marathwada, Chhattisgarh and Jharkhand and western Rajasthan will persist till April 20 . This is the season’s first widespread heatwave to affect the country .

title: 16 killed, 19 injured in boiler blast at Vedanta plant in Chhattisgarh
summary: The blast is said to have occurred around 2.30 pm at Vedanta’s Singhitarai power plant . After the pipeline burst, superheated steam with a temperature of around 600 degrees fell on the people who were having their lunch . Vedanta Limited Chhattisgarh Thermal Power Plant said a thorough investigation has been initiated .

title: Days after 28 cops suspended over death of missing girl, Jharkhand Police Association demands action against SP
summary: Jharkhand Police Association calls for suspension of Bokaro Superintendent of Police . 28 police personnel and staff of a police station in Bokaro were suspended in connection with alleged lapses in investigating the case of an 18-year-old girl who had been reported missing and found dead eight months later . The victim’s mother, Rekha Devi, also called for removal of senior police officers .

title: ‘Bristled with contradictions’: Oppn slams proposed Bills on delimitation, women’s quota
summary: Telangana CMRevanth Reddy urges PM Modi to convene an all-party meeting at the earliest . Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Stalin warns against 'bulldozing' amendment related to Women’s Reservation Bill . Congress leader Abhishek Manu Singhvi says Bill is “bristled with contradictions, illegalities and unsustainable proportions”

title: Jaishankar discusses Iran, Hormuz, Lebanon with Israel’s Sa’ar
summary: India's External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar spoke to Israel’s Foreign Minister Gideon Sa’ar on Tuesday . They discussed Iran, the Strait of Hormuz and Lebanon – as peace talks between the US and Iran in Islamabad came to a halt with no deal struck .

title: No wage hike for MG-NREGS workers for now as Centre looks to roll out VB-G RAM G
summary: It is the first time in more than a decade that the Centre has not released a revised NREGS wage notification in February-March . The Union Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD) has informed the states that the wages for the fiscal year 2026-27 will be notified when the G RAM G comes into effect . Until then, MG-NREGS will continue, it is learnt .

title: Trump dials Modi for 40-minute call; PM says ‘stressed importance of keeping Hormuz open’
summary: The phone call was the first between the two leaders after the US and Iran announced a conditional ceasefire last week . The two leaders last spoke on March 24, their first phone call after the start of the war on February 28 . Both sides underlined the importance of the Strait of Hormuz and keeping the waterway open, it signalled concern over disruption in global energy supply chains .

title: ‘From courtroom to community’: Jharkhand’s Simdega museum blends tribal memory with colonial history
summary: A colonial-era administrative building in Jharkhand’s Simdega, near the borders of Odisha and Chhattisgarh, has been turned into a museum . Located in the forested southwestern part of the district, the building carries a layered history . The museum brings together colonial records and objects from everyday tribal life .

title: Class 5 student dies days after 150 children who ate ‘stale food’ at Odisha residential school fall ill
summary: 150 students fell ill after eating at Kokabandha Ashram School in tribal-majority Mayurbhanj district . The headmaster of the school, Jayanta Kumar Panigrahi, has been suspended and an inquiry has been ordered . Parents staged an agitation after the death of the student and demanded action against authorities .

title: Nine feared dead in blast at Vedanta’s Chhattisgarh unit
summary: Blast is said to have occurred at the Vedanta Limited power plant in Chhattisgarh’s Sakti district on Tuesday afternoon . At least nine people are feared dead and several others seriously injured, police say . The plant says it is in the process of ascertaining details of the incident .

title: ‘Bhagyarekha of progress’: Modi launches Delhi-Dehradun highway
summary: The 210-km highway is expected to cut down travel time between Delhi and Dehradun to 2.5 hours . Built at a cost of Rs 11,868 crore, it will cut travel time by two hours to two hours . Modi said the transport infrastructure of the country is the “bhagyarekha” of progress .

title: Amid delimination debate, Telangana CM Revanth Reddy proposes hybrid model
summary: Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy has called for a protracted struggle to increase seats in the southern states on the pro-rata principle . Reddy said that Southern India will be meted out grave injustice on the 50 per cent seat increase formula .

title: Nitish Kumar resigns as Bihar Chief Minister
summary: JD(U) supremo Nitish Kumar resigns as Bihar Chief Minister . Kumar ends his tenure as the longest serving CM of the state . Kumar recommended dissolution of his cabinet, state minister Ram Kripal Yadav said . Kumar took oath as a member of Rajya Sabha last week .

title: 800 dog organs in formalin, a cancelled contract, and a suspected sterilisation fraud in MP
summary: Almost 800 preserved reproductive organs from dogs, stuffed into two containers, were recovered from a rented room in Madhya Pradesh’s Mandla . The discovery has raised questions regarding a municipal contract awarded to a Jabalpur-based private agency for the sterilisation of stray dogs across Mandla city . The allegation is that the agency intended to present the organs as proof of completed surgeries and claim government money for work that was never done .

title: ‘If BJP was so confident to win, then why did it use a weapon like SIR?’ Shashi Panja
summary: TMC minister Shashi Panja was one of 60 lakh-odd people whose names were put on the adjudication list by the Election Commission in the controversial Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral roll in West Bengal . Panja, the three-term MLA from Shyampukur, is contesting again after her name was included in the voter roll .

title: ‘Final cry of nation’s workers whose every voice was ignored’: Rahul Gandhi on Noida wage protests
summary: Leader of the Opposition (LoP) in the Lok Sabha and Congress MP Rahul Gandhi expressed solidarity with the workers protesting in Noida . Gandhi said that the protests were a final cry of India’s workers, “whose every voice was ignored” He said runaway inflation is “choking” and “drowning” the labourers in the “depths of debt”

title: ‘Final warning’ on delimitation: Stalin escalates federal fight, invokes Tamil Nadu’s agitational past
summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin issued what he described as a “final warning” to the Union government over the proposed delimitation exercise . He alleges that a Constitutional amendment was being pushed through without consultation during an ongoing election period . “Vanakkam. This video serves two purposes: to speak about the grave danger that has now reached the very doorstep ofTamil Nadu,” Stalin said .

title: On Pahalgam anniversary, India to highlight Pak-based terror at Washington exhibition
summary: The Indian Embassy in Washington will organise an exhibition centred around the theme of “Human Cost of Terrorism” The exhibition begins next week and is likely to be held at a public venue near Capitol Hill . It is learnt that it will focus on the 26 people who lost their lives on April 22 last year — 25 Indian nationals and one Nepali citizen .

title: Days after Kashmir universities cancel MoUs, US-based NGO ‘temporarily suspends’ activities
