summary: Kashmir Care Foundation is a US-based non-profit organisation of Kashmiri diaspora that organised mentorship programmes, seminars and webinars for students in the Valley . Three top universities of Kashmir, the University of Kashmir,. the Islamic University of Science and Technology (IUST) and the Sher-e-Kashmir University of Agricultural Sciences and Technology .

title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: Vijay unveils TVK manifesto
summary: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: Vijay unveils TVK manifesto . Vijay: TVK is a state of Tamil Nadu, Tamil Nadu and Tamil Nadu . Assembly elections 2026 will be held in the state capital, Delhi, on November 26 .

title: In a Jharkhand village, a 12-year-old’s death during Ram Navami sent the rumour mill into overdrive — until the truth emerged
summary: A 12-year-old girl from a village in Jharkhand’s Hazaribagh district was found dead under bamboo shrubs in an open patch less than a kilometre from her home . The mother filed a complaint naming a fellow villager, accusing him of allegedly kidnapping and murdering her daughter . But the man they detained was soon released and police said they’d found no evidence to connect him to the crime . The decision to let the man go sparked political ire, with right-wing groups and opposition parties targeting the government .

title: ‘Daughters can’t wait endlessly’: PM Modi issues open letter urging immediate passage of women’s quota
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi says Parliament is on theverge of ‘making history” as a special session prepares to discuss key amendments to the Women’s Reservation Act . The Opposition has been critical of the government for seeking its support for women's reservation without yet sharing draft Bill or calling an all-party meeting .

title: ED says I-PAC laundered Rs 50 crore, gets 10-day custody of its director
summary: Vinesh Chandel, a director and co-founder of I-PAC, was arrested in connection with a money laundering case linked to alleged coal smuggling . The Enforcement Directorate said the political consultancy firm was involved in laundering proceeds of crime to the tune of crores of rupees and the amount detected so far is about Rs 50 crore .

title: Misri discusses freedom of navigation with French Foreign minister; Jaishankar dials Kuwait, Singapore counterparts
summary: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri and France’s Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs Jean-Noël Barrot discussed the ways and means of restoring freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz . This happened on a day External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar spoke to Kuwait's Foreign Minister Jarrah Jaber Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah .

title: Ostracised, extorted, humiliated: Inside cases that led Rajasthan HC to act on khaps
summary: Rajasthan High Court issues strict directions to the state government to check the menace of khap panchayats in the state . The court noted misuse of community power; social boycott and isolation; forced monetary demands; suppression of personal choices; mental harassment and trauma; fear based control system; gender based vulnerability .

title: Dalit student’s death puts spotlight on caste, colour bias in Kannur college
summary: Nithin Raj R L (22), who hailed from Uzhamalakkal village in Thiruvananthapuram, died after a fall from a building at the Kannur Dental College campus on Friday . Police have registered a case under BNS section 108 (abetment of suicide) and SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act . Two faculty members have been named as accused in the case; college management has suspended them .

title: Case of the disappearing water bodies: Survey reveals 8,940 missing in Bihar
summary: The Centre’s latest water body census shows that 8,940 water bodies in Bihar are missing . According to the census, 45 percent of these water bodies are owned by the Bihar government . The vast majority — about 91 percent — are ponds, while the rest are lakes, tanks, reservoirs, check dams, and percolation dams .

title: Iran, India in touch on passage of ships through Strait of Hormuz: Envoy Mohammad Fathali
summary: The Iranian embassy is sending a fresh consignment of about 20,000 kg of medicine from India on Monday night . The latest batch will be sent in a Mahaan air flight fromDelhito Mashhad, sources said . The medicines are mainly for Iranians who have been injured in US and Israeli strikes in the war before the two-week ceasefire was declared on April 9 .

title: Stage set for NDA power transfer in Bihar, Nitish Kumar likely to resign today
summary: The stage is set for a transfer of power within the NDA government in Bihar . Chief Minister Nitish Kumar likely to resign from the post on Tuesday following his election to the Rajya Sabha . The new Chief Minister is expected to be from the BJP, marking a first for a party that traces its electoral history in Bihar to the 1962 election .

title: Panel examining SC status for Dalits post-conversion gets a third extension
summary: The Commission of Inquiry was appointed in October 2022 for a two-year period . Its tenure was initially extended for one year up to October 10, 2025, and later for six months up to April 10 . The third extension comes close on the heels of a March 24 Supreme Court ruling that the SC status is only to Hindus, Sikhs and Buddhists .

title: Himanta’s comments ‘false, defamatory’, led to ‘online abuse’: Assam poll debutant’s mother writes to women’s panel
summary: Sujata Gurung Chowdhury’s mother has written to the National Commission of Women regarding what she alleged were “false, defamatory and misleading statements” made about her by Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma . Sarma on several occasions targeted Assam Jatiya Parishad's CentralGuwahaticandidate Kunki Chowdury, as well as her family .

title: ED arrests I-PAC director Vinesh Chandel in Bengal ‘coal scam’ case
summary: Vinesh Chandel is a director and co-founder of political consultancy firm I-PAC . He was arrested in connection with amoney laundering case linked to alleged coal smuggling . West Bengal will have a two-phase poll on April 23 and April 29 . TMC general secretary Abhishek Banerjee described the arrest as 'alarming'

title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project: Anand bullet train station to be developed as multi-modal hub
summary: The Anand bullet train station will have a height of 25.6 metres and a total built-up area of 44,073 sqm . The station will feature three floors, with two side platforms and four tracks in between . NHSRCL has designed the station’s façade and interiors drawing inspiration from the fluid nature of milk droplets .

title: Tripura election panel countermands ADC poll in one centre over EVM tempering
summary: Tripura State Election Commission countermanded polling at a centre in the Tribal Areas Autonomous District Council (TTAADC) elections following evidence of tampering with Electronic Voting Machines . The Commission suspended voting at booth 17/39 of Hirapur Senior Basic School in the Pekuarjala-Janmejoynagar constituency, ordering a fresh poll for April 16 . The election was marred by sporadic violence, including crude bomb explosions and candidate attacks .

title: ‘Serial killer’ targeting women living alone arrested in Tirupati; linked to 21 cases across 3 states
summary: P Vishwanath, 45, allegedly targeted single women or those living alone across several states . He is linked to at least 21 offences, including six murder cases, police said . Police recovered 127 grams of stolen gold ornaments, Rs 20 lakh in cash and autorickshaw used in crimes .

title: ‘Hanging between trust and mistrust’: Sonam Wangchuk urges Centre to resume talks with Ladakh groups
summary: Sonam Wangchuk urges Centre to resume talks with groups representing Ladakh's socio-political leadership . Talks between the Union Ministry of Home Affairs and the two Ladakh groups have remained at an impasse since violence broke out in Leh in September last year .

title: Man missing after jumping in nullah to escape cow vigilantes, angry locals block J-K highway
summary: Tanveer Ahmad Chopan, 30, fell into a nullah while allegedly being chased by cow vigilantes . Angry residents blocked the Jammu-Srinagar national highway near Maggarkote in Jammu and Kashmir’s Ramban district . Four people have been arrested in the case so far, police said .

title: Hear her roar: Anita Chaudhary, 30, a guardian of the forest
summary: Anita Chaudhary is credited with helping rescue 500 animals and cracking down on smugglers and illegal miners . The Shergarh Sanctuary in Baran, Rajasthan, is spread across 9,880 hectares or 99 sq km . The sanctuary boasts of leopards, sloth bear, hyenas, wild boars, chinkaras and sambar deer . The WWF’s ‘Machchli National Award’ was the fourth recognition of her work .

title: Love triangle, hired gun, shared location: Wife arrested for ‘helping plot Odisha man’s murder’
summary: Soumya Sagar Samal, 23, was shot dead by bike-borne assailants in Odisha on April 6 . His wife, Subhasree Behera, had allegedly shared his location with the killers . Police suspect marital discord and a love triangle led to the killing .

title: After Nitish Kumar’s resignation, Bihar govt will be ‘run from Gujarat’: Tejashwi Yadav
summary: Tejashwi Prasad Yadav announced the restructuring of the Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) student wing . Yadav alleged that after Nitish Kumar resigns as CM, Bihar government would be “run from Gujarat”

title: From Maoist stronghold to tap water: Remote Abujhmad village gets first home supply
summary: Nelangur is among the remotest villages, located on hilly terrain about 52 km from Narayanpur district headquarters and nearly 8 km from Kuwakodi village in Maharashtra’s Bhamragad taluka . Last April, security forces set up a police camp, clearing the security vacuum and easing Naxal influence in the area .

title: MeT dept forecasts ‘below-normal’ monsoon rainfall on El Niño concerns
summary: India receives more than 70 per cent of its annual rainfall during the four-month monsoon season (June-September), with July and August contributing the most . This is the first time in at least a decade that IMD has forecast a below normal rainfall at the first stage of the season .

title: 213 km journey in 2.5 hours: PM Modi inaugurates Delhi-Dehradun Expressway
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modion inaugurated the Delhi-Dehradun Expressway on Tuesday . The 213-km-longexpressway will play a key role in easing congestion in the National Capital Territory (NCT) ofDelhi . The expressway is expected to reduce travel time between Delhi and Delhi to just 150 minutes (2.5 hours)

title: Railways expedites 827 km line projects to improve border connectivity in Rajasthan
summary: The Ministry of Railways has allocated Rajasthan – Rs 10,228 crore in budgetary support for FY 2026-27 . The national transporter has been working to connect various regions to the rail network so that the people of the remotest corner in the country can benefit from the facilities .

title: Congress issues whip to all its Lok Sabha MPs to be present for special Parliament sitting on April 16-18: ‘Most important’
summary: Congress party issues whip to all its MPs in Lok Sabha to be present for special sitting of Parliament from April 16 to 18 . Party requested all its members to remain present in the House from 11.00 am till its adjournment on all the three days, ‘without fail’

title: ‘Parliament making history’: PM Modi says special session to end decades of wait for women’s reservation
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi says Parliament is on the verge of ‘making history’ Parliament is set to discuss amendments to the Women’s Reservation Act . The Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, which provides one-third reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and state Assemblies, was passed in 2023 . The proposed amendments are essential to clear the path for their implementation, starting with the 2029 general elections .

title: MEA MoS, BJP’s Naqvi, Congress’s Khurshid attend event to mark 40th day of Khamenei killing
summary: Minister of State for External Affairs Pabitra Margherita attended a commemoration ceremony in the memory of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the late Supreme Leader of Iran, who was killed in a strike by US and Israel 40 days ago . Former Cabinet minister Mukhtar Abbas Naqvi attended the meet besides former Cabinet minister Salman Khurshid .

title: Bank Holiday Today: Banks to remain shut up to 4 days next week; Ambedkar Jayanti, regional New Year festivals to impact services
summary: Several Indian banks will remain closed between April 13 and April 19 due to regional festivals, including Ambedkar Jayanti, Pohela Boishakh, Bohag Bihu and Tamil New Year . While physical branch services will be unavailable on notified days, digital banking and ATM services will continue to function .

title: ‘15-20 men came on bikes, intercepted us 60 metres from police station’: BCCL officials ‘assaulted’ in Dhanbad
summary: Two senior officials of Bharat Coking Coal Limited were allegedly chased and assaulted by a group of men led by a suspected illegal mining operative in Jharkhand’s Dhanbad . The incident took place when a team of BCCL officials and Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) personnel visited the Block-2 mine area around 12.30 pm to probe repeated vandalism of surveillance cameras .

title: 3 Kashmir universities cancel MoU with US-based NGO
summary: Kashmir University, the Islamic University of Science and Technology (IUST) and the Sher-e-Kashmir University of Agricultural Science (SKUAST) Srinagar cancelled their MoUs with the US-based Kashmir Care Foundation (KCF) KCF describes itself as having been founded with a “vision to empower the youth of Kashmir”

title: Women’s quota: PM Modi seeks support, Opp says no details on delimitation
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi wrote to Parliamentary floor leaders across party lines seeking their support and cooperation . The Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam earmarked one-third reservation to women in LS and Assemblies, was passed in September 2023 . The move could become a rallying point for the Opposition .

title: ‘What were you thinking?’: Mehbooba Mufti targets Omar govt in J-K amid campaign to reclaim lost ground
summary: Mehbooba Mufti addressed the second public rally of the campaign in south Kashmir’s Pulwama, a PDP stronghold . In her address, the PDP chief took on Chief Minister Omar Abdullah, accusing him of “failing the people” of Jammuand Kashmir .

title: Massive search underway in Samba after suspicious movement reported at village near border
summary: A caretaker at an animal farm at Pangdour noticed movement of two unidentified individuals around 8.30 pm on Saturday and informed local police and security forces . While the suspects could not be traced in the area, a Special Operations Group of theJammuand Kashmir Police later found that the individuals were moving towards the SIDCO forest .

title: Dalit medical student’s death rocks Kerala, family claims college faculty humiliated him over caste, complexion
summary: Nithin Raj R L, 22, died after a fall from a building at the Kannur Dental College campus on Friday . Allegations emerge that he had been repeatedly subjected to insults and humiliation by faculty members in his college regarding his caste, complexion, and his parents being daily wage labourers . The family has expressed suspicion that foul play may be involved in the student’s death .

title: RBI proposes ‘Kill Switch’ to secure digital payments: What it means
summary: The Reserve Bank of India has proposed a ‘Kill Switch’ mechanism to enhance the security of digital payments . The move is expected to further reduce online fraud . Around 28 lakh digital payment fraud cases were reported in 2025, amounting to Rs 22,931 crore .

title: PM Awas houses on forest land: Centre tells SC about safeguards, duties under FRA
summary: In an ongoing matter on construction of houses on forest land, the Ministries of Tribal Affairs and Environment submitted a joint affidavit to the Supreme Court last month . The affidavit detailed safeguards built into the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006, ranging from multi-tier verifications to forest department’s oversight, and duties of forest rights holders on protection of habitats . The apex court is examining an appeal and a contempt petition in relation to construction of homes under PMAY-G on a forest land for a small community from the particularly vulnerable Sahariya tribe .

title: Poll debutant who sparred with Himanta summoned by Assam Police, her party slams ‘intimidation’
summary: Kunki Chowdhury is Congress-ally Assam Jatiya Parishad’s candidate from theGuwahatiCentral constituency . The 27-year-old is the youngest candidate in the fray in the Assam election . Assam Police detained three members of her campaign team in connection with the case .

title: ‘False claims’: India rejects China’s fictitious naming of places in Arunachal
summary: China has set up a new county in Xinjiang province near Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) and the Afghanistan border . The county, named Cenling, is located near the Karakoram mountain range and close to the borders with PoK and Afghanistan . China claims Arunachal Pradesh as part of southern Tibet . India describes the names as “inventions” by China, and has consistently dismissed them .

title: Delhi-Dehradun Expressway to cut travel time to 2.5 hours: How it compares with flight and train
summary: Delhi-Dehradun Expressway opens at a cost of around Rs 12,000 crore . The 213-km-long expressway, would have a spur towards Haridwar and also connect to Char Dham Highway . Travellers travelling to Mussoorie from Delhi, the expressway could further reduce total journey time to around 4 hours .

title: Amarnath Yatra to start July 3, last 19 more days than last year
summary: The Amarnath Yatra in the South Kashmir Himalayas will start on July 3 this year and conclude on August 28 . The annual pilgrimage, spanning over 57 days, will last for 19 more days than in 2025 . Registration facilities will be available at 554 branches of J&K Bank, Punjab National Bank, State Bank of India and Yes Bank .

title: ‘Her voice carried timeless brilliance’: PM Modi, Maharashtra CM Fadnavis mourn Asha Bhosle’s death
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi remembered her as the “one of the most and versatile voices India has ever known” She sang over 12,000 songs in more than 20 Indian languages, including Marathi, Hindi, and Bengali, as well as foreign languages . She was admitted to the city’s Breach Candy Hospital around 7 pm on Saturday .

title: FIR delayed, family harassed, victim ignored: Bokaro teen’s remains found after HC steps in, 28 cops suspended
summary: The Jharkhand High Court had flagged police inaction in the disappearance of an 18-year-old girl from Bokaro . The case took a dramatic turn Saturday with the recovery of her skeletal remains and the suspension of 28 police personnel over alleged negligence and suspected collusion . The girl had been missing since July 21 last year . The family had alleged that the police initially refused to file the complaint and discouraged them from naming any suspect .

title: Amarnath Yatra from July 3 to August 28; registration opens April 15
summary: The annual Amarnath Yatra to the cave shrine in south Kashmir will begin on July 3 and conclude on August 28 . Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha said the pilgrimage will span 57 days this year, making it slightly longer than recent years . Registration for the yatra has been made mandatory and will open from April 15 .

title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: Amit Shah says Bengal will ‘reply to bombs with votes’
summary: Amit Shah says Bengal will ‘reply to bombs with votes’ with votes . Amit Shah: Bengal will reply 'bomb bombs with bombs' with votes' Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates: Amit Shah . Bengal will be the only state to win a seat in the Assembly Election 2026 .

title: ‘Faith healing’ congregation in MP village lands two pastors in jail; police probe wider ‘conversion network’
summary: Police in Madhya Pradesh’s Guna district have registered a case against two pastors and their associates for allegedly running a religious conversion racket . The case gained traction after a complainant, identified as Brijesh Bairagi from Karod village, approached the police after witnessing the event earlier this month .

title: Ladakh secures distinct Aadhaar identity, UIDAI removes J&K tag
summary: ‘Ladakh’ replacing ‘Jammu & Kashmir’ in the State field, following the intervention of Lieutenant Governor V K Saxena . The UT administration took up the matter with the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) to ensure that Ladakh's distinct regional identity is accurately represented in Aadhar .

title: Himachal has over 300 cases of Section 118 violations pending
summary: More than 300 cases of violations of Section 118 of the Himachal Pradesh Tenancy and Land Reforms Act, 1972, including in high-profile projects such as the Chester Hills housing project in Solan, are under scrutiny of the State Revenue Department . Revenue Minister Jagat Singh Negi said the government has sought detailed inputs from all districts .

title: Exclusive | Before Bengal’s SIR row, Maharashtra CEO red-flagged to ECI: Need more time
summary: Maharashtra’s Chief Electoral Officer S Chockalingam wrote to the poll panel flagging that the timeline prescribed was too tight and sought sufficient time to complete the exercise . Maharashtra's letter was prescient given how the SIR process ran into trouble in West Bengal, where 89 lakh names have been deleted from electoral rolls .

title: Modi-Rahul interaction draws attention at Mahatma Phule event
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi at a programme in Parliament on the birth anniversary of social reformer Mahatma Jyotirao Phule . Modi is learnt to have inquired about the health of Rahul Gandhi’s mother and CPP chairperson Sonia Gandhi . The two leaders interacted in the presence of Speaker Om Birla, Union Ministers J P Nadda, Arjun Ram Meghwal and other dignitaries .

title: ‘Violation of Rule of Specialty’: Home Secretary flags procedural lapses in extradition cases
summary: Govind Mohan has raised serious concerns over procedural lapses in handling extradited individuals across certain jurisdictions . Such deviations could undermine India’s credibility in future extradition matters with foreign governments . Mohan cited some landmark rulings, but did not mention any individual who was extradited for specific offences and implicated in a fresh case .

title: Jaishankar discusses West Asia situation with his UAE counterpart
summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar met his UAE counterpart Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan in Abu Dhabi on Saturday . The two-day visit is important at a time when the talks between the US and Iran are taking place in Pakistan . The West Asia war and Iran’s chokehold of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway through which a fifth of global oil passes, had halted shipping .

title: Fire at Goa’s Dabolim Airport quickly contained; flights unaffected
summary: A minor fire broke out in an electrical switch room at the Goa International Airport at Dabolimon Saturday . The fire was quickly doused and no casualties were reported . Officials said the flight operations remained unaffected, and some passengers who were near the terminal area were “relocated to areas where there was no smoke”

title: Unnat Krishi Mahotsav | Centre developing state-wise agricultural roadmaps: Chouhan
summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan said the Centre will develop state-wise agricultural roadmaps as per each region’s agro-climatic conditions . Chouhan: Roadmaps will provide a complete, data-backed blueprint for success . Defence Minister Rajnath Singh inaugurated the three-day farm fair at Raisen from April 11 to 13 .

title: ‘Reliable, compassionate’: Iran embassy sends home medicines bought from donations in India
summary: A consignment of medical supplies donated by the people of India has been dispatched to Iran . The Iranian embassy in New Delhi used donations by Indians to buy medicines to send home . The initiative reflected India’s “sympathy and solidarity with the people and the Government of the Islamic Republic of Iran in these difficult times”

title: Born at Kuno, raised in the wild: Second Indian-born cheetah gives birth
summary: A female cheetah born inside the country delivered four cubs in the wild at Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh . Union Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav announced the news on social media . There are a total of 57 cheetahs in the state now, with at least 21 cubs .

title: Kerala BJP backs Christian leader in standoff with Catholic Church over ‘Congress tilt’
summary: Kerala BJP has rallied behind Christian leader Shone George, who is in a standoff with the Catholic Church over allegations that bishops instructed voters to support the Congress in the Assembly elections . Shone and his father P C George on Friday alleged that the Kanjirappally bishop had directed nuns to vote for Congress . The BJP state general secretary S Suresh said Saturday that Shone is a leader who fights for Christian causes .

title: Why RBI proposes 1-hour delay for digital payments above Rs 10,000
summary: RBI proposes one-hour delay for digital payments exceeding Rs 10,000 per transaction . Around 28 lakh digital payment fraud cases were reported in 2025, amounting to Rs 22,931 crore . RBI said that fraudsters are using a range of tactics, including bogus call centres, deepfake-driven impersonation and mule account networks .

title: Jharkhand treasury row escalates: BJP demands CBI probe, JMM calls scam a BJP-era legacy; Rs 33 cr flagged
summary: Political row erupts over alleged illegal withdrawals from government treasuries in Hazaribagh and Bokaro . Opposition BJP demands a probe by a central agency and the ruling JMM counters that the 'irregularities' date back to the previous BJP government .

title: Railways announces two ‘summer special’ trains on Mumbai-Howrah route: Check stoppages, timings
summary: The ticket booking will open on Sunday (April 12, 2026) at all computerised reservation centres across the country . The summer special trains will run as train number 01145 and 01146 . The train will run between Mumbai’s Lokmanya Tilak Terminus (LTT) and Howrah .

title: CAA push in Bengal: Will fast-track citizenship for refugee communities if BJP wins, says Modi
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi says citizenship will be fast-tracked for refugee communities if BJP forms government in West Bengal . At the same time, he issued a stern warning to ‘infiltrators’, asking them to leave the country . Modi accused the TMC of creating fear among refugee families while failing to secure their interests .

title: Hyderabad Metro Rail Phase 2 Project: Telangana govt submits 162-km expansion proposal to Centre – Check latest update
summary: The Telangana government has submitted a proposal for the construction of Phase 2 of the Hyderabad Metro Rail network, the central government said . Tokhan Sahu, Minister of State in the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, was responding to queries raised by Lok Sabha MPAsaduddin Owaisi .

title: Delhi Police arrest Bhubaneswar youth over ‘radical group’ links, taken on transit remand
summary: The Special Branch of the Delhi Police arrested a 22-year-old youth from Bhubaneswar for his alleged links with a ‘radical group’ The youth, identified as Sheikh Imran, was arrested from his residence in the Unit-6 area of Bhubanewar . The Delhi Police will investigate further to unravel his links with other extremist groups . The youth is suspected to have been influenced by radical views .

title: Bhubaneswar Metro scrapped or just on hold? Why BJP’s U-turn is sparking political fury in Odisha
summary: The Bhubaneswar metro project was initiated by the previous Biju Janata Dal government . It was to run on a 26-km route from Bhubanewar Airport to Trisulia near the Orissa High Court in Cuttack . With BJD’s fall in the 2024 elections, the project also hung fire .

title: ED raids Earth Infrastructures premises, seizes Rs 6.3 crore in cash, jewellery, and watches in money laundering probe
summary: The Enforcement Directorate conducted searches at 10 locations in Delhi and Gurgaon linked to real estate major Earth Infrastructures Ltd . The searches targeted directors, promoters, and associated entities of the Earth Group . The probe stems from five First Information Reports (FIRs) filed by DelhiPolice’s Economic Offences Wing .

title: Namastes and a rare moment: PM Modi speaks to Rahul Gandhi while paying tribute to Jyotiba Phule
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid tribute to Mahatma Jyotiba Phule on his 200th birth anniversary . Rahul Gandhi stood with Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, Minister of Health and Family Welfare JP Nadda and other Union ministers as PM Modi arrived at Prerna Sthal on the Parliament campus with his convoy .

title: Bengaluru-Mumbai Vande Bharat sleeper train gets Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw’s nod
summary: The semi-high-speed train is expected to further improve long-distance overnight travel on the route . The new orange and grey coloured train will run between KSRBengaluruand Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT),Mumbai . The first Vande Bharat Sleeper, launched in January 2026, operates between Howrah and Kamakhya .

title: Gold Rate Today, April 11: Check 18, 22 and 24 carat gold prices in Chennai, Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata and other cities
summary: Gold prices nudged higher on Friday, reversing the minor decline seen in the previous session . Chennairemained the costliest market, with 24K gold at ₹15,410 per gram, a premium that the southern city consistently commands . Price movement remains limited, suggesting that the market is still largely range-bound .

title: ‘Shocked’ to see officials he suspended still working at Mining Directorate, J-K Deputy CM demands probe
summary: Surinder Choudhary has slammed the Director Geology and Mining for retaining officials he had suspended or transferred . He also alleged irregularities in the directorate - which comes under him - calling it “a fish market and a source of illegal mining” The functioning of the mining department has previously been criticised .

title: ‘Opinions differ, but everyone is heard’: Speaker Om Birla on why discussions go ‘late into night’ in Parliament
summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla said everyone’s views and opinions should be heard in Parliament . He said on every Bill, the discussion often went beyond the allocated time, with maximum participation of the members . He called on young people to increase their participation to make governance more transparent .

title: A guiding light for generations: PM Modi pays tribute to Phule on birth anniversary
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi pays tribute to social reformer Jyotirao Phule on his birth anniversary . He said he was an example of how social change could happen within society . PM Modi sought to link his government’s policies with the inspirations derived from the life of Phule .

title: Women’s reservation law amendment: Congress may call Opp meet on April 15
summary: The party said it will call a meeting of top leaders of all INDIA bloc parties on April 15 to discuss the Opposition’s strategy on the issue . The Congress also said the government should call an all-party meeting after April 29, once polling for the West Bengal Assembly elections is over .

title: SIR in 10 states, three Union Territories so far: Electoral rolls trimmed by 5.58 crore, most in Gujarat, UP
summary: The SIR of electoral rolls, held in 10 states and three Union Territories so far, has led to the electorate being cut by 5.58 crore or 9.55% . Among the nine states, Gujarat saw the highest cut in electors (13.39%), followed by UP, Chhattisgarh and West Bengal . Andaman and Nicobar Islands had the highest decrease (16.86%)

title: Ceramic City kilns go cold over fuel crisis: ‘Hippo’ cylinders run out, workers leave
summary: Khurja, a town about 90 km from Delhi, has for decades been synonymous with Indian ceramics . More than 350 units operate here, producing everything from plates and cups to decorative trays and crockery that finds its way to hotel chains, canteens and homes across the country . But since the outbreak of the Iran war disrupted energy supply chains, most pottery manufacturers have been unable to run their plants due to an impact on gas supply and mounting fuel costs .

title: Vande Bharat Sleeper occupancy: How trains are performing
summary: The country’s first Vande Bharat Sleeper train was launched on the Howrah-Kamakhya route . The semi-high-speed train commenced its inaugural run on January 17, 2026 . It was flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi from West Bengal Malda Town railway station . The train is composed of 16 coaches: 11 AC 3-tier, four AC 2-tier and one AC 1st class .

title: Three students drown in Andhra Pradesh waterfall as selfie bid goes wrong
summary: Three girls drowned in Andhra Pradesh's Alluri Sitharama Raju district while attempting to take a selfie . The incident occurred on Thursday, following the declaration of summer holidays at their school . Police have launched an investigation and are in the process of registering a case under relevant sections of the law .

title: Vande Bharat Express train occupancy sees 34% YoY growth in FY26; how it performed since launch
summary: In FY 2025-26, theVande Bharat Express recorded a year-on-year (YoY) growth of nearly 34 per cent . Passenger numbers rose from 2.97 crore in FY 2024-25 to about 3.98 crore . More than 9.1 crore passengers have travelled on over 1 lakh trips on the Vande Bharat .

title: ‘He was an honest man, PDP worker’: Iltija Mufti appeals for body of man killed in Ganderbal encounter to be returned to family
summary: People's Democratic Party leader Iltija Mufti visits family of Rashid Mughal, killed in an Army encounter in Ganderbal . She appealed to Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sihha to return the body to his family . The L-G has already ordered a magisterial probe after questions were raised about the encounter .

title: ‘Tum kaale ho, you do not deserve me’: Wife arrested for ‘plotting’ murder of Madhya Pradesh man
summary: Priyanka Purohit allegedly left the front door of her house in Gondikhedha Charan village in Madhya Pradesh’s Dhar district unlocked . Her husband Devkrishna, a 28-year-old chilli trader, was killed with a bladed weapon on April 7 . She allegedly told police robbers tied her up, looted gold and silver worth Rs 3 lakh and Rs 50,000 in cash . But then came the twist: the jewellery was allegedly found inside the house, her phone records told a different story, and details of the incident changed with each retelling .

title: Pakistan terrorist tasked Dehradun man with filming key locations for attack: Special Task Force
summary: The Special Task Force in Dehradun arrested Vikrant Kashyap (29) for allegedly being connected to a Pakistani terrorist . The accused was allegedly sending locations and video footage of government and military establishments to Pakistan and planned to expand Tehreek-e-Taliban Hindustan (TTH) in India . The little-known outfit had allegedly taken responsibility for shooting two police officers near the India-Pakistan border in February .

title: Kerala polls: BJP Christian leaders say ‘ditched’ by Church over alleged UDF backing amid FCRA row
summary: Christian leaders in the Bharatiya Janata Party say they were 'ditched' by Church heads . They claim they urged people to vote for the Congress-led UDF . BJP has fielded several Christian leaders including Shone George in Pala and Poonjar . The BJP’s Christian outreach faced a setback after the Union government introduced a Bill in the Lok Sabha to amend the FCRA .

title: Acquitted in Malegaon blast case, Col Purohit  promoted to Brigadier, to serve for 2 more years
summary: Colonel Shrikant Purohit has been approved for promotion to Brigadier . He will now serve for at least two more years till the age of 56 . The retirement age for Brigadiers in the Army is 56, while for Colonels, it is 54 . He was acquitted in the 2008 Malegaon blast caselast year .

title: B.Tech student’s death at Bengaluru university: Father raises questions, Jharkhand minister steps in
summary: Lakshya Mishra, a 21-year-old B.Tech second-year student from Ranchi, died on April 7 at his university in Bengaluru . His father, Anjani Kumar Mishra took to social media, urging the government to initiate a proper investigation . The matter gained attention after a video by the student’s father went viral . Jharkhand Cabinet Minister Deepika Pandey Singh took cognisance of the case, urging Karnataka government to act .

title: ‘Shocks our conscience’: Supreme Court summons Ghaziabad police chief over 4-year-old’s rape-murder
summary: Supreme Court seeks presence of Ghaziabad Police Commissioner with “all original reports’ of the case on April 13 . The girl was allegedly lured away by a neighbour on the pretext of buying chocolates . When the child did not return, her father found her lying unconscious and soaked in blood in a field .

title: Bengaluru’s Kempegowda International Airport to handle 80 million passengers annually by 2030: Murlidhar Mohol
summary: Currently, the airport’s two terminals have a combined capacity of 51.5 million passengers per annum (mppa) The airport operator plans to increase terminal capacity to 80 mppa by 2030, along with phased expansion of taxiway and apron infrastructure between 2027 and 2030 .

title: ISI-BKI ‘espionage’ network targeting defence hubs busted, 11 arrested
summary: Delhi Police Special Police said it has dismantled an inter-state terror, arms smuggling, and espionage module . The module allegedly backed by Pakistan-based operatives linked to Babbar Khalsa International (BKI) and the Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) Police said they recovered nine solar-powered CCTV cameras installed near sensitive defence and security establishments .

title: Strong El Niño to emerge in Pacific Ocean after July, says NOAA
summary: A ‘very strong’ El Niño is likely to develop along the Pacific Ocean during the latter half of this year and persist till the end of 2026, the US-based National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) said on Thursday . The prevailing ENSO neutral conditions, which developed in March, will fade away and transition to El Niño during June – August period .

title: Lebanon under Israeli fire, India speaks out: Deep concern over civilian casualties, respect sovereignty
summary: India has been seen favouring Israel ever since the Israeli offensive in Gaza in 2023 which later expanded to Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah . It is aimed at restoring the balance with Iran, the benefactor of the Lebanese government and Hezbollah over the last several decades . The latest Israeli strikes on Lebanon, considered to be the most intense targeting of civilians in years .

title: 2.49 lakh hectare crop land affected by unseasonal rains, states directed to assess damage: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Chouhan
summary: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan said the Centre has instructed states to conduct an assessment of damage caused to various crops by the recent hailstorms and related weather events . The minister added that about 2.49 lakh hectares of agricultural land were affected across several parts of the country .

title: Sri Lanka’s cyclone-damaged railway lines back on track with India’s support
summary: The train services on Sri Lanka’s cyclone-damaged railway lines have resumed after the completion of restoration work by India . The tracks, which were damaged by Cyclone Ditwah, required urgent repairs for restoration . Railways remain one of the cornerstones of India-Sri Lanka development cooperation .

title: ‘BJP can never ally with those building Babri Masjid in Bengal’: Amit Shah on Humayun Kabir row
summary: Amit Shah said that the BJP would rather sit in the Opposition than ally with someone who is building Babri Masjid in West Bengal . Shah was speaking at an event in Kolkata while releasing the BJP manifesto . He said: “Mamata ji can make 2,000 videos. Humayun Kabir and the BJP are like south pole and north pole; we can never unite”

title: Nitish Kumar takes oath as Rajya Sabha MP, achieves rare feat
summary: Nitish Kumar took oath as a member of the Rajya Sabha on Friday . Known for his handling of coalition politics, Kumar played a key role in keeping the National Democratic Alliance in power in Bihar as well as the Centre . With this move, Kumar achieved the feat of serving every legislative body in the country .

title: SC stays criminal proceedings against priest booked for saying ‘only one religion…Christian’
summary: Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta ordered that the trial be stayed in the meanwhile . Vineet Vincent Pereira was booked under Section 295A of the Indian Penal Code . He was accused of hurting religious sentiments by proclaiming in prayer meets that “there is only one religion which is Christian”

title: Rubio set to visit India next month as Washington, Delhi reset ties
summary: Marco Rubio is likely to visit India for the meeting of Quad Foreign Ministers in the latter half of May . Preparations are underway to schedule the visits of Australian and Japanese Foreign Ministers around the same time . This will be a step towards holding the summit of Quad leaders later this year, sources said . Rubio’s visit to India, the first by the US Secretary of State and NSA from the Trump administration, was disclosed by Sergio Gor, the US Ambassador to India .

title: ‘BJP will implement Uniform Civil Code in 6 months if voted to power in Bengal’: Amit Shah
summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah released the BJP’s manifesto in Kolkata and promised to implement Uniform Civil Code in the state within six months of being voted to power . Shah said the public is now “fearful, disillusioned, and is seeking change from the heart” The BJP manifesto listed 15 promises for the development of Bengal and its people .

title: Assam: 3 members of Kunki Chowdhury team detained for ‘violations’
summary: Kunki Chowdhury is Congress-ally Assam Jatiya Parishad’s candidate from Guwahati Central constituency . The 27-year-old is the youngest candidate in the fray in the Assam election . She has denied the allegations against her and her social media team .

title: Singed by cash at home scandal, Justice Yashwant Varma quits, ending impeachment process
summary: Justice Yashwant Varma is facing impeachment proceedings over the alleged discovery of burnt cash from his Delhi residence last year . With his resignation, the future course of action over allegations levelled against him enters uncharted territory . Legal experts said that resignation is a step towards foreclosing a possible impeachment motion .

title: Passport row: Pawan Khera gets 1-week relief in case filed by Assam CM Himanta Sarma’s wife
summary: The Telangana High Court Friday granted relief for one week with conditions to senior Congress leader Pawan Khera . Congress leader was directed to refrain from making any further public statements on the subject matter of the case . The order has been issued only to enable Pawan to approach the competent court, which will consider any application for regular or anticipatory bail on its own merits .

title: Assembly Elections 2026 Live Updates:  ‘Won’t let Bengalis become minority’, Modi vows UCC in Bengal; targets TMC’s ‘Mahajungleraj’
summary: ‘Won’t let Bengalis become minority’, Modi vows UCC in Bengal . TMC’s ‘Mahajungleraj’ targets TMCs . Assembly Elections 2026 live updates from Bengal Assembly Election 2026 .

title: President nominates veteran JD(U) leader Harivansh for third term in Rajya Sabha
summary: Janata Dal (United) leader Harivansh has been nominated for another six-year term in the Rajya Sabha by President Droupadi Murmu . This ends speculation about whether he would continue his membership of the Upper House after making way for Nitish Kumar . Kumar is scheduled to take the oath soon and demit office as Bihar chief minister .

title: CWC meeting today: Party to finalise strategy on women’s reservation and delimitation Bills
summary: Congress Working Committee (CWC) will meet Friday to formulate and navigate the party’s strategy on the proposed constitutional amendments to the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam and a delimitation Bill . The CWC meeting will also discuss the political and constitutional implications of the proposed changes to the Women's Reservation Bill, the proposed delimitation, and developments in West Asia . Party chief Mallikarjun Kharge, CWC members, including Lok Sabha Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi, MPs Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and Abhishek Manu Singhvi .

title: Gold Rate Today, April 10: Check 18, 22 and 24 carat gold prices in Chennai, Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata and other cities
summary: Gold prices edged marginally lower on April 10, with 24-carat gold trading at ₹15,147 per gram . 22-year-old gold stood at � 13,884 and 18-carate gold at ⁹11,360 per gram, data quoted by Good Returns .

title: How women’s self-help group’s legal battle led to scrapping of Rs 60-lakh tender in Chhattisgarh
summary: The Chhattisgarh High Court quashed a tender floated by a government hospital in Jashpur district for food services for a year . The petitioner is a women’s Self-Help Group managing a restaurant, ‘Gad Kalewa’, at Jungle Bazar . The eligibility conditions required bidders to have a minimum average annual turnover of Rs 50 lakh for the last three years .

title: Building Rs 81,000-crore Great Nicobar Island: A greenfield coastal city, international airport with an eye on tourism and entertainment hub
summary: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands administration has outlined the proposed development of a greenfield coastal city that will rest on the pillars of an airport, a transshipment port, tourism and entertainment hub, and allied servicing industry . Public Works Department of the islands has sought suggestions and objections on the ‘draft master plan for Great Nicobar Island Development Area – 2047’

title: Kashmir scientists crack cultivation of rare Rs 40,000 morel mushrooms
summary: Morels or Morchella, locally known as Kangaech, that grows naturally in specific high elevation forest ecosystems during a narrow rainy season costs anything between Rs 15,000 to Rs 40,000 per kilogram . The cultivation of wild Morels in controlled conditions has been achieved independently by three people in SKUAST .

title: Record turnout in Assam (85%), Puducherry (90%); Kerala sees 78%, higher than 2021 elections
summary: Assam and Puducherry recorded their highest-ever turnout in the Assembly elections on Thursday . Kerala, too, saw an increase from last time at 78.03% for their first election after the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls was completed in February this year .

title: Minor missing for 8 months: Delayed FIR, HC orders removal of Bokaro cop
summary: Jharkhand High Court orders removal of officer-in-charge of a police station . Case pertains to a girl missing since July 21, 2025, from Bokaro . The family was even “threatened and discouraged” from filing the complaint . The matter will be heard on April 15 .

title: Manipur CM chairs high-level security meet after violence
summary: Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh chaired a high-level security meeting to review the prevailinglaw and order situation . The meeting was held at the Cabinet Hall of the CM’s Secretariat . Security has been placed on high alert in peripheral areas, particularly along border areas .

title: Centre notifies CAPF Act after President’s assent
summary: The Centre on Thursday notified the Central Armed Police Forces (General) Administration Act after the assent from President Droupadi Murmu . The new legislation was published in the Gazette on Thursday by the Ministry of Law and Justice . Aimed at creating a “comprehensive and uniform system’, the Act targets Group A General Duty officers .

title: Ex-IAS Subodh Agarwal arrested after multi-city hunt in Rs 20,000 crore Jal Jeevan Mission ‘scam’
summary: Rajasthan’s Anti-Corruption Bureau arrested absconding former IAS officer Subodh Agarwal from New Delhi . Earlier, a Jaipur court had issued an arrest warrant against the officer who had been evading arrest since long . Two private firms had submitted fake completion certificates along with a letter of award issued by Indian Railway Construction International Limited .

title: Market dispute turns deadly in Bihar’s Araria, man killed, accused lynched
summary: Two people were killed in Bihar's Araria district Thursday after a dispute in a crowded marketplace escalated into murder and led to a mob lynching . The incident occurred in the Forbesganj area, where a man identified as Nabi Hussain, a pickup vehicle driver and a resident of Amauna village under Bathnaha police station limits, had arrived at a local marketing yard . According to police, an altercation broke out between Hussain and a sattu seller, Ravi Chauhan, who allegedly attacked Hussain with a knife and severing his head .

title: ‘Opportunities for India-Denmark collab will expand significantly’: India’s first Danish Chamber of Commerce to come up in Delhi soon
summary: The initiative aims to support Danish companies operating in India or those considering business opportunities here . Danish companies already have a strong footprint in India with more than 225 subsidiaries employing over a lakh people . Total volume of bilateral trade in goods and services between India and Denmark is around $7.8 billion in 2024 .

title: Voters brave rain in Assam: 84.42% polling recorded by 5 pm
summary: Long queues were seen at several polling stations, even at the closing hour of 5 pm . The final voter turnout figure will be ascertained after the completion of polling in all locations . The turnout this time has seen an uptick since the last election, when the state had registered an 82.42% turnout .

title: Eye on energy, India rushes Jaishankar, Puri to West Asia amid fragile ceasefire
summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar reached Port Louis in Mauritius Thursday to participate in the 9th Indian Ocean Conference . Meeting with ministers from West Asia theatre countries Oman, Saudi Arabia and Egypt, from maritime vicinity partners such as Sri Lanka, Maldives, Seychelles, Madagascar and Tanzania .

title: Jammu and Kashmir launches 100-day ‘Nasha Mukt’ drive; LG Sinha to flag off padyatra in Jammu
summary: Lt Governor Manoj Sinha will flag off a mega ‘Padyatra’ from MA Stadium on April 11 . This will be followed by a series of high-impact awareness programmes aimed at eradicating drug abuse from the UT . A similar large-scale campaign will be launched in Srinagar in the first week of May .

title: NHAI sets Dec 2026 deadline for Sahibganj-Manihari Ganga bridge connecting Jharkhand & Bihar
summary: The 22-km-long project stretches from Sahibganj in Jharkhand to Narenpur in Bihar’s Katihar district . Once completed, the bridge will provide direct connectivity from the Santhal Pargana region to Bihar, West Bengal and the Northeastern states . The route is also expected to improve trade with Nepal, Bhutan and Bangladesh .
